title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic votive box; figure (Egypt)
desc: Hollow-cast copper alloy votive box, also known as relic-box or animal coffin; broken rectangular case surmounted by a group of solid-cast figures, one of a cobra with raised head and the other of a lizard; corroded; one side and end of case crushed in; case previously contained a mummified reptile or two (or parts of), now empty.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Bronze lamp with a rounded body, a wide rounded shoulder, and a short low-placed nozzle, slightly splayed, with a rounded tip and a circular wick-hole. The top is slightly dished, with concentric grooves round the small filling-hole. At the rear are traces of a lost handle. The raised circular base is applied, and perhaps belongs; within are `lathe-turned' mouldings.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight; ingot (Lower Egypt)
desc: Cast weight (?) or bent piece of ingot (?) in copper alloy.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Second Intermediate juglet (Egypt)
desc: Pottery Tell El-Yahudiyeh ware juglet; wheel-made; concave neck with rolled rim; vertical handle from below rim to shoulder; piriform body; button base; decorated all over body with closely spaced, punctured vertical zig-zags; traces of highly lustrous burnishing; fabric dark grey with red core; surface near base poorly preserved.

title: Meroitic model; axe (Jebel Moya)
desc: Bronze model axe-head: made out of thin sheet metal. It has splayed sides and a slightly concave elongated butt with thin tapering lugs.  It is moderately corroded. Some of the cutting edge is lost.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp decorated on the discus with Eros in a two-goat chariot on a panelled plinth. The angular shoulder is decorated with impressed ovules. At the rear is an unpierced Egyptian ring handle. Within the base-ring is impressed a bound triple branch. The nozzle is damaged.

title: Coptic; Byzantine stela (Egypt)
desc: Limestone stela; Greek text; representation of a dove beneath a cross.

title: Late Period votive box; figure (Egypt)
desc: Hollow-cast, rectangular case surmounted by a solid-cast figure of a cat seated at one end, in copper alloy; corroded but relatively well preserved; though the case is sealed, back of case looks slightly irregular, as if broken (broken before being sealed?); this votive box could contain parts of mummified cat, or be just symbolic without any animal remains since the size of the case is very small.

title: perfume-bottle (Egypt)
desc: Perfume bottle of brass.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from hard stone, likely calcite; carinated dome type with soft edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: 18th Dynasty funerary cone (Egypt)
desc: Pottery funerary cone.

title: New Kingdom; Third Intermediate textile; cloth; mummy-wrapping (Egypt)
desc: Textile fragment; torn strip of undyed linen cloth, probably a mummy-wrapping; deterioration of edges due to contact with black bitumen. Side edges torn, ends cut. The weave is plain [warp-faced simple tabby] and the threads are S-spun and S-spliced; the stitch gauge is warp c.28 threads per 10mm and weft c.11 picks per 10mm.

title: 26th Dynasty amulet (Egypt)
desc: Amulet in Egyptian blue, in the shape of a falcon, maybe associated with  the god Montu or Horus; standing on rectangle base and wearing a crown with two tall plumes and small disc; tail and feet in openwork; rather schematic work; broken suspension loop pierced widthways behind head; in soft pale blue paste.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; slightly damaged top due to possible reuse as hammer/percuteur.

title: Late Period amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition amuletic figure of a ram-headed god, probably Amun-Ra or less likely Khnum; features now indistinct; modelling crude, though lines shown on schenti and on face; plain back pillar, stepped out with right-angled edges below shoulders' height; loop for suspension, once on top of head, now broken off; remains of  pale turquoise glaze; fine and soft pale yellow core; broken below thighs and chipped in several places; surface very much worn.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Sherd, fragment. Inscription. Made of polychrome enamelled and gilded glass.

title: Ancient Egypt handle; vessel (Naukratis)
desc: floral scroll, part of a handle or other kind of attachment? Broken off at lower edge

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Grey steatite scarab, elytra marked, pierced longitudinally, base decorated with a representation of Thoth.

title: Late Period figure; votive plaque (Egypt)
desc: Naked female figure standing in nich or shrine. Schematic rendering of the figure. Crudely carved from a  rectangular limestone block to produce a relief votive plaque. Square-cut wig to the shoulders. Red and black painted decoration, detailed with incision. Portion including the head lost. Fragment in soft limestone.

title: Early Islamic doll; bead (Egypt)
desc: Doll; carved from animal-bone with incised decoration; hair represented by strings of beads.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period dice (Egypt)
desc: Dice made in soft white limestone; small circular incised (or drilled) shallow depressions indicated on each side of the dice; the numbers of circles is not what is expected on a regular dice with two sides marked by seven circles, irregularly places on the surface, two sides with six depressions; at last one regular side with four circles and another one with five: rigged dice?

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green glazed steatite scarab, elytra marked and humeral callosities present, feathered legs, legs cut through, pierced longitudinally, base inscribed with prenomen.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Mamluk dynasty bowl (Egypt)
desc: Bowl (sherd.base) on a high foot, with a three-barred medallion. Spur marks. Made of brown stained and honey glazed sgraffito pottery (red).

title: Graeco-Roman figure (Egypt)
desc: A hollow terracotta figure of Hathor, naked, with her hands to her sides. She has Isis-locks falling onto her shoulders, and wears an elaborate headdress of bound and leafy wreaths, with the ties hanging down beside her arms; it is surmounted by a large 'kalathos' with a notched upper edge: the surface of the 'kalathos' is eroded, and it may have had a Hathor/Isis crown in relief on the front. She has bracelets, an armlet on her left arm, and an anklet on her right leg. There is only summary tre

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Ramesside; Third Intermediate scarab (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made scarab in glazed composition; simply-modelled (?) rubbed back with only head and clypeus marked, but details might have disappeared; at side, crude and thick legs, with undercut triangle; underside incised with vertically arranged motif representing falcon-headed god (Horus, Ra-Horakhty or Montu?) crowned with sun-disc and small uraeus, walking towards right and holding large raised cobra by the tail to right; pierced lengthways; glaze lost; pale yellow core; surface very eroded.

title: Late Period dish (Egypt)
desc: A segmented pottery dish with two semicircular compartments, one of which is approximately half missing. Made from very coarse Nile siltware, low-fired and black in the centre of the section, with pale brown surfaces. The base is flat.

title: Ptolemaic vase (Lower Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition sherd, in shape of papyrus flower, hollow inside, with flat, smooth lip, on upper part of flower, and narrow rim at bottom with two concentric unglazed bands - for attachment to some other object? Probably the base of a large closed vase. Similar in design to other papyrus shaped floral rings. No decoration. Glaze thick, pale blue green of Ptolemaic type. Core fine, white to buff, good quality. Glaze of very fine smooth quality on rim and inside.

title: Roman Imperial bottle (Egypt)
desc: Colourless blown glass bottle with a thickened ground rim, a concave neck, an ovoid body and a tubular pushed-in base-ring with a pontil mark and kick. Decorated with a two-fold trail on the neck and wheel-cut incisions on the body.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Sherd, fragment. Figure. Made of red enamelled glass.

title: Late Period amulet (Naukratis)
desc: Mould-made amulet in glazed composition, roughly made, representing a mammalian animal, likely a sow, standing on a rectangular base; very crude, stylized features with a very few incisions (legs, head); suspension cylinder pierced lengthways added to the back of sow; green glaze mostly worn; very eroded surface.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp decorated on the discus with (on a panelled plinth) Eros seated on a rock playing with a dog. The angular shoulder is decorated with impressed ovules. At the rear is a ring handle. Within the base-ring is impressed a bound triple branch. The surface is damaged.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp decorated on the discus with Orpheus or perhaps Apollo under a tree, playing the lyre. The shoulder has a channel interrupted by a rectangular panel each side. At the rear is a ring handle. The lamp stands on a raised base. The nozzle is broken.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Egypt)
desc: Hemidrachm of Alexandria with the head of Vespasian 11.49gm axis: 12

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amuletic figure in glazed composition; double-sided represention of the dwarf  leonine god Bes; degenerate form of amulet with highly crude and schematic representation; body of Bes and his characteristic feathered crown crossed by more or less deep horizontal grooves, on both sides; suspension hole pierced widthways through the headdress; light turquoise glaze partly worn on one side; complete.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Limestone ostracon.

title: New Kingdom magical brick (Egypt)
desc: Clay magical brick decorated with a shabti.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical seal; pendant (Egypt)
desc: Bronze/copper alloy stamp seal or pendant; incised geometrical motifs on the two wider sides, and on the top, possible animal motif on the underside; the suspension hole at the top is filled with remains of a copper-based wire.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from dark grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: 4th Dynasty tomb-relief (Giza)
desc: Limestone relief of Iry: the slab is carved in fine low relief with a representation of Iry, wearing a leopard-skin and holding a staff and a sceptre. There are traces of black paint on Iry's wig which is of tight curls. He also wears bracelets, and around his neck hangs a curious leafy pendant strung between two beads. His musculature is carefully delineated, but his insteps are unnaturally arched. There are also five smaller representations of men, possibly sons of Iry: Nnk, Nfr-sšm-nsw, 'Iṯḥ,

title: New Kingdom scarab (Egypt)
desc: Faded brown steatite scarab inscribed with the name of Thutmose III.

title: Late Period furniture-fitting; figure (Egypt)
desc: Painted wooden furniture-fitting in the form of Sokar(?) as a mummified hawk resting on a rectangular plinth; wearing a collar and a counterpoise.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Middle Kingdom amulet (Egypt)
desc: An electrum 'sa'-amulet: made from four wires placed side by side and held together with five flat pieces of gold foil at the top, sides and bottom and soldered in several places. The object may have formed part of the clasp of a bracelet.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Laureate head of Galerius Augustus, right.; Concordia standing left, leaning on sceptre, holding cross-fold of drapery.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical; Phoenician; Late Period amulet (Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition (faience) amuletic figure of Anubis; mould-made and covered in a blue-green glaze; perforated for suspension through the middle back; human with jackal head standing on a low plinth; glaze cracked and worn in places.

title: Fatimid dynasty bowl (Qasr Ibrim)
desc: Bowl; glazed pottery (earthenware), painted with black circle and radial lines in black and green.

title: Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Late Antique lamp (Egypt)
desc: Oval pottery lamp with carinated body on a high, hand-modelled stand with spreading foot, concave underneath. The applied handle has a thumb-piece. The discus, flat with raised rim, is decorated with a band of ring-and-dot patterns in relief around the filling-hole and a row of raised points at edge. The shoulder has a band of ring-and-dot patterns below a row of raised points. The nozzle-top is decorated with a cross. The red-brown fabric is covered with remains of white slip, possibly applied

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: 12th Dynasty cylinder seal (Egypt)
desc: Glazed steatite cylinder seal inscribed with the Horus name, Nebty name, Gold Horus name and nomen of Nofrusobek, and the epithet beloved of Sobek lord of Shedyt.

title: Greek coin (Minted in: Kition)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure; amulet (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amuletic figure in glazed composition, representing probably Shu or  Heh, kneeling on a small rectangular base, leaning against a small back pillar; raised hands above his head, normally holding a gold nwb-sign (Heh) or with sun-disk (Shu), but not preserved here; modelling seems quite fine but surface totally eroded; pillar pierced widthwise, for suspension, at head level; faint traces of greenish glaze; pale yellow finely porous core; very worn out.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Roman Period figure (Egypt)
desc: A hollow terracotta figure of a Sothic dog standing to right on a low curved flat-fronted plinth, its head turned to the front. It is a spitz-type hound, but leaner than most. Its tail is tightly curled, and it wears a bell round its neck and bands round its middle. Plain back, with circular vent. Two-piece mould. Micaceous red-brown Nile silt, burnt dark brown over large areas. Traces of a white dressing overall, with minute surviving touches of pink paint.

title: Umayyad dynasty lamp (Egypt)
desc: Lamp; pottery; almond-shaped; with angular sides and a convex top decorated with eight arches containing crude designs of birds and trees; around the filling hole is a raised rim which extends down to the spout.

title: 18th Dynasty tomb-painting (Tomb of Nebamun)
desc: Fragment of a polychrome tomb-painting representing Nebamun seated (probably associated with the goose census scene from the same tomb) with remains of five vertical registers of polychrome-painted hieroglyphs.

title: Ptolemaic figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Head of female terracotta figure with hair arranged in 'melon style', following Tanagra types. Mould-made and solid. Broken at neck. Traces of white coating red paint on hair. Fine pink-brown (Nile silt?) fabric.

title: Late Roman; Early Byzantine weight (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy weight; square with convex profile; face engraved with double wreath enclosing cross above denominational mark; traces of inlay; quatrefoil in upper corners.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Ancient Egypt bolt (Naukratis)
desc: Bolt? Broken at one end, other and original. Flat back.

title: 13th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Glazed steatite scarab, elytra marked, longitudinally pierced, base inscribed with name of Senebtyfy the overseer of the work-enclosure.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast figure of standing man, performing a ritual, in copper alloy; priest with close-cropped hair, wearing short striated kilt; priest in a striding pose, standing on small rectangular base; holding an object, perhaps a small offering tray (?) or a lamp in his left hand; if identification of lamp correct, priest lights it up with his right hand; luminary rituals are well-known but the identification of the lamp is uncertain; bronze with dark brown patina; worn off features; complete.

title: Late Christian Period textile; veil (Qasr Ibrim)
desc: Small fragment of red open weave wool, perhaps from a woman's head-veil, a narrow simple band in black and a wider band in black and undyed cotton with a pattern of concentric hexagons.

title: Classical Greek votive plaque; relief (Athens)
desc: Terracotta relief plaque ('Melian relief') Death of Aktaion; a hound sinks its teeth in the hero's hand, urged on by the goddess Artemis.

title: Late Period scarab (Egypt)
desc: Glazed white steatite scarab: engraved in the edge with a hieroglyphic arrangement: 'wn', a female figure holding a flower on a long stalk 'nb' sign on edge behind her. This may refer to the goddess 'wnw.t' of Hermopolis.

title: Classical Greek; Hellenistic; Archaic Greek arrow-head (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy arrow-head. Barbed with broken and missing tang and barb. Ridge along middle with a central burr gives the arrow a diamond shape section.

title: Third Intermediate shabti (Egypt)
desc: Green glazed composition shabti with a lappet wig tied with a black fillet, a modelled face and crossed hands with two black hoes; column of black Hieroglyphic text down the leg section; seed-basket on the back.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Sheep or goat distal radius bone.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and exagonal from top; smoothed; complete.

title: 18th Dynasty necklace; bead; amulet; forgery (Egypt)
desc: Necklace in the form of a string of alternating spherical garnet beads and solid-cast gold fly amulets. Possibly a fake.

title: token (Issued in: Egypt)
desc: Brass tourist souvenir token.; Tughra at centre. Stars and moon around edges.; Male figure riding a camel at centre.

title: Graeco-Roman ostracon (Deir el-Bahri)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Roman Period ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Achaemenid; Archaic Greek arrow-head (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy arrow-head with slender leaf shape and trefoil section, long socket with side barb. Also known as trilobate, or 'Scythian type'.

title: Mamluk dynasty bowl (Egypt)
desc: Carinated brass bowl with a flat base and rounded rim; cast, decorated with silver and gold inlays, some of which were then decorated with chasing. Only traces of the gold inlays survive. The bowl is inscribed.

title: Ptolemaic; Late Period arrow-head (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy arrow-head with rhombic shape and trefoil section and socket.  Also known as trilobate, or 'Scythian type'. A socketted arrowhead with a triangular cross-section and the tip is missing. The edges are cracked.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp with an angular shoulder and a short rounded nozzle set off by a double scroll. The discus is decorated with Phthonos, a male figure with his hands to his throat, surrounded by animals and birds. On each side is a lug-handle. The base is undefined.

title: 18th Dynasty papyrus (Saqqara)
dateDisplay: 15thC BC (reign of Thutmose IV, by style)
desc: Book of the Dead of Nebseny; frame 5. At top: spell 148. Below: spell 30a continued; spell 26; spell 22; spell 23; spell 46; spell 100; spell 56.

title: 25th Dynasty; 26th Dynasty; 22nd Dynasty statue (Giza (Memphis) (said to have been)
desc: Bronze upper part of a standing man: with the remains of a sand quartz core. This fragmentary statue of a standing man wears a type of wig that was especially popular during the Twenty-first and Twenty-second Dynasties, and a modified version of the New Kingdom tunic, with less floppy sleeves. There seems to be no indication of the kilt or sash that one would expect with this garment. The man's bland, handsome face, a blend of several New Kingdom styles, resembles representations of Osorkon II a

title: Middle Kingdom stela (Egypt)
desc: Round-topped limestone stela of Ankhhor; representation of the deceased and wife beneath Hieroglyphic text.

title: Hyksos; 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Scarab carved from beige steatite; back has little detail, without details of elytra, only straight line across to represent edge of prothorax, with simple grooves  for clypeus in front; at side, legs simply indicated by carved out triangle and horizontal grooves; underside incised with motif arranged horizontally consisting of uraeus facing left on hill of creation, flanked by two beetle scarabs (the right one grasping a sun-disc between its front feet); details added by small incisions inside

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from white hard stone, likely calcite; uncarinated dome type; of ball shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: bowl (Alexandria)
desc: Rim sherd from a colourless buff-tinged glass convex bowl with vertical facets and four horizontal bands on the exterior.

title: Hellenistic transport amphora (Rhodes)
desc: Fragment of oval handle to sharpish turn of a Rhodian pottery transport amphora. Clay fine buff-brown. Rectangular stamp.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Hellenistic; Roman lid; box (Egypt)
desc: Bone lid of a small circular box; concave top with a inset underside for fitting into the box; off-centre hole representing the bore of the bone; top surface polished.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical; Phoenician; Late Period amulet; pendant (Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition (faience) amulet in form of a baboon; mould-made and glazed.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical; Eastern Mediterranean bead (Egypt)
desc: Barrel-shaped agate bead; perforated through the long axis for suspension; polished surface.

title: Late Roman; Early Byzantine weight (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy weight; square with convex profile; engraved with reversed denominational mark, with punched ornament in field; file marks on face and base.

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: Plaster cast, white colour, of sandstone relief-block from the rear chapel wall of pyramid N17 at Meroë, Sudan. Cast depicts part of a figure bearing an incense-burner; unfinished.

title: Classical Greek; Archaic Greek; Hellenistic; Achaemenid arrow-head (Egypt)
culture: Classical Greek; Archaic Greek; Hellenistic; Achaemenid
period: Classical Greek; Archaic Greek; Hellenistic; Achaemenid
desc: Copper alloy arrow-head with rhombic shape and trefoil section and socket. Also known as trilobate, or 'Scythian type'.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Bowl (sherd.base) with a fransisiyya (fleur-de-lys); brown-stained and green-glazed sgraffito pottery (red).

title: Late Period figure; votive plaque (Egypt)
desc: Reclining female figure on couch or recessed bed, with a child at feet (small standing figure behind feet of larger reclining nude female figure). Crudely carved roughly square limestone relief votive plaque. Square-cut wig to the shoulders, nose and mouth carved, eyes probably once carved but now lost due to worn surface. Faint blotches of red here and there on surface, probably from painted decoration. Soft  friable limestone. Top left corner of plaque broken off; flat back.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical; Phoenician; Late Period bead (Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition (faience) bead or amulet in form of wedjat-eye; mould-made and covered with a blue-green glaze (very worn), with the eye-ball rendered in black; perforated for suspension through the long axis.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Sheep or goat third molar tooth, left mandibular, with full wear.

title: Archaic Greek figure (Egypt)
desc: Upper part of a faience figure of Basht.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast figure, or more probably fragment of an amuletic group-figure, in copper alloy; only preserved figure depicting the goddess Nephthys, standing with her left arm along her body and her right arm raised to the side; wearing her characteristic headdress in the shape of a house and basket; simplistic and flat rendering; feet and right hand broken off; very corroded.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period bottle (Egypt)
desc: Small pottery bottle.

title: Ptolemaic oil-lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp with a double-convex body, a short round-topped splayed nozzle, and a raised circular base, flat below with a deep groove, and with an impressed letter alpha. The filling-hole is surrounded by two moulded rings, the outer one being surrounded by a horse-shoe of egg-and-tassel decoration in relief. Between the nozzle and the moulding is a scroll pattern. The shoulder is decorated with radiating grooves. There is a plain lug on the right side, and one in the form of a dolph

title: Graeco-Roman writing-tablet (Egypt)
desc: Rectangular fragment of a writing-board made of wood.

title: Early Byzantine ampulla (Alexandria)
desc: Earthenware ampulla. On each side, within a narrow plain border, Saint Menas stands to front, his arms outstretched in blessing. A relief  inscription is divided by his head. He has curly hair, and wears a short tunic, a long cloak and boots, and is flanked by camels. Both sides are from moulds produced from the same archetype. The mouth and handles are largely lost.

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green glazed steatite scarab, elytra marked and single humeral callosity preserved, pierced longitudinally, base inscribed with cartouche containing prenomen and epithet below winged disc.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amuletic figure in glazed composition, representing an Egyptian deity, maybe the baboon-headed god Thot, the ram-headed god Khnum or Amun-Ra Baded (?); standing in striding  pose, leaning against back-pillar; highly schematic, with crude incisions, making the  identification of the god represented uncertain; pierced widthwise through  head for suspension; greenish turquoise glaze; complete.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic votive box; figure (Egypt)
desc: Hollow-cast, copper alloy votive box, also known as relic-box or animal coffin; rectangular case surmounted by a solid-cast figure of a lizard; two eyelets for string on opposite corners of top of case; corroded; case complete, should contain a mummified lizard.

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast figure of the funerary god Osiris in copper alloy; depicted as mummiform and standing on a flat rectangular base; holding against his chest the sceptre Heka and the flail Nekhekh, hands side by side; wearing the Atef crown, composed of the white crown with uraeus on front, flanked by two ostrich feathers; feathers, beard, uraeus and insignia of power well detailed with small diagonal and horizontal incisions; flat cast; tang beneath the base; corroded, but quite well preserved and com

title: Hellenistic; Roman bead; amulet; pendant (Egypt)
desc: Eighty miscellaneous glass counters/inlays; cast. Surface very worn

title: Mamluk dynasty; Islamic textile; bag; hood (Egypt)
desc: Fragment of textile (bag or hood) of undyed, plain weave linen with red wool bands; A rectangle is folded over and sewn across one end, the seam decorated with an applied double cord of red wool; At the open end, there is a channel with the remains of a red wool draw string; No side seam preserved; Warp?: linen, c.21 tpc, mixed S and Z spun, no clear pattern; Weft?: linen mainly Z spun but some S spun, wool S spun, c.21 tpc; Edges: short lengths of two plain selvedges (near ends of channel), oth

title: spear (Nubia)
desc: Spear or lance with iron head and wooden shaft.

title: Second Intermediate papyrus (Egypt)
desc: Papyrus with literary texts on recto and verso; fifteen fragments belonging to the bottom edge of a roll; recto text is written in pages of horizontal lines, with vertical lines at the end; the verso text is written in pages of horizontal lines with verse points.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Period oil-lamp (Egypt)
desc: Pottery oil lamp. Egyptian 'frog and corn' type. Mould-made, with a circular reservoir with a central hole in the top with a concentric ring modelled around it. The top of the reservoir is decorated with an incised 'frog and corn' design. Incised ring on base surrounding raised pot mark. The nozzle projects from the body, and has incised decoration. Traces of burning through use. Reddish brown Nile silt fabric.

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Bronze figure of Wepwawet as a jackal; two uraei at the sides and a small worshipping figure before; Hieroglyphic text on one side of the plinth; two tangs beneath.

title: Roman Period mummy-label (Egypt)
desc: Cedar wood mummy-label of Senpsansnos, daughter of Harsiesis.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Hellenistic dish (Egypt)
desc: Mould-pressed mosaic glass dish with concave sides and a slightly convex base: formed from sections of multi-coloured glass canes (clear deep blue with opaque white spirals), interspersed with segments of yellow and white glass or occasionally gold foil sandwiched between two coloured layers. Two wheel-cut lines inside the rim.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Third Intermediate heart-scarab (Egypt)
desc: Serpentine (?) heart-scarab, elytra marked, unpierced, base inscribed with eight lines of painted hieroglyphs.

title: Greek coin (Abydos)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Diademed head of Zeus Ammon, right.; Two eagles standing on thunderbolt with wings open, left.

title: Early Islamic doll (Egypt)
desc: Bone doll; carved from animal-bone with incised details to represent the face and upper torso of a standing female figure.

title: Roman Period papyrus (Egypt)
desc: Funerary papyrus containing First Book of Breathing (three fragments) written in hieratic script.

title: New Kingdom funerary cone (Thebes)
desc: Pottery funerary cone.

title: dish (Egypt)
desc: Dish of brass.

title: Hellenistic necklace (Egypt)
desc: Necklace of gold lovers' knot beads separated by beads of amethyst and emerald.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Nubia and Sudan periods and cultures foundation-deposit; model; axe (Tomb of Harsiyotef)
culture: Nubia and Sudan periods and cultures
period: Nubia and Sudan periods and cultures
desc: Iron model axe-head: roughly made out of thin sheet metal. It has a relatively short blade with splayed sides and an almost straight cutting edge. The butt is slightly concave and the one intact lug is large and long. The piece is thickly corroded. There is a crack on one side.

title: figure (Aurangabad (Bihar))
desc: Miniature figure of Indian god (staff in left hand) made of clay, metal (?).

title: Late Roman magical gem; intaglio (Mediterranean)
desc: Magical gem; intaglio; coral; oval; bevelled edge; engraved on obverse: standing hawk; on reverse: inscription in hieroglyphs.

title: Umayyad dynasty vessel-stamp (Egypt)
desc: Stamp (vessel): mikyala (half-fals). Measure of whey (mishsh). With five line Kufic inscription. Fragment of rim attached. Made of green glass. Chipped at top.

title: Late Antique; Early Islamic slipper (Egypt)
desc: Leather slipper with a straight sole. The two layers of the sole have a reinforcement patch of three layers at the heel. The insole consists of two halves sewned lengthwise down the centre. The outer edges are included in the sole/upper construction (tunnel stitching). The upper consists of one layer and starts at the back of the heel. It has a red edge binding, which forms a decorative protruding element at the instep.

title: Third Intermediate amulet (Egypt)
desc: Blue/green glazed composition amulet in the form of the aegis of Hathor; double-sided; pierced longitudinally.

title: Hellenistic figure (Cyrenaica (historic))
desc: Terracotta figure of woman wearing a headdess and a veil. Mould-made from a two-piece mould and solid, originally joined to a hollow body. Traces of a white coating. Fine, dark red-brown clay, thin walled. Head only.

title: 18th Dynasty funerary cone (Egypt)
desc: Pottery funerary cone.

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Egyptian terracotta figure of a mammal, probably a cow, bull or horse. Head missing. Handmade and solid. Traces of white coating. Red-brown organic Nile silt with abundant organics, mica and white inclusions. Missing head and feet

title: stamp (Egypt)
desc: A circular stamp for a jar-stopper, with an applied stubby cylindrical grip, pierced by a horizontal hole; part is broken away. It has an incuse pattern: within a circle are three facing standing male figures, two with both arms akimbo, one with his right hand stretched out over a square object, apparently a chest. Each has a nimbus, and no doubt are saints; the centre and largest figure has an uncertain device above his nimbus. Between the heads is an equal-armed cross. Drapery falls behind eac

title: Persian Period bead (Egypt)
desc: Pair of beads; corroded; roughly hexagonal; hole through center.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains (Mediterranean)
desc: Shell of a marine gastropod mollusc of the Mediterranean 'Nassa gibbosula' species.

title: plaque (Egypt)
desc: Limestone plaque with Greek alphabetic inscription; illiterate metrical epitaph in four lines commemorating Herakleitos son of Attalos, a Macedonian. A single letter, possibly a 'delta' or uncertain meaning is preserved in the middle of the line below

title: Roman Period dice (Egypt)
desc: Square; worn; very light; incised "number" circles on sides.

title: dish (Egypt)
desc: Dish of brass.

title: dish (Egypt)
desc: Redware pottery dish with a shallow foot and convex sides expanding to the rim(chipped), the interior is inscribed with black painted Hieroglyphs including the name of Amun-Ra and an epithet and black painted Hieratic script and decorated with a representation of Amun-Ra traced in black paint.

title: 26th Dynasty trial-piece (Memphis)
desc: Quartzite trial-piece; a sunk relief representation of a falcon.

title: Roman Period; Coptic; Ptolemaic pendant; amulet (Egypt)
desc: Pendant made out of a flat, polished dark green stone (serpentine?), pierced with a small hole for suspension; incised motifs, Phoenician (less likely Greek) letters on both sides; on one side, very geometric representation of what seems to be a man (in the attitude of an orant or Bes holding animals?) with two round depression for his eyes; possibly used as an amulet.

title: Late Period bead; amulet (Egypt)
desc: Small amuletic bead in the shape of a couchant lion, carved from lapis lazuli; represented lying on a small rectangular base; simple rendering; longitudinally pierced for suspension; polished surface.

title: Late Roman; Early Byzantine stopper; seal-impression (Egypt)
desc: Jar stopper; plaster; whitish colour. Top is decorated with an impressed design of a central cross around which is a Latin inscription preceded by a cross. The latin text reads Spes in Deo (Hope in God, or in God we trust). Impression of organic material left by missing plug on the underside. Mark left by pull string on the side.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic votive box; figure (Egypt)
desc: Hollow-cast, copper alloy votive box, also known as relic-box or animal coffin; rectangular case surmounted by a solid-cast figure of a coiled snake, looped over itself to form a figure of eight; two eyelets for suspension on opposite corners of the top of the case; quite corroded; small end at the back open; such case previously contained a mummified snake (or parts of), now empty.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Laureate head of Maximian, right.; Genius standing left, holding patera & cornucopia.

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green glazed steatite scarab, elytra marked and humeral callosities present, feathered legs, pierced longitudinally, base inscribed with cartouche containing prenomen, flanked by 2 maat feathers and 2 nefer signs.

title: Byzantine; Coptic textile (Egypt)
desc: Very small fragment of woollen, multicolour tapestry (grouped warp) with plain weave at one edge; Part of a double band with pink birds and green leaves on a blue background; Warp: S-spun, c.17 tpc; Weft: S-spun, c.21 tpc in plain area, c.55 in tapestry; Edges all ragged.

title: New Kingdom funerary cone (Thebes)
desc: Pottery funerary cone, with inscription in relief.

title: 18th Dynasty plaque (Egypt)
desc: Green-glazed steatite rectangular plaque with one convex side decorated with a Wedjat-eye in low relief, longitudinally pierced, the flat side decorated with a representation of Maat within a panel surrounded by papyriform decoration.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from brownish grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with soft edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and flat top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Late Period amulet; pendant (Naukratis)
desc: Mould-made amuletic pendant in glazed composition, representing the head of the dwarf  leonine god Bes; bearded, with  protruding ears and a plumed  headdress; quite roughly modelled; worn features; flat plain back; pierced widthwise at base of crown for suspension; pale turquoise glaze, slightly decayed, with some details  (feathers, eyes, beard) roughly marked in brown glaze; complete.

title: Ptolemaic jar (Egypt)
desc: Fragment of the rim and body of a turquoise glazed composition cylindrical jar, the exterior is decorated with the upper sections of figures of Sekhmet and Ptah with indistinct hieroglyphs above.

title: Ptolemaic amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amuletic figure in glazed composition in the shape of a herm of a bearded man  (Hermes); modelling quite crude with simplified, blur features; blue turquoise glaze of rather bad quality; small suspension loop pierced widthways at the back, almost complete, loop bit chipped.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Shell of a marine bivalve mollusc of the Acanthocardia genus.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure-fitting (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast bird foot and lower leg in copper alloy; all four claws complete  with backwards facing one smaller; fitting element of a bird figure, most likely a falcon, often associated with Horus; tang at either end for attachment; complete fitting; corroded.

title: Graeco-Roman ostracon (Deir el-Bahri)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: 18th Dynasty tomb-painting (Tomb of Nebamun)
desc: Rectangular fragment (one of three) of a polychrome tomb-painting representing a banquet scene, divided into two registers: upper - three seated couples are attended by serving women and boys; lower - a group of women, seated apart from the men, are attended by a standing serving woman.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic shabti (Egypt)
desc: Blue glazed composition shabti.

title: Early Byzantine distaff (Egypt)
desc: Distaff; iron rod; one half square in section, with engraved geometrical ornament, terminating in a cross patée and surmounted by a hook; the other half round in section, probably once terminating with a ring (now broken off and lost).

title: Roman Period; Coptic; Ptolemaic pendant; amulet (Egypt)
desc: Pendant made out of a flat, polished dark green stone (serpentine?), pierced with a small hole for suspension; incised motifs, Phoenician (less likely Greek) letters on both sides; on one side, very geometric representation of what seems to be a man (in the attitude of an orant or Bes holding animals?) with two round depression for his eyes; possibly used as an amulet.

title: coin (Egypt)
desc: Gold coin.

title: Roman cup (Egypt)
desc: Greenish-colourless blown glass cup with a ground and polished rim, tapering sides and a base-ring with a central flat knob. The sides are decorated with bands of wheel-cut facets.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Late Bronze Age vessel (Cyprus)
desc: Neck of travertine vessel; slightly concave profile; stepped rim with flat surface and shallow engraved line below rim; entire neck formed separately, lower 5cm of neck designed for insertion into body of vessel; drilled and ground.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic votive box; figure (Egypt)
desc: Hollow-cast, copper alloy votive box, also known as relic-box or animal coffin; rectangular case surmounted by a solid-cast figure of a lizard; one eyelet for suspension on top of back of lizard; case opened on small back side; should contain a mummified lizard (or parts of); plater or baked clay visible inside; quite corroded.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: 25th Dynasty; 26th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Scarab carved from light brown steatite; back crudely detailed with only clypeus and head marked; at side, legs only  indicated by two horizontal grooves; underside inscribed with hieroglyphs arranged vertically: sun-disc flanked by uraeus to the top, men-draughtboard sign in the middle and shield sign (usually used to write the name of the goddess Neith) flanked by addorsed yod, underneath; longitudinally pierced.

title: 1st Dynasty; 2nd Dynasty vase (Egypt)
desc: Spheroidal vase of black-and-white andesite porphyry: with a rounded base and two tubular handles. The rim is thin, flat-topped and sharp on the outer edge. Both handles are covered with rectangular plates of thin gold, cut out into an openwork cross. The interior is well-hollowed.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Ancient Egypt jar (Asyut)
desc: Redware pottery jar with a shallow pointed base, convex sides, a short concave neck and an everted rim, decorated with a white slip.

title: Meroitic cup (Faras)
desc: Wheelmade kaolinite cup with plain rim, rounded body and rounded base. Covered in thick cream slip interior and exterior. Exterior decorated with two friezes - a border frieze around the rim, featuring black painted lotus flowers in a single row, with a red base to each flower, and a wider frieze on the belly featuring debased ankh symbols spaced. Two black painted lines are above, below, and between the friezes. The base has a painted elongated scallop pattern.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure (Egypt)
desc: Limestone ithyphallic figure of naked youth with side-lock, probably Harpocrates (Horus). Seated with head leaning forward. The figure's skin is painted pink. Fragment of head and top of torso only. Soft limestone.

title: Ptolemaic goblet (Alexandria)
desc: Painted mould-made Egyptian goblet. Lower body sherd with an applied sprig made in a plaster mould. The sprig shows a woman dancing, wearing a long chiton delineated in red, with two narrow black clavi; the right breast is outlined in black. Her hair, or wig, is also painted black; she may hold an object in her left hand, possibly castanets. The interior has a red wash. The sprig and the sherd are of the same fabric, a buff marl clay with a few particles of mica and sand.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Sherd, fragment. Inscription. Made of white, green enamelled glass.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman askos; figure; rattle (Egypt)
desc: Egyptian terracotta askos or flask in the form of pig or boar. The vessel has a cylindrical neck with a flaring mouth rising from the back of the pig; a spout, broken at its tip, is placed at the animal’s nose. The body was thrown on the wheel and then manipulated into shape, with several applied elements: the neck of the flask, the feet, the ridged mane, the ears. The tail may have been applied or modelled from the thrown pot (its tip is broken), and the eyes were formed by pressing a hollow ro

title: Roman Imperial coin (Antinoupolis)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Rosette-diademed bust of Honorius, right, draped and cuirassed.; Emperor standing facing, head right, holding standard and globe.

title: Roman Period mould; stela (Original from: Egypt)
desc: Mould of sandstone stela recording the burial of the Buchis bull in the reign of the Emperor Valerian.

title: Hellenistic askos (Egypt)
desc: Pottery askos in form of bull; wheel-made body with a finely detailed, mould-made head; the spout and handle are hand-made and applied; four short stumpy legs; strap handle from the behind the animal's head to the middle of the back; tubular spout inserted into the front of the body; hole at rear of the body, for filling the vessel or else to facilitate firing; incised branch-motif on sides of body; buff-pink clay; repaired from fragments, surface abraded in places.

title: Roman Period vessel (Egypt)
desc: Wheel-made pottery rim sherd. Orange fabric. Slightly glossy brown slip.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Ptolemaic pendant (Egypt)
desc: Gold-glass pendant with a standing figure of Harpocrates.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period; Late Period nail (Egypt)
desc: Small iron nail with square shank.

title: cast; relief (Original from: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Plaster cast of Hellenistic relief made of marble: the Apotheosis of Homer with Zeus, Apollo and the Muses. The poet is crowned  by Time and the World, and acclaimed by other personifications.

title: Ptolemaic pendant (Egypt)
desc: Greenish-blue glass pendant with Bes in relief.

title: Roman cameo (Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Cameo of glass paste imitating lapis lazuli: female bust to right.

title: Early Islamic doll (Egypt)
desc: Doll; bone; with legs and arms clearly delineated; one arm missing; the ears are pierced and have incomplete earrings attached.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Byzantine; Coptic comb (Egypt)
desc: Wooden comb with two rows of teeth; central rectangular open-work panel depicting three human figures and a lion; decorated with annules on both sides.

title: stamp-seal; seal (Egypt)
desc: Stamp-seal carved from stone; with oval device on base deeply incised with plant (palm branch?); narrow top with suspension loop now snapped off; in grey stone, smoothly polished.

title: Archaic Greek figure (Egypt)
desc: Faience headless figure of an ape.

title: Late Period amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast figure of Mahes (or Mihos), son of Bastet in copper alloy; the lion-headed male deity is depicted standing, in a striding pose, on a thin base, with his hand on both sides along his body; wearing a short kilt and the Atef crown, composed of the white crown flanked by two ostrich feathers and possibly with uraeus on front; suspension loop to the back of his head, so might have been used as an amulet; well preserved.

title: Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Mamluk dynasty bowl (Egypt)
desc: Bowl (base). Fleur-de-lys. Made of yellow glazed and scratch-marked pottery (red).

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Small mammal bone fragment.

title: Roman finger-ring (Alexandria)
desc: Gold finger-ring; hollow hoop, rounded without and expanding at the ends into roughly-shaped heads of serpents; in these have been set pear-shaped garnets (one now missing); between the ends of the hoop is soldered a crescent-shaped setting containing a garnet of that form.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: 26th Dynasty statue (Egypt)
desc: Lower part of a green basalt kneeling naophorous statue of the great steward Peftuaneith; Osiris within the shrine; three columns of incised Hieroglyphic text on the back-pillar; six columns at the front of the shrine that include the cartouches of Apries; two rows round the plinth; head and upper body lost.

title: Late Period figure; votive plaque (Lower Egypt)
desc: Reclining naked recumbing female figure, very schematic, on couch or recessed bed. At her feet and perpendicular to her body, small naked recumbing female figure, highly schematic as well, probably representing a child. Crudely carved rectangular limestone relief votive plaque. Square-cut wig to the shoulders. Red paint remains; toes detailed by incision.Soft limestone.

title: Late Period arrow-head (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy arrow-head with rhombic shape and trefoil section and socket.  Also known as trilobate, or 'Scythian type'.

title: Fatimid dynasty lamp (Egypt)
desc: Pottery lamp; hand-modelled pottery with traces of yellowish-green glaze; pig-shaped.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains (Mediterranean)
desc: Shell of a marine gastropod mollusc of the Mediterranean 'Conus Mediterraneus' species.

title: Mamluk dynasty bowl (Egypt)
desc: Bowl (base). Made of sgraffito green glazed pottery (red).

title: Greek coin (Tell el-Farain)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Diademed head of Zeus Ammon, right.; Two eagles standing on thunderbolt with wings closed, left; to left, cornucopia.

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Blue glazed composition scarab, elytra marked and humeral callosities present, pierced longitudinally, base inscribed with a cartouche, containing prenomen, flanked by three pairs of uraei.

title: Roman Period bell; crotal; votive offering; fitting (Egypt)
desc: Cast object in copper alloy; possibly a bell or a fitting (for a crotal?); thin walls, bit distorted; covered by corrosion, especially internal surface very encrusted.

title: Roman Period papyrus (Egypt)
desc: Papyrus written with black ink with demotic funerary text; vignette, 1 l. of pseudo-hieroglyphic and 11 ll. of demotic (Book of Breathings) on recto, 1 l. of demotic on verso.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Third Intermediate textile; cloth (Egypt)
desc: Fragment of linen cloth;  medium-coarse quality. Multiple blue band above long twisted warp fringe; other edges torn or ragged. The weave is plain [warp-faced simple tabby] and the threads are S-spun and S-spliced; the stitch gauge is warp c.17 ends per 10mm and weft 9 picks per 10mm. The warp fringe is made of two groups of 3-4 threads "z-plied" then "s-twisted" together.

title: 18th Dynasty; 19th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green glazed steatite scarab with the elytra marked and humeral callosities present; longitudinally pierced; base inscribed with the prenomen of Thutmose III.

title: Hellenistic figure (Egypt)
desc: Head of gypsum plaster life-sized female figure; eyes once held inlays(?); damage to lips.

title: Coptic; Byzantine stela (Egypt)
desc: Limestone stela of Pleinos(?); Chi-Rho within a wreath at the top; Greek text below; cross patee and two ankhs at the bottom.

title: Roman bottle; unguent-bottle (Egypt)
desc: Greenish blown glass bottle; rim outsplayed and folded up and in; tall cylindrical neck with angular junction with body; flattened 'disc' body of true 'candlestick' type; base concave, pontil-mark.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast copper alloy figure-group consisting of a king with nemes offering a ring-shaped object to a divine child with sidelock, uraeus, headcap, protruding headband, and lunar disc (Khonspakhered ?) squatting on a lotus. Child, lotus, king and base are all separate elements.

title: bowl (Alexandria)
desc: Rim sherd from a colourless buff-tinged glass convex bowl with vertical facets and four horizontal bands on the exterior.

title: Umayyad dynasty vessel-stamp (Egypt)
desc: Stamp (vessel): mikyala. Measure of whey. Six line Kufic inscription. Made of green glass.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure (Egypt)
desc: Limestone sculpture fragment in Egyptian style of child with a shaved head and side-lock. Probably from an ithyphallic Harpocrates figure. Traces of black paint around eyes (?). Badly worn.

title: Roman Period papyrus (Egypt)
desc: Funerary papyrus containing Second Book of Breathing written in hieratic script. Text on recto and verso.

title: Ancient Egypt artefact; brick; weight (Naukratis)
desc: Clay fragment resembling part of a wasp's or bee's nest. possibly a ballast brick fragment

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period; Late Period nail (Egypt)
desc: Small iron nail with square shank.

title: Kerma Classique sandal
desc: Sandal for the right foot, one of a pair with 55441.1, made of leather, with rounded toe, toe strap missing, part of ankle straps remaining, badly worn.

title: Hellenistic; Roman hinge (Egypt)
desc: Ivory cylindrical hinge-piece (?) with two lathe turned bands and carved notch at one end; hole drilled into centre on one side but not pierced through; polished surface.

title: Graeco-Roman ostracon (Thebes)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Ptolemaic; Late Period amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast amuletic figure of the god Harpokrates, or other divine child, in copper alloy; god represented as naked male child, slightly bent, with left arm by his side, the other one bent to hold right hand to his mouth; wearing the sidelock of youth to the right side of his head; suspension ring at the back; complete, but worn and very corroded.

title: Coptic; Byzantine stela (Egypt)
desc: Limestone stela decorated with a representation of a dove and a wreath.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Byzantine; Coptic textile (Egypt)
desc: Fragment of wool, weft-faced plain weave textile, mainly plain cream or yellow, leaf-shaped tapestry (single warp threads) motif in red and dark blue; Double row of twining; Warp: S spun, c.10 tpc; Weft: S spun, c.32 tpc in plain areas, c.45 in tapestry; Edges: one selvedge with fringe (loops apparently on both sides), others ragged

title: New Kingdom shabti (Egypt)
desc: Twelve painted wooden shabtis of Henutmehyt.

title: Hellenistic; Ptolemaic block (Egypt)
desc: A rectangular panel with a raised border, now damaged.  There are seven lines of inscription with the traces of incised lines.  The inscription is a dedication of a precinct to Poseidon Hippios by Chares, son of Apollonides, the priest, on behalf of Ptolemy V Epiphanes and his queen Cleopatra.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic bead; seal; amulet (Egypt)
desc: Tubular bead in glazed composition; elaborate incised decoration consisting of standing male figure holding long bent staff, scorpion and snake; appears like a cylinder seal; amuletic function?; pierced longitudinally for suspension; light turquoise glaze, quite worn; yellowish porous paste.

title: Roman Period; Coptic textile; hair-net (Egypt)
desc: Fragmentary conical hair-net of undyed linen which is now straw-coloured to dark brown; patterned with lozenges produced by areas of dense and less dense netting; the hair-het is drawn into the central point by increasing the grouping together of the individual threads; part of both sides of the opening preserved with, on one side of a seam, a solid edge formed by two cords passing through short loops and, on other side, longer loops; the draw string now missing; a lock of human hair is preserve

title: 26th Dynasty coffin (Thebes)
desc: Base and lid of the anthropoid outer coffin of Ameniryirt, Priest of Amun and Servant in the Household of Amenirdis, foot of the lid is broken away, decorated with polychrome painted vignettes and texts from the Book of the Dead, showing the deceased before Ra-Horakhty, enthroned, and Osiris with Anubis, below, mummifying the body, the deceased is also represented adoring a solar barque, inside the base Anubis, flanked by Isis and Nephthys, is shown supporting the mummy of the deceased.

title: 19th Dynasty stela (Egypt)
desc: Limestone stela of Hormin: a small round-topped stela consisting of two registers with incised texts and figures in sunk relief. In the upper register Thoth, lord of Hermopolis, stands on the right before an altar on which rests a water-pot cooled by a lotus-flower. Amun-Ra, lord of the thrones of the two lands and foremost of Ipet-Sut, is seated on a throne in the centre and behind him stand Mut and Khons. In the lower register the wʿb-priest and temple-scribe Hormin is kneeling on the right in

title: Coptic; Early Byzantine; Roman Period bell (Egypt)

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)

title: Ancient Egypt burnisher (Naukratis)

title: Late Period inlay; artefact (Egypt)
desc: Roughly rectangular, oblong object, finished at three sides, the fourth broken off. Outer surface smooth with four incised circles with central dot; small drilled hole near one of the finished edges, possibly so as to attach the piece to another object (as inlay?). Carved from large mammal bone then polished and incised.

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)

title: Ptolemaic lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery plastic lamp in the form of Harpokrates as a nude seated boy, wearing an elaborate head-dress and a bell round his neck, and holding a shrine of Athena in his left arm and a torch in his right hand. Athena wears a long chiton and helmet, and holds a shield and spear. The boy sits on a lamp has a slightly domed top and a concave-sided round-topped nozzle. On top of the head is a pierced lug for suspension, decorated with impressed ovules. Covered with a red slip.

title: Roman flask (Egypt)

title: Second Intermediate; 18th Dynasty axe (Egypt)
desc: Large, heavy bronze axe-head: with a circular blade and an angular butt. It is thickly corroded and in poor condition. It has broken in two at the waist and been repaired in modern times with a metal band. One of the rear corners is missing. Traces of textile survive in the corrosion product.

title: Roman bottle; unguentarium (Egypt)
desc: Bluish-green blown glass bottle with a rim folded outwards, upwards and inwards, a cylindrical neck tooled at the base, an inverted convex triangular body, and a flattened base with no pontil mark.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)

desc: Pottery jug.

title: foot-rasp (Egypt)

title: Ptolemaic figure; theatre mask (Egypt)
desc: Egyptian terracotta model or figure attachment in form of comedy mask. A model of a dramatic mask, an Old Man from the New Comedy with a short beard or, less likely, a  Slave. He has a wrinkled brow, heavy eyebrows, large eyeballs and a wide open mouth. Finished along its lower edge, it is broken away elsewhere. Pressed into a one-piece mould, and the mouth cut out; the beard is striated by paring on the outside. A white wash or coating is preserved, over which red and black paint were added and

It was also interpreted as a model steering-oar from sacred boat from a model funerary barge or, judging by its size, from the processional bark of a god; the well-carved jackal-head on the butt suggest either Anubis or Wepwawet. The blade is slightly flattened and widened continuation of the loom, without a shoulder, and the loom is stout. Most of the length has been covered with a black substence containing beeswax, oil or fat, and a small amount of

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Period jar; vessel (Egypt)

The decoration is in the form of a cross-hatched lozenge, with circles in the centre of each section; the double lines that define the space and the circles are painted red. Maybe givemn a red wash, but no slip. Pronounced wheel-made ribs on inside, that also has dark staining from pitch(?). Red-brown Nile silt with abundant mica.

title: Late Christian Period comb (Qasr Ibrim)
desc: A wooden comb with flat broad ends, curved so that the comb is wider at the edges than the middle. The tapering teeth are shorter in the centre than those on the ends.  Most of the 27 large teeth on one edge are broken. Most of the 70 fine closely-space teeth on the other edge are preserved.  Decoration comprises incised concentric circles; on one side a central group of 9 and two pairs at the ends. On the other a central pair and two groups of 6 concentric circles at the ends.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))

desc: Pottery funerary cone.

title: New Kingdom amulet (Egypt)

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Period oil-lamp (Egypt)

title: Ancient Egypt artefact; fitting (Naukratis)

desc: Pottery ostracon.

The weave is plain [warp-faced simple tabby] and the threads are S-spun and S-spliced; the stitch gauge is warp c.36 ends per 10mm and weft 18 picks per 10mm. Holes partly due to insect damage.

title: Roman beaker (Egypt)

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure-fitting (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast headdress in copper alloy; representing a Hathoric crown, formed of sun disc and horns, with a uraeus wearing a small Hathoric crown welded at the base of the sun disc; belongs to a figure of a goddess, maybe Hathor or more likely Isis; small tang to insert the crown on the top of the figure; relatively well preserved; link between uraeus and large Hathoric crown shows some modern repairs.

title: Late Period figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Egyptian ithyphallic limestone figure. Representation of naked youth with side-lock, probably Harpocrates. Seated with knees raised and phallus extending forward and up. Crudely carved from soft Egyptian limestone. Tip of phallus missing and eroded.

title: Hellenistic brazier (Turkey)

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)

Naked boy riding a quadruped, probably a lion (?) or great cat (?); human figure broken off above waist, and at ankles; animal, missing head and legs, variously broken; fragment of tail survives at back; glaze greenish, with luminous surface; core- dark buff, very hard.

title: 12th Dynasty cylinder seal (Egypt)

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))

desc: Fragment of papyrus bearing 7 lines of Coptic text written against the fibres (verso).  Part of a letter.

title: Late Period amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast amuletic figure in copper alloy; depicting a jackal-headed deity, most likely Anubis or Wepwawet, in striding pose; head of jackal with erect ears, facing forward, arms by side; very crude and flat design; suspension loop at the back of the head; broken off below knees; corroded.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period jug (Egypt)
desc: Pottery jug.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; polished surface; chipped top.

title: Roman finger-ring (Egypt)
desc: Gold finger-ring; set with sardonyx.

title: Phoenician; Cypro-Archaic; Late Period scarab (Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition (faience) scarab with inscribed motif on base; mould-made and glazed (now white but traces of green remain); perforated for suspension; simple detailing on the beetle; on the base is a lion passant, facing the left, above which are crudely incised hieroglyphic signs (Gardiner V 30 and N 17, then R8 and F 35, plus several unidentifiable ones), probably 'Lord of Two Lands'; incised line borders the field; upper left part of the base is broken.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic aegis; amulet; votive offering (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast aegis in copper alloy; amulet depicting broad collar with stepped upper edge topped by head of Sekhmet, or other lion-headed goddess, wearing a tripartite wig, with sundisk and uraeus in front; behind sun-disk, loop for suspension, and below counterweight; part of disk and counterweight at back broken off; corroded, surface very worn off.

title: Late Period figure; votive plaque (Lower Egypt)
desc: Reclining female figure on couch or recessed bed. Broken, feet missing. Crudely carved rectangular limestone relief votive plaque. Square-cut wig to the shoulders. Soft limestone.

title: Ancient Egypt nail (Naukratis)
desc: Fragment of a bronze nail; broad, flat head; heavily incrusted.

title: Late Period; 26th Dynasty amulet (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amulet in glazed composition representing either a Djed pillar or  the ancient Egyptian deities Heh or Shu, leaning against a plain back pillar; degenerate form of amulet with highly crude and schematic representation; body of amulet crossed by more or less deep horizontal grooves; suspension hole pierced widthways; light greenish turquoise glaze; complete.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Laureate head of Maximian, right.; Genius standing left, holding patera & cornucopia.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Red pottery ostracon with buff slip, from the body of a vessel, with remains of eight lines of black-painted demotic text on the exterior.

title: Late Roman; Early Byzantine weight (Egypt)
desc: Copper alloy weight; square; face engraved with herring-bone wreath enclosing Latin cross flanked by denominational mark; in each corner of the face a pattern composed of six punched dots; file marks on the sides and base.

title: 26th Dynasty amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Gold amuletic pendant: this tiny solid cast figure represents Ptah, the creator god of Memphis, patron of craftsmen. Characteristically, he is depicted mumrniforrn with only his hands emerging to grasp a 'was' sceptre with its distinctive slanting animals head. However, he does not wear his usual straight divine beard, and instead of wearing a skull-cap appears to have a shaven head. There is a suspension loop in the small of his back.

title: Ptolemaic amphora (Alexandria)
desc: Fragment of shoulder and wall of Painted red ware pottery table amphora. Red slip outside. Horizontal black and white bands, framing a row of white, almost vertical strokes and a band of white tendrils and berries. Micaceous orange brown Nile silt with few white inclusions.

title: Late Period bead (Egypt)
desc: Small circular white faience fragment with central hole. Probably fragment of bead.

title: Late Period amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amuletic figure in glazed composition, representing the hippopotamus-headed goddess Taweret; in striding pose with arms on her sides and a long tail decorated  with incised chevrons; small loop pierced widthwise, above head, for  suspension; relatively well modelled representation, even though slightly schematic; light turquoise glaze well preserved; almost complete, right leg slightly chipped.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from dark grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; top worn.

title: Roman vessel; unguent-bottle (Egypt)
desc: Greenish blown glass vessel; cylindrical neck with constriction at base; inverted triangular body with convex sides; flat base, no pontil-mark.

title: Coptic; Islamic textile (Egypt)
desc: Two fragments of undyed linen, with narrow band in mauve wool (c.3mm wide); (a)=c.4x24cm (b)=1.5x21cm; warp-undyed linen (S/c.24), weft-undyed linen (S/c.22), mauve wool (Z/c.3 paired); one simple selvedge, other edges cut or torn; supplementary weft passes over and under groups of 8 warp ends

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Shell of a marine bivalve mollusc of the Mediterranean Arca noae species. This valve joins with 2012.5016.28.

title: 18th Dynasty heart-scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green basalt heart-scarab, elytra marked, unpierced, base inscribed with eight lines of hieroglyphs.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight; tool/implement (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Fatimid dynasty filter; jug (Egypt)
desc: Filter set in neck of larger handled jug sherd, incised and pierced with inscription with surround of radial bars. Made of unglazed pottery (earthenware), buff slip.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; polished surface; complete.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Shell of a marine bivalve mollusc of the Mediterranean Arca noae species. This valve joins with 2012.5016.29.

title: Ptolemaic papyrus (Egypt)
desc: Book of the Dead of Tasheretenmin in many fragments mounted in four frames, in hieratic script. Drawn vignettes.

title: Late Period amulet; figure (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made amuletic figure in glazed composition, representing Harpokrates; god  represented as naked male child, in striding position on a rectangular base, leaning against a back pillar; left arm to his side, right arm bent to bring his hand to his mouth, and with the traditional child-lock on the left side of the head; very roughly made; very crude and blur features; suspension hole pierced widthways partly in the back pillar behind his neck; pale green glaze, mostly decayed; complete.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Limestone ithyphallic figure of naked youth, probably Harpocrates (Horus). Seated with knees raised on a rectangular plaque, and phallus extending forward clasped in both hands. The figure's skin was painted red (?). Soft limestone. Head lost.

title: Late Period bead (Egypt)
desc: Round bead in glaed composition; stylised rosette; top with added yellow glaze in relief in the centre; underside flat; worn glaze; surface worn and white.

title: Archaic Greek artefact; slab (Lower Egypt)
desc: Limestone slab bearing a grid of incised lines and graffito letters on one side and graffiti on the other.

title: Late Christian Period; Post-Christian textile (Nubia)
desc: Three fragments of plain weave, buff, cotton texile; largest piece (warp and weft: 1 ply Z, dia. 0.2-0.8mm, 12x13 tpc) has remains of a sewn opening, sewn in running stitch using cream yarn (1 ply S, dia. 0.5mm). The third fragment (220x110mm, plain weave, 13x13tpc) has a section turned over (sewn with running stitch) to form a triangular patch. There is a hole at the middle of the longest edge of the triangle and another similar hole in the centre of the fragment; edges ragged.

title: Roman Period; Byzantine stopper; seal-impression (Egypt)
desc: White plaster wine-amphora stopper with a rough back with many deep criss cross lines, the impressions of organic matter, probably of a palm fibre plug (now lost). The stopper is made from lime plaster or lime and gypsum  mix. It has been impressed with a circular stamp. The stamp appears to depict a Chi-Rho surrounded with a mutilated Greek inscription.

title: Third Intermediate papyrus (Thebes)
desc: Third Intermediate Period Book of the Dead of Dimutiudu in hieratic script, extracted from Osiris figure EA9872 into which it had been inserted without its opening vignette in the nineteenth century.

title: New Kingdom; Third Intermediate textile; cloth (Egypt)
desc: Textile fragment; torn strip of linen cloth, medium to fine quality. Undyed, bleached (through washing?); colour now cream/greyish-cream. Edges torn. The weave is plain [warp-faced simple tabby] and the threads are S-spun and S-spliced; the stitch gauge is warp c.28 ends per 10mm and weft c.16 picks per 10mm.

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: Plaster cast, white colour, of unfinished sandstone relief-block from the rear chapel wall of pyramid N17 at Meroë, Sudan.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Sherd (rim). With pseudo-inscription. Made of white slipware and olive-yellow glazed and incised pottery (red).

title: Umayyad dynasty weight (Egypt)
desc: Weight: fals weight of 30 kharrūba. Ten line Kufic inscription. Made of green glass.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from reddish grey hard stone (basalt?); carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; polished surface; complete.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Fragment of right mandible bone from sheep or goat.

title: New Kingdom funerary cone (Egypt)
desc: Pottery funerary cone.

title: Ptolemaic stela (Saqqara)
desc: Round-topped limestone stela; scene of the deceased adoring Osiris; eight lines of Hieroglyphic text and two of Demotic.

title: Roman bottle; unguent-bottle (Egypt)
desc: Greenish blown glass bottle; rim folded; cylindrical neck with constriction at base; inverted triangular body with convex sides; concave base, no pontil-mark.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; broken in half.

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: Plaster cast, white colour, of sandstone relief-block from the rear chapel wall of pyramid N17 at Meroë, Sudan. Cast depicts part of a figure bearing an incense-burner; unfinished.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Limestone ithyphallic figure of naked youth with side-lock, probably Harpocrates (Horus). Seated with knees raised on a rectangular plaque, and phallus extending forward on which a tambour or drum rests, clasped in both hands. The figure's skin is painted red. Soft limestone. Tip of phallus missing. Straw adheres to mud on base.

title: Cypro-Archaic II; Cypro-Classical I; Eastern Mediterranean ring (Egypt)
culture: Cypro-Archaic II; Cypro-Classical I; Eastern Mediterranean
period: Cypro-Archaic II; Cypro-Classical I; Eastern Mediterranean
desc: Fragment of a silver ring; made of spirally curved silver wire, parts of four turns survive.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Sherd (base). With deer, blue and white. Made of cobalt decorated and glazed, crackled pottery (buff).

title: Ancient Egypt kohl-stick (Naukratis)
desc: Bronze kohl stick; slender, round, tapered; incrusted.

title: Iron Age bead (Phoenicia (historic))
desc: Small glass eye-bead. Rod-formed. Wide bore. Light turquoise background with eye motifs in blue and white.

title: Late Period game-piece; figure (Egypt)
desc: Gaming piece with head of a figure carved from limestone. Very crude work. Circular body shape.

title: Late Period figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Egyptian ithyphallic limestone figure. Representation of naked youth, probably Harpocrates. Seated on a rectangular base with knees raised and phallus extending forward. Crudely carved from soft Egyptian limestone. Traces of pink paint. Head lost.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Graeco-Roman ostracon (Karnak)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Hellenistic bowl (Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Hemispherical laced glass bowl; colourless with translucent yellow threads and translucent brown rim; rounded rim; hemispherical body; convex base; body formed by a pair of coils made from twisted canes of bichrome glass wound spirally from the centre of the base to below the rim; each cane was made by drawing out and twisting a double gather of colourless glass and yellowish glass; the coils were laid side by side but with their thread decorations in opposite directions, thus forming a chevron

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman nail (Naukratis)
desc: Copper alloy nail.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Bronze miniature lamp with an elongated carinated body, a wide filling-hole, and a flaring round-tipped nozzle, broken at its end, with an oval wick-hole. At the rear is a ring handle, much lost. The lamp stands on a flat pointed-oval resting-surface.

title: Meroitic textile; garment; fringe (Nubia)
desc: Fragment of an elaborate fringe, probably from a garment, in undyed cotton and blue wool, the upper part a latticework band, below, hanging cotton threads.

title: Early Christian Period textile; garment; mantle; cloak (Nubia)
