desc: Fragment of thick wool cloth, probably from a cloak, mainly dark brown naturally pigmented with a band of brocaded decoration in pink, dark green, dark blue, dark red and undyed wool, a repeating motif of dotted lozenge with vertical bars to each side. Selvedge in contrasting dark blue weft.

title: Roman Period oil-lamp; money-box (Egypt)
desc: Pottery money-box(?) or oil-lamp(?). Pale buff slip (may not be intentional) covers the surface in places. Burnishing on lower wall; domed upper body with round central aperture, strongly hipped carination and deep body tapering to flat base; rectangular slot through base and lower wall; two large chips to rim. Red-brown Nile silt with frequent fine-medium mica and fine-coarse lime.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Bust of Severus II, radiate, draped & cuirassed; right.; Emperor standing right, receving small Victory on globe from Jupiter, leaning on sceptre; left.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic votive box; figure (Egypt)
desc: Hollow-cast copper alloy votive box, also known as relic-box or animal coffin; rectangular case surmounted by a solid-cast figure of a coiled snake, looped over itself to form a figure of eight; two eyelets for suspension on opposite corners of top of case; one small end sealed and the other open; two modern drill holes in base; corroded; such case previously contained a mummified snake (or parts of), now empty.

title: 26th Dynasty stela (Thebes (historic - Upper Egypt))
desc: Sycomore fig wood stela of Padiamunranebwaset: this crudely painted round-topped stela contains three registers. In the upper register is a winged sun-disk with uraei. In the middle register the figure of Osiris stands on the right next to an altar on which rests a water-pot cooled by a lotus. On the other side of the altar stand the four sons of Horus. Below are four lines of text containing a prayer to Osiris on behalf of Padiamunranebwaset, son of Kʒp.f-n-ḥʒ-Mnṯw and the lady (Tʒ)-šrỉ(t)-(n)-

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Fragment of a statuette of a kitharode. Man, playing kithara, upper part only. Compact white Egyptian limestone. The musician holds his instrument against his left shoulder, grasping it with his left hand while he plucks the strings with his right. The whole figure missing from the waist down; also the left fore-arm with much of the kithara, the right shoulder and right upper arm. Chips missing from nose and hair to either side of head. Traces of black on the hair and the right eye; faint traces

title: Roman finger-ring (Alexandria)
desc: Gold finger-ring; flattened oval bezel with engraved design: kneeling figure.

title: Ptolemaic vase (Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Fragment of Ptolemaic Alexandrian painted and moulded red-ware pottery goblet or amphorae. Dark brown, red, pink and white. Red-brown micaceous Nile silt fabric.

title: Ayyubid dynasty painting (Egypt)
desc: Painting; A battle beneath the walls of a town.  Two horses almost collide in the foreground, while archers and footmen fight in the distance.  No text.  Opaque watercolour on paper.

title: token (Issued in: Egypt)
desc: Brass token, value of 30.; Inscription at centre.; Inscription and value at centre.

title: Ancient Egypt architecture; artefact; grinding-stone (Cemetery)
desc: Fragment of circular lense-shaped object with sharp edges, in white-grey translucent marble; 1.7cm wide zone around edges on both sides smoothed, centre roughly picked (the centre preserved only on one side, other side broken off so not sure if same here); fragment of architecture reused as a grinding-stone (?).

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Egyptian-style nude female terracotta figure with a column/pillar along the back as seen on amulets of Isis-Hathor. Traces of a thick white coating. Red-brown Nile silt fabric. Base is missing. Mould-made and solid. Red-brown Nile silt with grey surfaces and core, abundant mica and sand inclusions. Body missing head.

title: Late Period amulet (Egypt)
desc: Amulet carved from white stone picked out in black (diorite or possibly more likely soapstone) , in the shape of a one-sided right wedjat-eye; detailing of eye and brow lightly and accurately incised; pupil picked out in black, now faded; ridged tubular loop for suspension above, pierced lengthwise; scaltered black marks on front and back.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains (Red Sea)
desc: Shell of a marine gastropod mollusc of the Conomurex fasciatus species.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Mamluk dynasty textile; furnishing (Egypt)
desc: Fragment (from a stripe) of undyed (unbleached) linen embroidered in dark blue cotton. Field design of repeating zigzags with small flowers at points. Border of lozenges and angular interlace. Simple tabby and darning stitch. Warp: linen, S-spun, ca 16 threads/cm. Weft: linen, S-spun, ca 12 threads/cm. Edges: on plain selvedge, two torn edges and one hemmed edge. Sewing: hemming in S-spun-Z-ply linen thread; embroidery in Z-spun cotton.

title: Ancient Egypt artefact (Naukratis)
desc: Fragment of a base?, square in shape, broken at one side, cylindrical protrusion on one side and square cut out on other. Red paint on side with cylindrical protrusion. Sandstone or fine sandy marl clay.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp decorated on the discus with a slave holding an amphora. The wide rounded shoulder is decorated with plant tendrils in relief. On top of the nozzle is an indistinct mask. At the rear is an applied handle (broken); on each side is an ornamental lug. Within the pseudo-base-ring are traces of an impressed inscription.

title: Ptolemaic stela (Egypt)
desc: Round-topped limestone stela of Tjaiemhotep: the size of this piece alone indicates the importance of Tjaiemhotep own right. In the figured scene, beneath the usual winged sun disc, Tjaiemhotep herself stands at the right with hands raised in the attitude of prayer. Anatomically correctly, but by Egyptian artistic conventions most unusually, she is depicted with both breasts shown. The hieroglyphs before her provide her title, name and mother's name alone.  The  signs above the conventionally he

title: Roman Period oil-lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made terracotta 'frog' oil-lamp with incised frog motif. An ovoid oil-lamp, with deep, rounded sides, and a nozzle fully merged with the body. The upper surface of the oil-lamp is decorated with a conventionalized pattern of a frog. Brown Nile silt. Rude work.

title: 20th Dynasty ostracon (Deir el-Medina (Thebes))
desc: Ostracon with the 'Instruction of Amennakht': limestone ostracon painted with twelve lines of hieratic. This copy is well written on a carefully chosen ostracon and the scribe's handwriting seems modelled on Amennakht's own. It was certainly an exercise, since the date is written in red under the final line, as was a common practice of apprentices. The red points mark the ends of lines of verse; in 11. 9-10 a mistake has been made, and there is one point too many (similar mistakes occurs in 11.5

title: 18th Dynasty stela (Egypt)
desc: Round-topped limestone stela of Merysekhmet depicting the deceased child seated on the lap of his mother with an offering-table to the right and wedjat-eyes and Hieroglyphic text above; two rows of Hieroglyphic text below.

title: New Kingdom scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green glass scarab, elytra marked and humeral callosities present, pierced longitudinally, base inscribed with cartouche containing prenomen flanked by 2 uraei, Hathor-head flanked by 2 uraei below.

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: Plaster cast, white colour, of sandstone relief-block from the rear chapel wall of pyramid N17 at Meroë, Sudan. Cast depicts the upper body of Isis, facing right, with left arm raised; hair of figure draped over right shoulder.

title: Ancient Egypt rod; hook (miscellaneous); staple
desc: Bent iron rod, corroded, broken off at one, possibly both ends. Possibly a hook or staple.

title: 18th Dynasty finger-ring (Alexandria)
desc: Gold finger-ring: with a swivelling cornelian bezel in form of a cat on a plinth.

title: Umayyad dynasty coin-weight (Egypt)
desc: Coin-weight: dīnār. Seven line Kufic inscription. Made of green glass.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period jug (Egypt)
desc: Pottery jug.

title: Hellenistic transport amphora (Rhodes)
desc: Fragment of lip, neck and oval, tapering handle to sharp turn of a Rhodian pottery transport amphora. Clay fine buff; cream slip. Rectangular stamp with torch symbol.

title: Roman Period papyrus (Egypt)
desc: Funerary papyrus of Apollodotus containing Second Book of Breathing (abridged version) written in hieratic script recto and verso.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Ancient Egypt dish (Naukratis)
desc: Dish in copper alloy; convex with thick, rolled rim; flat base; thick walls; heavily modeled; incrusted.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Qing dynasty snuff-bottle (China)
desc: Snuff-bottle. On one face, a red flower like an iris with black leaves, on the other and inscription. Made of turquoise enamelled ceramic, porcelain.

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Cast figure in copper alloy; depicting a jackal-headed deity, most likely Anubis or Upuaut (Wepwawet), seating with arms resting on his thighs; most of surface is missing; corroded.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: bead; 珠 (和田)
desc: Etched cornelian bead; square tabular with single diagonal drilled perforation; partly pink, partly dark brown, decorated on both sides with the same "etched" design of  small equal-armed cross set within a double border of diamonds, the cross being orientated in the same axis as the perforation; complete but chipped.

title: arrow-head (敦煌)
desc: Arrowhead with a triangular body, notchless and stemmed. Made of bronze.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Spout (?) of a vessel in the shape of a giraffe's (?) head. The animal was moulded very carefully, additional detail was added using incised lines.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: fascine (敦煌)
desc: Sample of calcined reeds, originally coming from a stack of fascines.

title: Qing dynasty; Xianfeng coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: coin; amulet; spade-money (China)
dateDisplay: 19thC (date of production (?)); 20thC (date of production (?)); 10 (inscription of original spade-money); 7 (inscription of original round coin in the design)
desc: Copper alloy coin-shaped charm. Modelled on the spade-money issued by  Wang Mang in AD 10. The side illustrated here gives the inscription found on  the obverse (front) of Wang Mang spade money, written in the 'hanging  needle' style of calligraphy. The hole at the top is designed to look like another of Wang Mang's coins, this time a round coin with a square hole, issued in AD 7.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Clay figurine of a monkey. The animal is shown supporting its head with its left hand while the right hand clasps the left elbow. Although very small, the monkey is depicted in great detail, with the fur and fingers rendered by using incised lines.

title: vessel (Khotan)
desc: Cylindrical wood vessel. The vessel is decorated with three standing figures alternating with three panels decorated with characters. The decoration was carved in relief, additional details were added with incesed lines. The vessel was made of dark wood and has a lot of sand deposits on the surface.

title: coin (Khotan)
desc: Coin. Sino-Kharoshthi coin. (Awaiting full details).

title: wall-painting (敦煌)
desc: Fragment of a wall-painting with a representation of a Bodhisattva. Only the head and the upper body remain. The Bodhisattva with black hair, red lips and bright green eyebrows has both hands folded before his chest (anjali mudra). He is portrayed wearing heavy jewellery including large earrings in the elongated earlobes and a necklace. Remains of a crown are also visible. All the outlines are in black and were then coloured in. The pigments used are still very vibrant. The backing material used

title: Qing dynasty coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Jiaqing coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: seal (敦煌)
desc: Wooden seal. On one side the small rectangular block-seal has two Chinese characters carved into it which Stein (1921, 778) identified as chang shou (長寿/ literally"prolonged old age"). On the other side, one additional character was carved which has not been completed. The seal is pierced transversely.

title: arrow (敦煌)
desc: Fragment of a wooden arrow shaft. Partly decorated with black paint.

title: coin (Löwenstein)
desc: Silver coin.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Stucco plaque showing the Buddha standing on a lotus throne. His right hand is raised to the shoulder, palm facing outward, in the abhaya mudra (the gesture of no-fear), the left hand holds the robe. The head with the elongated earlobes, the black hair and the protuberance is sourrounded by a halo. The pigments which were used to decorate the figurehave mostly flaked off, only some white pgments can still be seen.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Head of a deity. Made of stucco.

title: skull-cap (Turfan)
desc: Skull-cap; made of cotton; four triangles of black cotton, sides sewn together to make peak; four rectangles sewn around bottom of triangles to make sides; two opposing sides bent inward so cap lies flat; inside lined with red cotton; outside embroidered with white curvilinear designs on each triangle, band of white wavy lines around side.

title: capital; model (Xinjiang)
desc: Capital ( with shaft). Made of terracotta.

title: figure (Xinjiang)
desc: Clay figure of a standing monkey. The animal holds both arms in front of its chest, the left arm supporting its chin. The figure was moulded using red clay and covered with a brown slip before being fired.

title: Qing dynasty coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: vessel (Khotan)
desc: Wall sherd of a wheel-thrown clay vessel with an appliqué in the shape of a woman. Only her head and upper body remain. She is shown in three quarter view, her right arm raised to her shoulder.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Stucco head of a male figure. Mould-made, with very delicated features.

title: coin (Khotan)
desc: Coin. Sino-Kharoshthi coin. (Awaiting full details).

title: wall-painting (敦煌)
desc: Fragment of a wall-painting with a representation of a Bodhisattva. Only the head and the upper body remain. The Bodhisattva with black hair, red lips and bright green eyebrows has both hands folded before his chest (anjali mudra). He is portrayed wearing heavy jewellery including large earrings in the elongated earlobes and a necklace. Remains of a crown are also visible. All the outlines are in black and were then coloured in. The pigments used are still very vibrant. The backing material used

title: figure; vessel (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary round clay plaque depicting a human face in full frontal view. The face is very broad with bulging eyes, flared nostrils and short rounded ears which are adorned with earrings. The fact that the figure is flat on the reverse side suggests that it was applied to another object, probably a vessel.

title: Qing dynasty coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: vessel (Khotan)
desc: Wall sherd of a wheel-thrown clay vessel with an appliqué in the shape of a woman. Only her head and upper body remain. She is shown in three quarter view, her right arm raised to her shoulder.

title: Qing dynasty; Jiaqing coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: block; incense-holder (敦煌)
desc: Wooden block. The object has an octagonal shape and six round holes drilled into the upper side. Traces of black paint are visible on the outside. The use of the object is not entirely clear but it could be some sort of incense holder.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary clay figure of horse and rider. Only the legs of the rider remain, the upper body is missing. The figure was moulded using grey clay and details such as mane, harness etc. have been rendered using incised lines and dashes.

title: coin (Khotan)
desc: Coin. Sino-Kharoshthi coin. (Awaiting full details).

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: vessel; figure (Khotan)
desc: Wall sherd of a ceramic vessel. The outside of the wheel-thrown vessel was decorated with an appliqué in the shape of a lion's head. The animal is shown in full frontal view, head surrounded by the mane. Right above the lion, the remains of a handle can still be seen. The vessel was further decorated with incised lines.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Buddha head. Made of stucco.

title: medal (Shanghai)
desc: Struck brass uniface medal with red enamel decoration and red and yellow ribbon with bar attachment.; The Chinese flag above the outline shapes of the five provinces/regions in the northwest of China: Xinjiang, Qinghai, Gansu, Inner Mongolia and Ningxia. Below this are two bands bearing the inscription – one red, one white.

title: helmet (Miran)
desc: Fragment of a helmet. The helmet was made of leather which was reinforced with a coating of red and black lacquer. A thin strip has been left untreated and thus the leather is visible.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Fragmentary clay figure of a camel. Only the front part of the animal remains, the forelegs are also missing. The fur is indicated by incised lines.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Round clay figure depicting a lion's face. The animal is shown in full frontal view with its head surrounded by the mane. The representation is very stylised. The fact that the figure is flat on the reverse side suggests that it was applied to another object, probably a vessel.

title: coin (Löwenstein)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Tang dynasty; 唐代 armour; 盔甲 (China)
desc: Fragment of armour consisting of six rectangular scales. They were made of leather reinforced by several layers of red and black leather. The scales were then joined together with leather strips.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Head of a deity. Made of pigmented stucco.

title: Qing dynasty; Xianfeng coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary round clay figure depicting a lion's face. The animal is shown in full frontal view with its head surrounded by the mane. The object was made using a mould and covered with a white slip before being applied to another object, probably a vessel.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary clay figurine in the shape of a horse's head. The object was moulded in a rudimentary fashion and covered with a light brown slip before being fired. Additonal detail was added using incised lines.

title: architectural fitting; ornament (Khotan (town))
desc: Rosette with petals. Made of terracotta.

title: Qing dynasty; Xianfeng coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Mask of a face: bottom section missing. Made of terracotta.

title: Republic banknote (Issued in: Xinjiang (autonomous region))
desc: Banknote.

title: coin (Löwenstein)
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Qing dynasty coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Avalokiteśvara (Object); 聖觀自在菩薩 (Object)
desc: woodblock print with added mount and colours

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Clay plaque in the shape of griffin. The griffin is shown with a horses head and neck as well as a beak. The fact that the object is flat suggests that it was used as an appliqué for another object, probably a vessel.

title: figure; vessel (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary clay figure depicting a lion's face. The animal is shown in full  frontal view but only the face remains, the mane is mostly missing. The object was most likely applied to another object, probably a vessel.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Round clay plaque depicting a human face in full frontal view. The face is very broad with bulging eyes, flared nostrils and short rounded ears. The fact that the figure is flat on the reverse side suggests that it was applied to another object, probably a vessel.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: arrow (敦煌)
desc: Fragment of an arrow shaft. The object was made of reed, both ends are frayed.

title: figure (Xinjiang)
desc: Fragmentary figure of a griffin, here depicted as an animal with the head of a  bird of prey, a horn and a horse's neck. Details such as the mane or the  plumage of the wings were rendered using incised dashes and lines. The head  of the animal once formed an extension of  the handle of a vessel.

title: seal-case (敦煌)
desc: Rectangular seal-case made of wood. The centre has been sawn out in a square.

title: coin (Khotan)
desc: Coin. Sino-Kharoshthi coin. (Awaiting full details).

title: Republic banknote (Issued in: Xinjiang (autonomous region))
desc: Banknote.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Jiaqing coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary clay figurine of a monkey. Only the upper body remains.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Figure of Buddha: feet missing. The head has been reattached, and looks out of scale with the figure. Made of pigmented stucco.

title: bead; 珠 (和田)
desc: Round glass bead, made in the millefiori technique. The object is broken in half.

title: plaque (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary relief plaque showing the Buddha. He is shown seated against a vesica bordered by  lotus petals. His legs are crossed, the eyes closed and the hands ,which are covered by the folds of his garment, are held in meditation position (dhyāna mudrā). The fragmentary head is surrounded by a halo and has strongly elongated earlobes. The figure was made of yellowish clay using a mould and was covered with a white slip before being fired.

title: comb (Khotan)
desc: Wooden comb beater with a long, narrow handle. This tool was used during weaving to pack the weft together tightly; the teeth of the weaving comb would fit between the warp threads allowing the weaver to exert pressure on the accumulating weft.

title: Qing dynasty coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: arch (Khadalik)
desc: Wooden object. According to Stein (1921a, 195) this is the upper part of an arch, with a pointed centre and three foliations on each side. The front is decorated with elaborate carvings depicting acanthus leaves and rows of beads. On one side a small wedge-shaped tenon projects.

title: coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Coin associated with Yakub Beg. Awaiting full details.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: plaque (Khotan)
desc: Clay plaque of unknown use. The object is decorated with a scene depicting the façade of a building. One arched niche and the remains of an additional one are visible, in between them is a decoration consisting of a rosette. Inside the niche, the head and upper body of a person wearing an elaborate headdress can be seen. He holds a garland with both hands. Above the niches a fillet consisting of alternating beaded and plain bands can be seen.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Copper coin.

title: coin (Khotan)
desc: Coin. Sino-Kharoshthi coin. (Awaiting full details).

title: vessel; figure (Khotan)
desc: Wall sherd of a clay vessel. The sherd of the wheel-thrown vessel was decorated with an appliqué clay plaque in the shape of a face. The smiling human face is shown with a broad nose, rounded ears adorned with earrings and large round eyes. The hair is short, the forehead marked by a central dot.

title: ornament; 裝飾物 (和田)
desc: Crescent-shaped ornament cast in bronze.

title: Tang dynasty; 唐代 armour; 盔甲 (China)
desc: Fragment of armour consisting of six rectangular scales. They were made of leather reinforced by several layers of red and black leather. The scales were then joined together with leather strips.

title: Tang dynasty; 唐代 armour; 盔甲 (米蘭)
desc: Fragment of armour consisting of four rectangular scales. The scales were made of carved leather and covered with several layers of black and red lacquer. The decoration consists of red semi-circles. Some are still held together by leather strips.

title: artefact (敦煌)
desc: Object made of a wooden stick around which a coarse string is wound. The usage is unclear.

title: Qing dynasty coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Republic banknote (Issued in: Xinjiang (autonomous region))
desc: Banknote.

title: figure (Khotan)
desc: Clay figurine showing a kneeling monkey holding a drum on his thighs. The animal was is depicted in great detail, using incised lines to depict fur, fingers etc.

title: plaque (Khotan)
desc: Round clay medallion. The medallion was part of a bigger statue, probably a decorative ornament of the halo. It has a flower in the centre which is surrounded by a ring of leaves or flames. These are encircled by a double band which also forms the edge of the medallion. 		The reverse shows imprints of reeds/organic materials which were use in  wall-facing.

title: figure (Khotan (town))
desc: Head of a bird. Made of terracotta.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin (Xinjiang)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Song dynasty vessel (敦煌)
desc: Sherd of a ceramic vessel with creamy white crackled glaze.

title: arrow (敦煌)
desc: Fragment of an arrow shaft. The object was made of reed, both ends are frayed.

title: coin (Khotan)
desc: Coin. Sino-Kharoshthi coin. (Awaiting full details).

title: plaque (Khotan)
desc: Fragmentary relief plaque showing the Buddha. He is shown seated against a vesica bordered by  lotus petals. His legs are crossed, the eyes closed and the hands ,which are covered by the folds of his garment, are held in meditation position (dhyāna mudrā). The fragmentary head is surrounded by a halo and has strongly elongated earlobes. The figure was made of yellowish clay using a mould and was covered with a white slip before being fired.

title: handle (Khotan)
desc: Figuratively decorated handle of a clay vessel. The handle was moulded in the shape of a lion with the head of the animal forming an extension of the handle. The mane is emphasised by incised lines. The object is fragmentary and only the upper part of the body of the animal remains.

title: ear cup (敦煌)
desc: Fragment of an ear cup made a of lacquered wood. Part of the wall and one of the originally two handles of the elliptical vessel remain. The inside is painted using red lacquer, the outside seems to have had a red background with broad black lines. The remainder of a pattern consisting of red concentric circles can still be seen on these, the circles seem to have been applied using a stamp or a stencil. The object is very worn and some of the lacquered surfaces has flaked off or disintegrated.

title: Ming dynasty; Hongzhi dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain dish. Decorated inside with incised dragon painted in green enamel directly on biscuit. On outside, two incised dragons, one in yellow and the other in aubergine enamel, against colourless glazed background with incised waves. There is a mark on the base.

title: Yuan dynasty vase (Jingdezhen)
desc: Vase with moulded decoration modelled after a bronze vessel. Made of qingbai glazed porcelain. The side handles in the form of phoenix are pierced possibly for ring handles.

title: Qing dynasty incense-burner (China)
desc: Bronze incense-burner. Dragon-shaped handles.

title: Guangxu banknote (Issued in: China)
desc: Banknote.; Two dragons and flaming ball/pearl; waves below. Printed in brown, green and grey.; Five bats. Printed in grey and brown.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi bottle (China)
desc: Bottle, flask-shaped, flattened sides, slender neck; two scroll handles. Hard opaque ware with soft-looking glaze of yellowish buff, crazed in parts. Ornament moulded in low relief, dragon rising from waves into clouds surrounded by border of key pattern. Late copy of old Ding Yao.

title: Song dynasty mould (Tangyangyu)
desc: Mould. Head and part of a dragon (facing left). Made of earthenware.

title: Eastern Zhou dynasty tile (China)
desc: Tile. Made of moulded earthenware. Half moon shaped decorated with a taotie face.

title: Six Dynasties mask (China)
desc: Mask. Taotie mask. Made of openwork bronze.

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli box; cover (Jingdezhen)
desc: Round porcelain box and cover with underglaze blue, touches of overglaze green and yellow on a red enamel ground. This round box stands on a thick tapering foot ring and has a domed cover with a flat circular top. Where the tray and cover meet, both are thickened with a raised horizontal band. The box is decorated with a blurred underglaze blue design of four boys playing in a garden, with two pairs of dragons fighting over a flaming pearl around the sides of the cover and two dragons each chasi

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque (dragon-shaped). Perforated. Made of incised and carved jade (green.brown).

title: Han dynasty rubbing (China)
desc: Rubbing of Han Dynasty mythical animal with cloved feet and single horn depicted within striated border. Ink on paper.

title: Qing dynasty bowl (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain bowl. Inside, a roundel with stylised phoenix in flight in underglaze blue against yellow ground. Same design outside, repeated four times. There is a mark on the base.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory figure of the Buddhist guardian, Weituo (Sanskrit name Skanda), wearing elaborate helmet and armour and holding his vajra staff with a coiled dragon. Wooden stand.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi water-pot (Jingdezhen)
desc: Small porcelain water-pot of globular form. Underglaze red with large scale five-clawed dragon chasing flaming pearl through dense red clouds. Dragon's eyes appear to be painted in underglaze blue.

title: Shang dynasty pendant (China)
desc: Dragon pendant of very translucent yellow jade with speckles, inclusions and calcification polished to a fine gloss.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Seated bearded figure of a civil god, wearing circular qilin rank badge on front and back. Made of bronze.

title: Tang dynasty; Five Dynasties; 唐代; 五代 painting; 繪畫 (第17窟)
culture: Tang dynasty; Five Dynasties; 唐代; 五代
period: Tang dynasty; Five Dynasties; 唐代; 五代
desc: Very fragmentary lower section of a Pure Land painting with terraces and mythical birds (kalavinkas). The fragment on the right has a figure with a halo. Ink and colour on silk.

title: Han dynasty rubbing (China)
desc: Rubbing of a Han Dynasty relief with mythical animal within border. Ink on paper.

title: Ming dynasty; Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory figure of a standing luohan holding a qilin. Wooden stand.

title: Qing dynasty teapot; lid (Dehua)
desc: Tea-pot and lid. Phoenixes, plum sprays and scrolls. Lid with peony blossom. Made of moulded and Blanc de Chine porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty seal (China)
desc: Seal with tortoise body and dragon head and carved with a four-character inscription in relief in seal script on base. Made of jade.

title: Ming dynasty wall-painting (Xinxiang)
desc: Wallpainting (fragment). Flying phoenix. Painted in ink, colour on plaster.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Two porcelain cups with almost rectangular mouth rim created by four neatly-cut flutes. Covered in transparent yellow glaze applied directly to the biscuit. Incised dragon on each side outside and flaming pearl inside on the bottom. Base also glazed yellow, with inscription.

title: Song dynasty block; mould (Tangyangyu)
desc: Block (mould-making). Head of a dragon. Made of earthenware.

title: Song dynasty mould (China)
desc: Mould. Head of mythical beast. Made of earthenware.

title: Qing dynasty lid (China)
desc: Carved ivory box lid with a mythical bird with long tail feathers standing among flowers, rocks and lingzhi fungus.

title: Ming dynasty figure (China)
desc: Figure of a mythological lion with curly mane, pierced jewel on the back and long trappings to hide supporting pillar. Made of pottery, glazed blue and turquoise, but glaze damaged on the plinth.

title: Qing dynasty jue (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain vessel of ancient bronze jue form, with three thin legs, long spout, and two short columns rise from the rim in line with a simple loop handle surmounted by an animal head. The vessel has pale yellow enamel glaze.  There is a taotie mask design incised on the exterior.

title: Western Zhou dynasty cheek-piece (China)
desc: Bronze cheek-piece (harness.dragon-shaped.one of a pair).

title: plaque (Tibet)
desc: Plaque ( with monster's head). Made of bronze.

title: Qing dynasty amulet (China)
desc: Amulet. Monster. Made of carved jade (brown).

title: Ming dynasty box (Yunnan)
desc: Circular box with phoenixes and a large peony spray on the cover, peonies below, all with a coarse diaper grouond. Made of carved buff-brown, green and red lacquer.

title: Yuan dynasty; Ming dynasty figure (China)
desc: Figure (mythical bird creature). Made of pierced and engraved jade (green.brown).

title: Qing dynasty box (China)
desc: Carved ivory box decorated with the design of two dragons chasing the pearl on the sides. The base bears a spurious four character Qianlong mark.

title: Ming dynasty altar-vase (China)
desc: Two altar-vases with mythical beast heads on the shoulder handles. The body and neck is decorated with bands of geometric and wave patterns. Made of bronze. The vases have no solid base.

title: Qing dynasty dish (Dehua)
desc: Dish. Dragons chasing pearl. Made of Blanc de Chine and incised ceramic, porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty plaque; figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory plaque showing a pile of books supported by auspicious clouds from a qilin.

title: Shang dynasty mask (China)
desc: Taotie mask appliqué of translucent yellowish green jade with dark brown area with earth encrustation and traces of cinnabar.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu coin
desc: Silver coin.; Inscription.; Dragon.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong bottle (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain bottle with pear-shaped body and two dragon handles. Fine white glassy porcelain delicately painted in mixed enamels in 'Gu Yue' style with rock, roses and other flowers. Inscription on neck. Pair with PDFA817.

title: Shang dynasty pendant (China)
desc: Dragon pendant of pale green translucent jade with light brown veining and inclusions with some earth encrustation. The curled bottlehorn dragon has a prominent head with fierce eyes, open jaws displaying jagged teeth and a crenellated spine.

title: Qing dynasty jar; cover (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain honey pot with a round body, flanged shoulders and cylindrical neck, and cylindrical cover with a flat top.  There are two five-clawed dragons chasing flaming pearls in underglaze copper red against a background of waves in underglaze blue. There is a mark on the neck.

title: Qing dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Belt-hook (dragon head-shaped) A much smaller dragon on the belt hook body.. Made of wood.

title: Ming dynasty; Hongwu banknote (Issued in: China)
desc: Banknote: 1 guan (1 string of 1000 cash).; Six-character inscription giving name of banknote across the top. Below this a border of dragons. At the centre is an illustration of 1000 coins (strung as 10 groups of 100 coins). The two-character inscription above this gives the denomination; there is also a four-character inscription (in a folded style of writing) at either side. The longer inscription below gives further details and warns against counterfeiting.

title: Qing dynasty belt-buckle (China)
desc: Buckle (belt.part of). Dragons. Made of silvered and repousse bronze.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain cup.  The cup has a white body.  There are phoenixes in 'anhua' slip on the interior, flowers around the cavetto, and a flower in the well. There is a mark on the base.

title: Qing dynasty hanging scroll; painting (Beijing (city))
desc: Hanging scroll. Celebrating the Lantern Festival, with blue dragon, fish and other lanterns. Figures in buildings and in procession on road. Ink and colour on silk. Signed.

title: Qi Lin 麒麟 (Object)
desc: Woodblock print in ink and colour on paper. Popular print of a female deity, two children and a qilin, with inscription Qilin.

title: Qing dynasty; Yongzheng; Ming dynasty dish (China)
desc: Dish. Dragons and butterflies. Famille rose, ruby back, enamelled with slipware; incised ceramic, porcelain. Anhua decoration of dragons.

title: Daoguang censer (China)
desc: Censer in the shape of an ancient gui with elephant handles and three animal-head legs. Decorated with leiwen and birds. Made of bronze. Inscribed.

title: Qing dynasty wine-cup (Dehua)
desc: Cup (wine). Rhinoceros horn shape. With dragon among clouds, tigers, qilin, crane, a beribboned pearl & lingzhi. Ring base. Made of applied and Blanc de Chine porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Belt-hook (dragon-headed). A much smaller dragon on the belt hook's body. Made of stained  ivory (?).

title: Ming dynasty; Jiajing cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain cup. There is an incised design of two dragons among clouds on the exterior.

title: Ming dynasty; Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Jade plaque. Dragon and 9 characters.

title: Han dynasty mould (China)
desc: Mould. Dragons and tigers. Made of carved earthenware (grey).

title: tablet; religious/ritual equipment (China)
desc: Daoist ritual tablet (lingpai) engraved with wind god on one side, figure with dragon's tail and ritualistic characters on the other. Made of wood.

title: Qing dynasty knife (China)
desc: Knife. Made of jade (green), silver handle. The handle is in the shape of a dragon swallowing the blade.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain dish. Covered in pale yellow glaze with incised design of dragons pursuing pearls. Thin, roughly-applied high-fired glaze on base, with inscription.

title: Qing dynasty fan (China)
desc: Carved ivory brisé fan with a design of two phoenixes among peonies and lucky emblems on an open background, painted in colours and gilded.

title: Eastern Zhou dynasty thumb-ring (Shanghai)
desc: Archer's thumb-ring, decorated with a spiraling pattern, like dragon scale. Made of red-pigmented and incised jade.

title: Warring States period figure (China)
desc: Dragon silhouette of opaque brownish white jade with calcification and soft polish. The stylized, elongated curvilinear dragon with its head turning back towards its tail is decorated with raised spiral curls and highlighted by striated volutes extending from the body. A vertical perforation runs through the body.

title: Qing dynasty joss-stick-holder (China)
desc: Joss-stick holder; tortoise and dragon-shaped. Made of blue glazed and jade inset porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong box (China)
desc: Box (peach-shaped). Peaches. On lid, Spring character, Shoulao with dragon and cloud scrolls. Made of carved lacquer (red).

title: Qing dynasty vase (China)
desc: Pair of vases. Made of grey glazed, crackled and gilded porcelain. Monster handles. Monochrome.

title: Yuan dynasty; Ming dynasty altar-vase (China)
desc: Bronze altar-vase. Two dragon-headed handles, foot damaged.

title: Qing dynasty table-screen (China)
desc: Carved ivory table screen with a young scholar holding a fly whisk in a garden on one side and highly coloured phoenix with peonies and pine on the other side.

title: Qing dynasty bottle (Dehua)
desc: Bottle. Dragon. Made of applied brown crackled porcelain.

title: Han dynasty seal (China)
desc: Seal with knob in the shape of bixie (辟邪) and carved with four-character inscription in intaglio in seal script on base. Made of gilded bronze.

title: Qing dynasty; Guangxu snuff-bottle; spoon; stopper (China)
desc: Snuff-bottle with spoon (ivory?) and stopper (meatl collar with red glass insert?). Dragon and flower panels. Made of moulded and pierced and famille rose enamelled ceramic, porcelain.

title: Ming dynasty; Longqing cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain wine-cup with rounded sides and slightly everted rim. There are dragons in overglaze red enamel on the exterior, with double lines in underglaze blue at the rim and the foot. There is a mark in underglaze blue on the base.

title: Qin dynasty rubbing (China)
desc: Rubbing. Rubbing of a bronze mirror with stylised dragons on spiral ground within a 12-pointed star. Rubbed in ink on paper.

title: Han dynasty model building/structure (China)
desc: Kennel. Two dragons. Made of glazed (iridised) ceramic, earthenware.

title: vessel (China)
desc: Vessel. Ding-shaped with taotie decoration + inscription Gang Fu Ji. Made of bronze.

title: Han dynasty seal (China)
desc: Seal with knob in the shape of a bixie (辟邪) and carved with four character inscription in intaglio in seal script on base. Made of gilded bronze.

title: Qing dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Belt-hook (dragon-headed). A much smaller dragon on the belt hook's body. Made of jade (white).

title: Northern Dynasties rubbing (China)
desc: Rubbing. Rubbing of a bronze mirror with four characters around knob with eight confronted dragons within 16-pointed star. Rubbed in ink on paper.

title: Tang dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque. Phoenixes. Made of carved mother-of-pearl.

title: Qing dynasty snuff-bottle; stopper (Jingdezhen)
desc: Snuff-bottle with stopper. Dragon among waves. Made of blue, red underglaze porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Figure of mythical animal with headless creature or object on his back. Made of carved soapstone.

title: incense-burner (China)
desc: Bronze incense-burner and stand. Dragons with inscription Yu Jang Zhen wan.

title: Ming dynasty dish (China)
desc: Underglaze blue painted dish with qilin and bracket-lobed rim

title: finger-ring (Southeast Asia)
desc: Gold finger ring: the shoulders ornamented with chased serpent or dragon's heads; the rectangular bezel with seal characters much worn. There is a residue of red seal paste on the bezel.

title: Qing dynasty vase; model (Jingdezhen)
desc: Vase with two handles in the shape of monsters, with pendant rings. Dragon and phoenixes. Made of blue underglaze porcelain.

title: Han dynasty rubbing (Maoling)
desc: Rubbing. Dragon with four legs, twisting its head and looking back. Found on brick. Ink on paper.

title: Western Han dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Dragon plaque of very translucent pale green jade with areas of calcification polished to a very high gloss.

title: Qing dynasty; Republic figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory figure of Iron-Crutch Li (Li Tieguai), one of the Eight Daoist Immortals, leaning on his iron crutch and wearing worn and tattered clothing with a lingzhi fungus on the shoulder. A mythical animal is seated under his right foot.

title: Eastern Zhou dynasty tube (miscellaneous) (Xinzheng)
desc: Tube. Dragons. Made of carved jade (yellow-brown).

title: seal; 密封、印 (約特干)
desc: Rectangular bronze seal with a small loop-shaped handle. The base of the seal is decorated with an intaglio design showing a horned dragon.

title: Ming dynasty; Jiajing vase (Jingdezhen)
desc: Double gourd shaped porcelain vase with low foot rim formed from a recessed base.  There are four repeated dragon medallions against a honeycomb diaper background in underglaze colbalt blue on the exterior. There is an 'F' mark on the base.

title: Qing dynasty dish (China)
desc: Dish. Dragons. Made of green, red enamelled ceramic, porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Figure (dragon head). Made of stone (pink). Broken in various places.

title: Western Han dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Dragon plaque of very translucent pale green jade with areas of calcification polished to a very high gloss.

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain dish with underglaze blue, overglaze yellow, red and black enamels. This dish has rounded sides, an everted rim and a tapering foot. It is decorated with five dragons, two outside, two in the cavetto and one shown full face in the centre. Before the first firing, these were delineated in underglaze blue and reserved in white on an underglaze blue ground. Then the dragons were painted yellow, outlined in black and red. Details such as their scales and eyes were added in red and the dish

title: Six Dynasties; Han dynasty fitting (China)
desc: Gilded bronze fitting (dragon-shaped).

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi bowl (China)
desc: Bowl. Inscription, with phoenix medallions, clouds and flowers. Made of blue underglaze and enamelled ceramic, porcelain. Famille verte.

title: Shang dynasty; Anyang he (China)
desc: Bronze vessel of the type called he. It has four cylindrical legs above a lobed body of four sections. Each lobe is decorated with a taotie's head bearing ram's horns in relief. The horns are neatly flattened against the vessel's sides. The same taotie appear on the lid. The band around the neck has a decoration of dragon-like creatures in low relief. A ram's head sits on top of the handle.

title: Qing dynasty; Yongzheng jar (China)
desc: Jar. Made of porcelain, decorated in underglaze blue and overglaze enamel colours (doucai). Dragon. Mark.

title: incense-holder; snuff-bottle (China)
desc: Snuff-bottle with a dragon. Made of carved soapstone (black).

title: Eastern Zhou dynasty; Qin dynasty rubbing (China)
desc: Rubbing. Flying phoenix, from a tile. Ink on paper.

title: Ming dynasty; Zhengde dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Shallow porcelain dish. Underglaze blue with two five-clawed dragons among floral scrolls on the exterior and on inside cavetto. Five-clawed dragon among floral scrolls in a roundel on the interior.  Cloud motif band on foot. There is an inscription on the base.

title: Ming dynasty vase (China)
desc: Bronze archaistic vase. Decorated with interlaced dragons.

title: Eastern Zhou dynasty; Han dynasty ring (China)
desc: Ring. Taotie face. Made of bronze.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory figure of a luohan with heavy beard holding books in his hands and his left foot rests on a qilin.

title: Qing dynasty; Qianlong dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain dish with gently curved sides and slightly eveted rim. There are two dragons chasing a flaming pearl in green overglaze enamel on a yellow enamel ground on the exterior, and an undecorated interior. There is a mark in underglaze blue on the base.

title: Qing dynasty mask (China)
desc: Mask (dragon). Made of openwork bronze.

title: Qing dynasty; Republic figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory figure of a Daoist immortal on qilin with one hand holding a child seated in front of him and holding a flying whisk in the other. There is an integral ivory stand.

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque (Imperial). Dragons, bats and inscription. Made of lacquered and gilded wood.

title: Qing dynasty belt-buckle (China)
desc: Buckle (belt.part of). Dragons. Made of silvered and repousse bronze.

title: Qing dynasty altar-vase (China)
desc: Vase (altar). Zun-shaped with hooked flanges, dragons and spirals. Made of carved bronze.

title: The Xing Hou gui (Object)
desc: Bronze vessel of the type called gui. This vessel is known as the Xing Hou gui based on its inscription. It has a rounded body constricted at the neck; deeply curving sides are complemented by four handles. An intaglio cicada is located opposite each handle. Each of the four compartments of the vessel's surfaces is filled with a fabulous creature that seems to have been based upon an elephant; it has a long trunk and two roundels above its head, suggesting the bumps on an elephant's forehead. Ot

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque, imperial, with detachable dragon's head and steps. Made of lacquered and gilded wood.

title: Ming dynasty; Jiajing cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain cup. The cup has blue-tinged glaze. There is an incised design of two dragons among clouds on the exterior.

title: Ming dynasty water-pot (Found/Acquired: China (South-East)
desc: Pot (water). Dragon. Made of glazed and carved ceramic, porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque ( with taotie head). Taotie and scrolls. Made of pierced jade (grey).

title: Ming dynasty vase (Zhangzhou)
desc: Zhangzhou export ware porcelain vase of form inspired by ancient bronzes, with two large square handles. Painted in Swatow style with red, green, turquoise and black overglaze enamels. Band of chevrons in red at rim and flowering branches at neck, in red and turquoise. Two large ogival panels enclosing mythical animal qilins on body, with lotus sprays in between. Auspicious emblems on foot.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Figure. Kui Xing. Made of gilded and lacquered bronze.

title: Yuan dynasty; Ming dynasty altar-vase (China)
desc: Vase (altar). Li-ding-shaped with taotie patterns and waves. Made of bronze.

title: Qing dynasty vase (China)
desc: Vase (hexagonal); with two handles; panels with lions and phoenixes; on the foot and lower part of the neck a band of a bronze pattern, with character at intervals; arabesques, beadwork, etc. Mark. Made of olive-green tea-dust glaze and gilded porcelain.

title: Ming dynasty; Qing dynasty; Kangxi print (Suzhou)
desc: Woodblock print. Deity. Zhong Kui assaulting a demon with an axe. Printed in colour on paper.

title: Qing dynasty box (Jingdezhen)
desc: Circular porcelain box with domed cover. The box has yellow glaze on the exterior, plain white felspathic glaze on the interior. There is a dragon among clouds incised on the cover and waves on the lower section.

title: Han dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque engraved with a dragon. Oblong. Made of white glass, with a blue glass bead inlaid in the center. Remains of gold leaf in quatrefoil.

title: immortal
desc: Carved ivory table screen, with two standing figures from Shuihuzhuan (Water Margin). Gongsun Sheng is depicted holding a cup in one hand and a magic sword in the other while Li Kui is holding an axe. On the other side is an inscription taken from a poem by Cao Tang (act. 9thC) about Queen Mother of the West visiting Emperor Wu of Han. Both images and inscription were rubbed with black ink.

title: Long San Shen Wei (Object)
desc: Woodblock print. Deity. Dragon-headed figure in civil costume with 2 others. Ink and colour on paper.

title: Zhou dynasty; Han dynasty pendant (China)
desc: Pendant. Dragon's head both ends, spirals and striations, perforated. Made of engraved jade (white).

title: Qing dynasty mask (China)
desc: Mask (dragon). Made of openwork bronze.

title: Qing dynasty seal (China)
desc: Square seal with the knob in the form of a coiled dragon and carved with four-character inscription in relief in seal script on base. Made of iron.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi vase (China)
desc: Vase, decorated with dragons. Made of enamelled famille noire porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty cup (Dehua)
desc: Dehua porcelain cup in the shape of a carved rhinoceros horn cup. The cup has a white body and clear glaze.  There is a qilin looking up at a bat, moon, and pine branches in relief under the rim. The base is unglazed.

title: Ming dynasty incense-burner (Longquan)
desc: Porcelain incense burner in the form of a well with moulded, openwork and incised biscuit decoration and green glaze. This incense burner is in the form of a covered well. It has a four-sided hollow base with legs at each corner which are slightly splayed and take the form of auspicious 'ruyi' clouds. Each opposing side of the base is decorated with a shallow relief-moulded design of two peonies or a horse. Above this is a short four-sided balustrade with T-shaped openwork arranged at different

title: Ming dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Plaque (belt). Dragon,birds and flowers. Made of gilded and engraved jade, bronze.

title: Qing dynasty seal (China)
desc: Carved ivory seal with a figure of a Buddhist luohan and a qilin on his shoulder.

title: incense-burner (China)
desc: Incense-burner. Gui-shaped with stylized taotie. Made of silver inlaid bronze.

title: Zhou dynasty figure; pendant (China)
desc: Figure (mythical bird). A very long open beak with a delicate curved end. The lower part of the beak is perforated. Made of jade (blue-green.corroded).

title: Qing dynasty jue (China)
desc: Pair of jue. Bronze with roughly cast decoration of taotie.

title: Qing dynasty dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain dish.  The saucer has a white body and blue-toned glaze.  There are dragons in anhua slip on the interior. There is a mark on the base. Pair with A485.

title: Shang dynasty; Anyang figure (China)
desc: Figure (taotie mask). Made of jade.

title: Qing dynasty scabbard slide; sword (China)
desc: Scabbard slide. 2 dragons interlaced tails,on stud is a bat. Made of carved jade (ivory).

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli stem cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain stem cup with underglaze blue decoration. This stem cup has a rounded bowl, a solid spreading stem with stepped edge and a recessed base. Modelled after a Xuande period imperial porcelain cup, it is decorated with nine 'hai shou' [sea animals] reserved in white on a ground of swirling blue waves. Two rabbits are reserved in white on the stem and the exterior of the bowl shows a winged dragon with a fish tail, an unidentified flying creature, a flying lion, a fish, a 'qilin', a rabbit,

title: Qing dynasty snuff-bottle; stopper (China)
desc: Snuff-bottle with spoon-stopper. Dragon and pearl. Lion on spoon-stopper (ivory). Made of incised and white glazed porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi saucer; cup (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain cup and saucer.  The cup has rounded sides and slightly everted rim and two shallow dragon-shaped loop handles.  Saucer has short flared sides and flanged central recess to hold the cup.  The cup and saucer have pale yellow enamel over a plain felspathic glaze.  The cup has dragons pursuing pearls incised on the exterior.  The saucer has an incised dragons among clouds, with waves at the centre and a narrow wave band at the rim, with similar motifs underneath. There is a mark in underg

title: Ming dynasty tile (China)
desc: Tile (part of). Dragon. Made of yellow glazed earthenware.

title: Qing dynasty box (Jingdezhen)
desc: Small circular porcelain box. Underglaze blue with scholar leaning against a pine tree in a garden landscape with pagoda above a band of five-clawed dragons chasing a flaming pearl. Turbulent waves on lower sides. There is an inscription on the base.

title: Shang dynasty ring (China)
desc: Dragon annulus of green jade with earth encrustation.

title: Guangxu banknote (Issued in: China)
desc: Banknote. Printed in blue, orange, red and brown.; Dragon motif.

title: Qing dynasty cup (China)
desc: Stemmed cup decorated with phoenix and dragon. Made of porcelain with incised transparent glaze. Mark.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi dish (China)
desc: Pair of dishes decorated with inscription and qilin. Made of famille verte enamelled porcelain. Mark.

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli bowl (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain bowl with rounded sides. The bowl has yellow glaze, with blacklines at the rim and the base. There are two five-clawed dragons among clouds incised on exterior with key-fret around the foot-ring, and a blue line at the rim and a ring at the bottom under a plain felspathic glaze. There is a mark in underglaze blue on the base.

title: Tang dynasty spout (China)
desc: Dragon head spout of white jade with some mottling finished to a soft polish. Open jaws reveal fangs and teeth; the curled horn is perforated for a spout or liquids to be poured from its open mouth. A plain extension from the base has two perforations for attachment.

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli bottle (Jingdezhen)
desc: Hexagonal porcelain bottle with underglaze blue decoration. This bottle is pear-shaped with six sides and a hexagonal foot and was probably modelled on a metal-work prototype. Each face is painted in a vivid cobalt blue with a bracket-lobed cartouche enclosing a fabulous beast. These are, clockwise: a horse, lion, leopard, 'qilin' with spots, 'qilin' with scales, and another 'qilin' with spots. The remainder of the body is covered with an inverted-Y diaper pattern in white on a blue ground, with

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli base (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain figure, with detached stand with underglaze blue decoration. Shoulao, the stellar god of longevity. He perches on the back of a kneeling deer, rests one hand on the deer's head and holds a 'ruyi' sceptre or magic wand in the other. He is identified by his characteristic bald phallic cranium, elongated earlobes and long beard. He is dressed in robes of office and his shoulders are ornamented with roundels containing phoenix among clouds. Similar roundels with cranes decorate the knees a

title: teapot (Tibet)
desc: Copper tea-pot washed with tin to appear as silver. The handle is shaped like a dragon, and the spout resembles a makara. The metal filigree is cast and then soldered and riveted onto the main body.

title: Ming dynasty; Jiajing bowl (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain bowl with reserved white decoration on a blue ground. This shallow bowl has slightly flared sides, a raised 'mantou' centre and a recessed base. It belongs to a relatively rare group of Jiajing porcelains where the decoration is executed in reserved white on a blue ground. Inside it is decorated with a prancing five-clawed dragon and outside with two scaly dragons with waves and clouds separated by roundels enclosing the character 'shou' [longevity]. The base is also glazed blue and ca

title: Qing dynasty seal (China)
desc: Seal with tortoise body and dragon head and carved with a four-character inscription in relief in seal script on base. Made of jade.

title: Qing dynasty; Republic figure (China)
desc: Carved ivory figure of a man on a dragon.

title: Ming dynasty saucer; dish (China)
desc: Saucer, dish. Dragons,inscription. Made of copper-red underglaze and blue glazed porcelain.

title: Han dynasty mould (China)
desc: Mould. Dragons and tigers. Made of carved earthenware (grey).

title: Qing dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Belt-hook ( with dragon head). Made of crystal.

title: Han dynasty mirror (China)
desc: Bronze mirror. Dragon and tiger.

title: Qing dynasty ornament; jewellery (China)
desc: Ornament (phoenix-shaped). Made of pearl, amethyst inset gold.

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli dish (China)
desc: Dish. Dragons. Made of enamel cloisonne metal.

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque. Double headed mythical animal. Perforated. Arc shaped.  Made of jade (brown.green).

title: Qing dynasty seal (China)
desc: Seal with knob in the shape of a qilin with head turning backward. Carved with five-character inscription in intaglio in seal script on base. Made of gilded bronze.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi saucer; dish (China)
desc: Saucer, dish. Inscription with dragon. Mark. Made of blue underglaze porcelain.

title: Ming dynasty; Zhengde jar (Jingdezhen)
desc: Vessel in the form of a zhadou or 'leys porcelain jar', with wide flaring neck. Underglaze blue with scrolling five-clawed dragons amid lotus flowers around the interior and upper part of the interior. There is an inscription on the base.

title: textile; painting (China)
desc: Kesi. Tightly woven picture. Dongwanggong wearing a robe decorated with "shou" (long life) characters; Xiwangmu arriving on a phoenix. Also Daoist emblems of long life, including gourds, peaches, crane. Made as a birthday gift.

title: Ming dynasty; Jiajing jar (China)
desc: Jar with lid, decorated with phoenix designs. Made of engraved and blue glazed porcelain.

title: Ming dynasty; Yongle flask (Jingdezhen)
desc: Large porcelain flask of bian hu form. Underglaze blue with large three-clawed dragon among scrolling lotus. Similar scrolling lotus on neck and squared spirals at the rim.

title: Qing dynasty figure (China)
desc: Seated deity on a separate throne with hands clasped in front of chest and robes with painted decoration of dragons. Made of bronze with painted decoration. Original rail and one leg of throne missing.

title: Qing dynasty vase
desc: Porcelain vase, with long neck, two elephant-head handles and pendant rings. The vase has crackled white glaze. There are incised medallions of dragons on both sides.

title: Qing dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Belt-hook ( with dragon head). Made of jade (green).

title: print (China)
desc: Woodblock print. Long Wang Zhi Shen, with dragon. Wrapped in 1982A1217.068a-c. Ink on paper.

title: Han dynasty; Warring States period hu (China)
desc: Hu, wine vessel. Decorated with interlaced dragons. Made of bronze with traces of gold and silver inlay.

title: Eastern Zhou dynasty bell (China)
desc: Bronze bell. Animal masks, figures and dragon.

title: Ming dynasty; Qing dynasty vase; hu (China)
desc: Vase, hu. Taotie design. Made of bronze.

title: Qing dynasty belt-fitting (China)
desc: Belt-hook (dragon head-shaped) A much smaller dragon on the belt hook body.. Made of wood.

title: Qing dynasty seal (China)
desc: Seal with knob in the shape of a Qilin and carved with four-character inscription in relief on base. Made of bronze.

title: Qing dynasty jar; cover (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain honey pot with a round body, flanged shoulders and cylindrical neck, and cylindrical cover with a flat top.  There are two five-clawed dragons chasing flaming pearls in underglaze copper red against a background of waves in underglaze blue. There is a mark on the neck.

title: Qing dynasty; Yongzheng dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Porcelain dish. Covered front and back with pale yellow glaze. Impressed designs of five-clawed dragons among clouds in centre and flower sprays round the side. Four five-clawed dragons among clouds on the back. Bluish-white glaze on base, with inscription.

title: Ming dynasty figure (China)
desc: Mythical beast of opaque greyish green jade with inclusions and black surface skin areas polished to a good gloss.

title: Ming dynasty; Wanli ink-stone (Jingdezhen)
desc: Rectangular porcelain ink-slab raised on a splayed plinth, with open-work sides, partly open-work top, unglazed central panel for holding water, and deeply recessed panel for holding water. There are lotus panels on the plinth, dragons chasing pearls over waves on each side, two dragons on the top, full-faced dragon in the water-pot section, and two dragons on the base.  There is a mark in underglaze blue on the base.

title: Qing dynasty ewer (China)
desc: Ewers (pair) Dragon. Mark. Made of enamelled and gilded porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi bell (China)
desc: Bronze Chinese bell, with dragon, cloud and characters.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi bowl (Jingdezhen)
desc: Bowl. Phoenixes and birds in trees, with fish, dragons and three legged toad. Made of blue underglaze porcelain. Mark.

title: Han dynasty jar (China)
desc: Small Jar. Red earthenware with green lead glaze. Ovoid body, short neck and small mouth.  Round the shoulder a band of moulded decoration representing huntsmen, tigers, boars, a dragon and a bear across a hilly landscape.

title: Qing dynasty brush-pot (China)
desc: Carved ivory brush pot with two phoenixes surrounded by pine trees, peony and lingzhi fungus. Wooden base.

title: Song dynasty bowl (Jiangxi)
desc: Bowl. Foliate sides and incised with fabulous beast. Made of engraved and Qingbai glazed porcelain.

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque ( with taotie head). Taotie and scrolls. Made of pierced jade (grey).

title: Qing dynasty cong (China)
desc: Cong. Each side has a Taotie mask carved on. Very stylised. Made of jade.

title: Ming dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Rectangular open work white jade plaque of a stylised dragon cavorting amid lingzhi and foliage surrounded by a border of indented circles. On the plain reverse side at either end are two small perforations drilled from both sides.

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi figure (Jingdezhen)
desc: This mould-made porcelain figure of the smiling Tang poet, Li Bai, shows him in a characteristic pose, staring wistfully into an empty wine jar. He is dressed in a scholar’s hat, a robe decorated with clouds, and a crane rank badge. The wine jar is painted with a dragon holding a lingzhi fungus with a band of plum blossom around the shoulder and further flowers around the neck in green, aubergine, yellow and black enamels. This was used as a brush washer on a scholar’s desk.

title: Qing dynasty water-pot
desc: Porcelain water-pot.  The water-pot has a white body and cream white crackled glaze.  There is a cloud collar moulded around the rim, medallions of dragon head in waves around the sides, and a petal panel at the base.

title: Tang dynasty incense-burner; ding (China)
desc: Incense-burner, ding. Dragons in relief. Made of glazed pottery.

title: Ming dynasty; Jiajing box; lid (China)
desc: Large circular lidded box. Lid made of carved lacquer (colours) with dragon among clouds and Shou 壽 character. Flowering and fruiting branches on the sloping sides. Made of carved and gilded lacquer (colours and black), with inscription 大明嘉靖年製 (made in the reign of Jiajing of Great Ming) on base. h

title: Ming dynasty brush-pot (China)
desc: Carved ivory brush pot with the design of a flying phoenix and two birds among flowers and tress. The ivory base is a later addition.

title: Ming dynasty; Qing dynasty cup (China)
desc: Jade cup. Dragon and bird.

title: Qing dynasty; Yongzheng hand-warmer; cover (China)
desc: Hand-warmer, decorated with dragons. Made of openwork bronze. Inscription.

title: Qing dynasty plaque (China)
desc: Plaque ( with taotie head). Taotie and scrolls. Made of pierced jade (grey).

title: Qing dynasty; Kangxi dish (Jingdezhen)
desc: Dish. Inscription, with bird (magpie) on fruiting apricot branch. Made of famille verte enamelled porcelain. Golden dragons on a red  background on exterior. The porcelain is incised beneath the decoration.

title: Qing dynasty bowl (Jingdezhen)
desc: Bowl with underglaze copper-red dragons in roundels.

title: Yuan dynasty; Ming dynasty jar (China)
desc: Jar. Dragons and phoenix. Made of black painted and Cizhou, turquoise-blue glazed stoneware.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Naqada III; 1st Dynasty mace (Egypt)
desc: A pear shaped mace-head of red breccia: with a carefully polished surface. The vertical perforation bears the marks of rotary drilling, carried out in one direction from the bottom to the top.

title: Roman bowl (Egypt)
desc: Pottery: Egyptian Red Slip Ware bowl; light brown clay with abundant fine mica and fine-medium lime; occasional large grog; overall mid red-brown slip, burnished on upper surface, matt on underside; broad horizontal rim and deep body, rounded wall and broad ring foot; groove near outer edge of rim.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical amulet; bead (Phoenicia (historic))
desc: Glazed composition (faience) Egyptianising pendant in the form of a stylised heart. Mould-made. Suspension loop at top. Light turquoise glaze.

title: Christian manuscript (Qasr Ibrim)
desc: Two torn manuscript pages from a Greek text giving the Nubian or Egyptian version of an Acts of the Martyrs, written on both sides.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period amphora (Egypt)
desc: Small pottery table amphora.

title: ostracon (Monastery of St Phoibammon)
desc: Limestone ostracon; figure of Jonas standing with arms raised.

title: Coptic arch (Saqqara (Memphis))
desc: Limestone segment of an arch; foliate decoration on the underside. The face depicts a nereid on the back of a dolphin with her scarf billowing above her head.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: 26th Dynasty stela (Egypt)
desc: Sycomore fig wood stela of Qeres: this round-topped stela is divided into four sections separated by multi-coloured borders.  In the upper section is a winged sun-disk beneath which is a scarab beetle and a šn-sign. On either side of these an 'wedjat'-eye and a recumbent jackal are depicted. In the second is a 'kheker'-frieze. There is a scene in the third register below a sky-sign with stars. The deceased stands on the right with arms raised in worship before an altar on which rests a water-pot

title: cast; statue (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: "Meroë" Head of Augustus: curved fragment of plaster cast relating to the oversized 'Meroë' head of Augustus; fragment has few identifiable features other than vertical markings on the outer surface. Fragment is white in colour.

title: 19th Dynasty statue (Temple of Ptah)
desc: Left fist, grasping a roll, from a colossal red granite statue of Ramses II.

title: Ptolemaic ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: Plaster cast, white in colour, of sandstone relief-block from the rear chapel wall of pyramid N17 at Meroë, Sudan. Cast depicts the vulture-headdress and horned sun-disc of Isis; headdress is worn by a figure facing right, figure's raised left arm is also visible.

title: Third Intermediate scarab (Egypt)
desc: Sard scarab engraved on the base with a seated ibid-head figure, probably Thoth, and Egyptian hieroglyphic signs in the field; perforated for suspension; the figure faces left, above his head is a disc and crescent, and above this a winged solar disc; on either side are feather-like motifs, the one in front is possibly the ntr sign, the one behind is uncertain; below the figure in the exergue is a crescent-shaped motif; the border is defined by a groove.

title: token (Issued in: Egypt)
desc: Octagonal brass token, value of 5.; Sun face at centre. Repeated dots as frame.; Inscription at centre.

title: Mamluk dynasty basin (Italy)
desc: Basin with cover. Pseudo kufic. Made of engraved and silver inlaid brass.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic spacer-bead (Egypt)
desc: Glazed composition rectangular spacer-bead, with four lateral perforations. Square in section, with the holes pierced alternatively either side of central line(notional). Glaze strong, shiny pale green, good quality, in a thin layer, with slightly grainy, bumpy surface. Core-fine, off-white chalky texture.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from dark grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic situla; votive offering; model (Egypt)
desc: Cast model of situla in copper alloy; slender, bag-shaped with pronounced button-shaped pointed base; two opposite vertical suspension rings welded at mouth (one eyelet broken), handle missing; undecorated; corroded and slightly encrusted.

title: Roman bottle (Egypt)
desc: Dark blue blown glass bottle with opaque white trails; rounded, neck cylindrical with deep concavity in middle; globular body, with faint vertical corrugations; trails on neck only, all originally from one drawing.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Mamluk dynasty vessel (Egypt)
desc: Sherd (rim), fragment. Inscription. Made of red enamelled and gilded glass.

title: Ptolemaic; Roman Period amphora (Egypt)
desc: Small pottery table amphora.

title: Middle Kingdom scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green glazed (traces of) steatite scarab, feathered legs, pierced longitudinally, base inscribed with hieroglyphs within a broken spiral border.

title: Archaic Greek amulet (Egypt)
desc: Faience amulet: aegis of a deity wearing the crowns of Upper and Lower Egypt.

title: Roman Imperial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Brown steatite scarab; longitudinally pierced; the base is inscribed with the prenomen of Thutmose I above an incised representation of a papyrus boat.

title: 17th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Steatite scarab inscribed with the name of Kamose (?).

title: Late Period heart-scarab (Egypt)
desc: Green stone heart-scarab, striated elytra, unpierced, base decorated with thirteen lines of hieroglyphs.

title: Coptic; Early Byzantine figure; relief (Egypt)
desc: Figure; bone; carved in relief; nude woman walking to right; she wears a loose drape wrapped around her and carries a tympanum(?) in her right hand; another tympanum(?) between her feet.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: Coptic; Byzantine textile; garment; tunic; ribbon (Egypt)

The filling-hole is surrounded by an imprinted ridge on the discus and radiating ridges on the shoulder, extending down the nozzle. The top of the nozzle is flat with a ridge on either side that ends in volutes. Red-brown Nile silt fabric.

Two holes are pierced, not quite level, near the ears. A hole has been bored into the neck for a modern stand. Two-piece mould. Micaceous orange-brown N

He pushes down strongly on the handle and has sunk up to his ankles in the freshly turned moist soil.

title: Ptolemaic lamp (Egypt)

title: Roman Period sealing (Egypt)

Adjacent are small oval impressions from intaglio gems, one with a raised longitudinal spine, and parts of two others. Unfired pale yellow-brown mud.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from dark grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Fatimid dynasty filter; jug (Egypt)

title: Ptolemaic; Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery lamp with a flat bowl-like body, a wide circular base, a short splayed nozzle, and a side-handle of vertical ring-form, mostly missing, decorated on top with grooves and loops. The discus edge is decorated with a band of ovules between moulded rings. The centre of the concave discus is missing; four subsidiary filling-holes alternate with four raised pellets near the discus edge.

title: 26th Dynasty textile; mummy-padding (Egypt)
dateDisplay: 600 BC (circa - if from Dr. Granville's mummy)
desc: Textile; mummy padding; approximately oval pad of bunched and folded linen, undyed, but now buff to brown-black in colour (oily stains ?). Part of a triple self-band on underneath. The weave is plain [warp-faced simple tabby] and the threads are S-spliced and S-twisted; the stitch gauge is warp c.23 ends per 10mm and weft c.12 picks per 10mm.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains (Mediterranean)

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Limestone ostracon.

title: Greek coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper Alloy coin.; Laureate head of Zeus Ammon, right.; Two eagles standing left on thunderbolt; to left, cornucopia.

desc: Shell of a marine gastropod mollusc of the Bittium genus.

title: 18th Dynasty papyrus (Memphis (historic - Egypt))
desc: Book of the Dead with name-space left blank; coloured vignettes; frame 1.

title: Ptolemaic mummy-mask; cartonnage (Egypt)
desc: Painted and gilded cartonnage mummy-mask with a striped lappet-wig; representation of Khepri on top of the head; Hieroglyphic text around the fillet; on the back are three painted panels, the uppermost depicting Sekhmet, Isis, Horus and Osiris, the lower two depicting winged deities.

title: Middle Kingdom model group; figure (Egypt)
desc: Wooden model of a sailing-boat with crew: the depth and beam of the hull are normal, but with comparatively little sheer; painted red, stern slightly higher than bow. It is carved from a solid block of wood. Deck is hollowed out leaving low gunwales running from stern to the beginning of the raised fore-deck. After-deck is slightly raised and painted red; the gunwales bore black marks of which only a few are now clearly visible. The after section of the longitudinal strip is painted red with bla

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from dark grey hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Roman bottle; unguent-bottle (Egypt)
desc: Greenish blown glass bottle; rim outsplayed and folded up; cylindrical neck with constriction at base; body with flat S-shaped sides; concave base, no pontil-mark.

title: Ramesside papyrus (Deir el-Medina)
desc: Papyrus with satirical vignettes: a figured scene in which animals ape human activities, but in a topsy-turvy world, they act against their natural instincts. The lion does not attack the gazelle but plays a board game, probably 'senet', with her. The pair grasps the playing pieces with great difficulty; the lion also holds a dice made from an animal bone. Even when the lion wins, he claims his reward in the bedroom: although the end of the papyrus is damaged, it is surely the same gazelle who i

title: Late Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Cast figure of the funerary god Osiris in copper alloy; depicted as mummiform and standing; holding against his chest the sceptre Heka and the flail Nekhekh, hands side by side; wearing the Atef crown, composed of the white crown flanked by two ostrich feathers and with uraeus on front; feathers, beard, uraeus and insignia of power detailed with small diagonal and horizontal incisions; tang beneath feet; complete and slightly corroded.

title: Early Byzantine ampulla (Egypt)
desc: Ampulla; clay; flat, circular sides with designs in relief. On side (a), within a double border of studs and small dentils, is a head of Saint Menas to right. He has curly hair, and drapery round the neck. On side (b), within a border of studs, is a relief inscription. The mouth is largely lost. Orange clay with a buff surface; a few white inclusions.

title: 18th Dynasty scarab (Egypt)
desc: Steatite scarab, base inscribed with prenomen.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic votive box; figure (Egypt)
