title: First Intermediate; Middle Kingdom axe (Egypt)
desc: Rounded arsenical copper axe-head with four drilled binding-holes and two lugs. It is thickly corroded, and clearly preserved in the corrosion product is the impression of linen fabric. The line of the haft is also visible, and each of the perforations bears remains of the original binding-thong.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic figure-fitting (Egypt)
desc: Solid-cast bird foot and lower leg in copper alloy; all four claws complete with backwards facing one smaller; fitting element of a bird figure, most likely a falcon, often associated with Horus; tang at either end for attachment; complete fitting; corroded.

title: Roman Period ornament (Egypt)
desc: Mosaic glass slice: on a lavender-blue field is a standing 'ba'-bird with unusually large wings, one extended at shoulder height, the other drooping down. The colours are red, yellow,  blue, and  black. The 'ba'-bird has a white head with black hair. An exceptional detail is that the bird's mouth is wide open as if shrieking - a vivid illustration of the decline of an ancient iconography. The scale is small and the quality very fine. Set in a nineteenth-century gold mounting.

title: Early Byzantine textile; tunic (Egypt)
desc: Fragment of textile from a tunic; linen; decorated with two square medallions, each containing an oval medallion with a horseman; four smaller oval medallions containing figures of Eros and four vases; woven in blue on a buff ground.

title: Greek coin (Minted in: Kition)
desc: Silver coin.

title: Roman lamp (Egypt)
desc: Mould-made pottery plastic handle broken from the rear of a lamp, decorated with Isis enthroned, suckling Horus; the rear of the throne is indicated by impressed lines. At the rear is the upper spring of an applied loop handle, and a firing-slit. Covered with a red slip.

title: Third Intermediate textile; mummy-wrapping (Egypt)
desc: Textile fragment; torn strip of undyed linen cloth; medium quality, one end discoloured by resin(?). The weave is plain [warp-faced simple tabby] and the threads are S-spun and S-spliced; the stitch gauge is warp c.29 ends per 10mm and weft c.11 picks per 10mm. The sides are torn, one end is cut and the other is broken. Presumably from a mummy.

title: 26th Dynasty amulet-mould (Egypt)
desc: Large terracotta amulet-mould in Nile clay, to cast figure/amulet of Sekhmet or other lion-headed goddess; goddess crowned with sundisk and uraeus, in striding pose (on low square base?); in reddish brown Nile clay with mica inclusions and quartz and other inclusions; thick traces of white-beige paste inside impression.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Roman jug (Egypt)
desc: Greenish blown glass jug with an outsplayed lip, a thickened rim, a neck expanding downwards to a bulbous body, a thick solid drawn-out stem, and a splayed base with a moulded rim. The ribbed handle is splayed at the rim attachment and has a top wing. Decorated with a trail on the upper side of the base.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Cypro-Classical bead; amulet; pendant (Egypt)
desc: Heavily decayed glazed composition (faience) item, roughly spherical, a irregular-shaped bead or possibly the head of an Egyptianising amulet depicting an animal - lion or monkey? - since what looks like an ear can be sign on one side; rod- or -mould-made; the paste is white, but with a green-blue core, possibly the traces of a metal suspension wire; specks of blue glaze preserved in places.

title: Roman Imperial bottle (Egypt)
desc: Base and body of a (child’s) head flask, mould-blown, janiform (double-faced) Neck and rim not preserved. Glass is of natural blue-greenish colour with iridescence (bright-green and gold).

title: cast (Original from: Meroë (historic - Nubia))
desc: Plaster cast, white in colour, of sandstone relief-block from the rear chapel wall of pyramid N17 at Meroë, Sudan. Cast depicts the vulture-headdress and horned sun-disc of Isis; headdress is worn by a figure facing right, figure's raised left arm is also visible.

title: Meroitic; Ottoman dynasty textile; bag (Qasr Ibrim)
desc: One corner of a goat hair cloth, probably once a bag. Plain selvedge at one side; looped fringe along bottom. Warp-faced weave, colour of hair, the warp changing in stripes from selvedge in.

title: Cypro-Archaic; Phoenician; Eastern Mediterranean scarab (Egypt)
desc: Blue glazed composition (faience) scarab; mould-made and glazed; decorated with a figure standing on a lion passant with tail in the air; two leaf-like objects in the field above the animal and in front of its head; the field is enclosed by a border line; perforated for suspension.

title: Ptolemaic figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Limestone ithyphallic figure of naked youth with side-lock, probably  Harpocrates (Horus). Seated with knees raised on a rectangular plaque, and  phallus extending forward on which a tambour or drum rests, clasped in both hands. The figure's skin is painted red. Crudely carved from soft Egyptian limestone. Complete.

title: Roman Period ostracon (Karnak)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Ancient Egypt; Roman animal remains
desc: Shell of a marine gastropod mollusc of the Murex genus.

title: Late Period; Ptolemaic weight (Egypt)
desc: Dome-shaped weight carved from black hard stone, likely basalt; carinated dome type with well-defined edge; of truncated conical shape, with flat base and convex top; smoothed surface; complete.

title: Ptolemaic bowl (Lower Egypt)
desc: Rim fragment of thick walled vessel probably a shallow bowl. Dark blue turquoise faience with crudely drawn black stripe. At base inside appear two black marks like the radiating petals of a crude rosette. Though inside is rougher and somewhat worn it is still glazed. probably same vessel as Eg. Inv. 3577.8

title: Late Period figure (Lower Egypt)
desc: Limestone ithyphallic figure of naked youth with side-lock, probably Harpocrates (Horus). Seated upright, looking to the side, and holding his colossal phallus with both hands to his side. The figure is on an oval plinth. The figure's skin is painted pink. Soft limestone.

title: Late Period pendant; amulet (Egypt)
desc: Amuletic pendant carved from calcite, heart-shaped (less likely representing a vase); smoothed surface; suspension loop pierced widthways above; complete.

title: Late Period; Graeco-Roman ostracon (Egypt)
desc: Pottery ostracon.

title: Roman Imperial beaker (Egypt)
desc: Miniature Pottery: coarse ware beaker; mid-brown clay with similar finish; frequent fine mica; plain rim, tall cylindrical neck, short rounded body, tapering to flat base; groove at base of neck; two holes pierced just below rim for suspension.

title: Roman Period figure (Egypt)
desc: Small white terracotta human head with eyes, nose, mouth, ears, and hair indicated. There are visible dark pores throughout the head. The face is flat. The head has short curly hair. The head is broken at the chin and affixed to a black painted wooden base with a dark gray adhesive filler. The base is trapezoidal, with 1334" in red ink on the bottom. Handmade.

title: Meroitic; Roman textile; cushion-cover; wall-hanging; tapestry (Mediterranean)
desc: Fragment of tapestry, probably from a cushion-cover or wall-hanging, simple repeating design of four-petalled rosettes with round dots between. Ground of undyed linen, dots of purple wool with yellow wool centres. Rosettes in purple, red or turquoise wool.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Alloy coin.

title: First Intermediate; Middle Kingdom amulet; bead; pendant (Egypt)
medium: glazed composition; cornelian; amethyst; feldspar; jasper; steatite; ivory
desc: A long string of fifty amulets amulets: of various materials, five beads and four pendants. The beads are all of glazed composition: the conical bead, mis-shapen barrel and oblate bead are all green, and two short barrels are blue. Three pendants are drop-shaped and made of cornelian, blue-green glazed composition and amethyst. The fourth is a flattened wide drop of amethyst.  Of the three baboon amulets two are of amethyst and one of cornelian. There are two green feldspar and three amethyst ha

title: Roman Provincial coin (Minted in: Alexandria (Egypt))
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: The Mausoleum at Halicarnassus (Series)

title: tile (Deruta)

title: East Greek; Archaic Greek chalice; heavy chalice (Chios)
desc: Body sherd of Chian Mature Animal Style pottery chalice or heavy chalice; white slip; interior: glazed streaky black; above, part of lotus flower in added white outline with added red centres; below, two white bands; exterior: painted decoration consisting of part of bull’s head, to right; rosette as filling ornament.

title: Roman coin (Rome)
desc: Copper alloy coin.

title: Etruscan votive figure (Etruria)
desc: Bronze votive statue of a woman depicted striding forward and looking ahead with hands raised.  Large almond-shaped eyes, elegantly arched brows, and full lips; hair in stylised waves which frame the face and fall in a finely incised and crinkled mass down her back. Around her neck are two substantial necklaces, one beaded and the other plain. Her movement presses the material of her chiton, or tunic, against her body, revealing the form of her legs.  The chiton is decorated with dotted lines fo

title: Greek button (East Greece)
desc: Diamond-shaped pottery 'button' with two holes, flat shape, one side smooth, the other rough; edges bevelled on both sides, made from a recut pottery sherd; light orange-brown micaceous clay.

title: Hellenistic; Parthian ornament; ear-ring (Iran)
desc: Gold ornament, perhaps part of an ear-ring, with some enamelling on surface.

title: Roman dish (Caubiac)
desc: Silver serving dish decorated with Bacchic scenes. Graffiti on the base give the weight of the silver before and after working (7.5 and 6.5 pounds). Other graffiti name the owner as Felicis.

title: Attic lamp (Athens)
desc: Pottery lamp with a globular wheel-made body, a narrow rim with a deep groove, a flat-topped tapering nozzle and a horizontal band handle. Decorated with a black glaze.

desc: Brown glass paste intaglio with a white stripe: Philoctetes fanning his foot.

Shown are two male figures, one in the upper portion and one in the lower portion, riding a horse and a camel respectively. The figure at the top wears a crown, while the one below wears a hat. They both have a halo around their head, wear a loose tunic and high boots, and hold a shallow bowl in their right hand. On the top left, there is a black raven or crow. title: handscroll; painting (Painted in: China)

The painting shows Śākyamuni at the opening of the scroll, on the right, seated amidst Buddhist and Daoist deities viewing numerous demons trying to raise the bowl with a hoist and tackle. Other spirits and gods, including the Mother of Lightning and the Duke of Thunder, lend their support to Hārītī, who stands at the centre. In the ‘Samyuktaratna-piṭaka’ the Mother of Demons 'exhausted

title: Ming dynasty figure (Dehua)

His hands are clasped together and feet splayed apart. His eyes are bulging and he frowns. He is dressed in military costume with boots, armour and a loose robe over the top, around which he wears a wide girdle secured by a belt at the front. He also wears a compact hat with ribbons tied together at the top of his head. Unusually, his sleeves are also tie

desc: This badge shows a side-view of a bird wearing a crown. Surface details are articulated using low relief lines. The bird's feet are missing.

title: Late Medieval floor-tile (Meaux)
desc: Earthenware floor tile, lead-glazed; mosaic.

title: Late Medieval floor-tile (Chertsey)
desc: Earthenware floor tile, lead-glazed.

title: Late Medieval floor-tile (Malvern)
desc: Earthenware floor tile, lead-glazed.

title: Late Medieval floor-tile (Cheshire (historic))
desc: Earthenware floor tile, lead-glazed; lion; plus one loose fragment.

title: coin (Minted in: London (England))
desc: Silver coin.

title: Early Byzantine bracelet (Qaw el-Kebir)
desc: Bracelet; bronze; with overlapping terminals.

title: Western British Iron Age coin (Pershore)
desc: Silver coin.; Stylised head, right.; Triple-tailed horse, left.

title: medal (London)
desc: Struck silver medal.

title: glass rod; cane (Italy)
desc: Cane or rod; glass; short section with image at each cut end of the three heroes of Italian Unification: Giuseppe Garibaldi, Victor Emmanuel II and Camillo Benso, Count of Cavour).

title: Hispano-Visigothic coin (Minted in: Narbonne)
desc: Gold coin.; Cruciform sceptre between facing busts of Egica and Witiza.; Monogram with name of the mint Narbonne.

title: medal (France)
desc: Silver medal.; Bust of Diane de Poitiers, left.; Juno, with her peacock, waters with her milk a lily (France) which arises  from a vase between the legs of a recumbent woman (?Peace) holding a  cornucopia. The sun dissipates clouds above. Palm and laurel branches in the  exergue.

title: Late Medieval seal; seal-impression (France)
desc: Seal-matrix: Guillaume Loat; bronze; circular; short hexagonal handle ending in loop; animal walking; star; legend; with wax impression.

title: Classic Maya jar (Pusilha)
desc: Large fragment of a pottery jar reformed from 18 sherds to approximately half of side and bottom of vessel. Painted with twist and bud motiffs, a rosette motiff, and 'u' forms in red and yellowish red, organised in bands betweeen black lines.

title: Chuquibamba textile
desc: Textile fragment; cotton warp and camelid weft; tapestry; checkerboard layout of motifs; eight-pointed stars alternate with bird motifs; all colour-patterned; remnants of cotton stitching along two sides; tan, dark brown, red and cream.

title: figurine (Mexico City)
desc: Figurine (one of four); made out of sugar; in the form of skull; decorated with white piped icing for facial features; blue stripes over red foil diamond on top; green foil on forehead; green foil in eye sockets. Used for Day of the Dead Festival.

title: ornament (Lambayeque (department))
desc: Artefact in form of pierced star, ornament (?) made of copper.

title: Chancay; Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; camelid fibre; slit tapestry with cross knit loop stitch along one edge; bird motifs and geometric elements outlined in black and red; bird motifs enclosed within red field, part of a larger figure, mostly missing.  Tan, red, black, white and green.

title: Chancay tapestry (Peru)
desc: Textile; long fringed border(?); slit tapestry; cotton warps and camelid wefts; double serpent motifs with serrated outlining; small bird and diamond motifs in spaces between serpents; red fringe sewn along one edge: plain weave; cotton warps with camelid wefts extended and looped to make fringe; yellow, tan, red.

title: figure (Mexico City)
desc: Figure; made of papier mâché over cane framework; in the form of Biafran skeleton; skeleton painted brown with silver markings, white eyes and teeth; wears white papier mâché cloth over left shoulder; skeleton seated with knees bent, right arm raised. Used at Day of the Dead Festival.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked 'North 17'.

title: figurine; pendant (Mexico)
desc: Figurine, pendant (?) carved of quartz (aventurine); in the form of human head, two holes pierced in back for suspension.

title: Moche vase (Trujillo)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle with globular chamber and painted in dark red slip with motifs consisting of  two versions of the Strombus monster with big rounded eyes, long tongue, a mouth with big fangs and a long tail.

title: Classic Maya figurine (Guatemala)
desc: Animal's head figurine made of pottery.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The object is marked 'Left Side 182'

title: bag (Peru)
desc: Bag; textile; camelid fibre; warp-faced plain weave; decorative stripes: central one of square motifs, flanking ones are dashed lines - complementary weave(?); sewn up at one side - other is open.   Red, indigo and white.

title: Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; strip; two weft selvedges; cotton warps and camelid wefts (except for cream cotton); slit tapestry; design of two standing frontal anthropomorphic figures with hair or headdress(?) in form of four upraised antennae-like appendages; eyes and teeth outlined in different colours; tan, buff, red, white, black.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile border fragment; camelid fibre wefts; slit tapestry with woven fringe; interlocking  birds in diagonal rows outlined in red; tan, red, indigo.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle; straight spout, round-profiled stirrup and square chamber.  Seated cross-legged male figure surmounts chamber; fringed loincloth; headcloth/cap tied under chin with tassels at back and mesh pattern on top.  Monkey lug at spout base.  Incised markings on loincloth and hands.  Two sets of vertical moulds used - one for stirrup and the other for the chamber and figure - seams run down sides.  Arms of figure modelled and applied.  Well-burnished.  Orange.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; camelid fibre; complementary weave(?); two bands with geometric fish motifs(?) outlined in black flank on either side, a central panel with interlocked zigzag motifs outlined in red; red, tan, black/dark brown, white.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked 'North 102 W'.

title: Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Moche vase (Trujillo)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle with painted decoration, depicting two warriors taking the form of anthropomorphised foxes wearing helmets tied under the chin and carrying spears and helmets. They are depicted agains a desert background.

title: Late Intermediate textile (South Coast Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton warps and camelid wefts; interlocked tapestry; design-field divided into four interlocked stepped frets, two featuring diagonal rows of serrated serpents(?) and two with rows of flying bird motifs; along one edge fabric  has been rolled and sewn down to form a border.  Brown, buff, dark brown, red, indigo and green.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile; two pieces sewn together; cotton; lace gauze in zigzag pattern; white.

title: Nasca textile (Peru)
desc: Textile motif; brown plain weave  ground cloth covered with  cross-knit loop stitch enbroidery; depicts a human face wearing feather headdress with two ear spools and a chin pendant; light brown, dark blue, red, white.

title: Chuquibamba textile
desc: Textile fragment; portion of tunic; camelid warp and weft; plain weave stripes alternate with bands of complementary warp  weave; 3/1 interchange; diverted warps; broader bands feature eight-pointed star motifs on divided rectangular backgrounds; narrower stripes feature geometric elements - bars and frets.  Red, tan, indigo and cream.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from shallow, out-flared pottery bowl or plate (possibly a tripod) with basal ridge. Very eroded overall, inner light brown ground with red and black bands; outer red ground with black bands above ridge.

title: Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton plain weave with paired warps and wefts; painted design; square outlined segment with feline motif(?); row of volutes at one edge; cream and brown.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Complete textile; two rectangular textiles sewn together to form a tube; cotton warps and camelid wefts (grey/brown stripes also cotton wefts); plain stripes alternating with decorative stripes of slit tapestry featuring volute motifs; some supplementary outlining of motifs in stem stitch?; browns, red, grey.

title: Mapa de Santa Barbara Tamasolco (Object); Pintura de propriedades de descendientes de los Señores Tlaxcala (Object)
desc: Codex; Dominated by representations of two churches, that of Santa Barbara (centre) and Santa Ana (right), the map drawn on bark paper, is a plan of lands, roadways and a water-course in the state of tlaxcala. Also shown are vegetation, including a large tree (centre), and four human figures, three grouped beneath the tree and the fourth in the lower left-hand section of the map. A number of place signs are drawn in native style (such as the frog, tamazolin in Nahuatl, representing Tamasolco). A

title: Chimu-Inca textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; long strip; cotton Z-plied warps and camelid wefts (but white is cotton); two weft selvedges; tapestry with longer slits closed at regular intervals by interlocking wefts; eccentric wefts outline the diagonals of the design;  column of feline figures, boldly outlined in contrasting colours; accompanied by several small bird and fish motifs.  Red, tan, brown, white, pink.

title: Chancay; Rimac loom (Peru)
desc: Wooden loom with partially woven textile; cotton; heading cord arrangement is shown; double balanced weave; patterning of felines and stepped frets with scrolls; tan and pale brown.

title: Late Classic Maya; Postclassic Maya figurine; bell; pendant (Palenque)
desc: Gold pendant (bell?) with a miniature portrait representing a Maya ruler.

title: tunic (Peru)
desc: Tunic; two textiles, folded over and sewn together with laced stitching(?); camelid warp and weft; slit tapestry with dovetailing; geometric design of nested stepped diamonds within rhomboidal fields; tan, brown, red, white.

title: Ica tapestry (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton warp, camelid fibre weft (except white cotton); slit tapestry; diagonal rows of geometric elements; thick dark brown/black outlining; red, dark brown/black, cream, indigo, green.

title: box (Gallup)
desc: Silver and turquoise box.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Body sherd from shallow, pottery bowl or plate, part of basal flange remains. Inner has yellow/red ground with red band and black lines; outer is unslipped.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle; rounded-square profiled stirrup, straight spout and cylindrical chamber.  Surmounting chamber is seated bird (of prey?) with prominent chest.  Vertical mould seams down sides including stirrup spout.  Polished.  Black.

title: Mogollon bowl (Casas Grandes)
desc: Pottery bowl in the form of a female figure; painted facial features and geometric designs in brown/black and orange on a cream ground.

title: charm; pendant (Peru)
desc: Blue broken stone, pendant (charm) in the shape of a bun.

title: cord (Peru)
desc: Braided cord; wool; black, tan and red.

title: Nasca textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; band, disintegrating at either end; camelid wefts(?), 2-ply warps; slit tapestry; 6 geometric motifs in segmented layout; red, dark brown, tan, blue, green.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked 'East 74'. Exposed metal rod embedded in the plaster at the back of the mould to form a loop.

title: mace (Peru)
desc: Cast copper or copper alloy six pointed mace head.

title: tunic (Peru)
desc: Ceremonial shirt comprised of two textiles sewn together and folded with neck slit left open; sides are unsewn; ground cloth is cotton  plain weave with single warps and paired wefts; feathers are sewn on in overlapping rows by quills; design of large yellow oval on orange background with blue border around neck opening.

title: vessel; sample (Mexico)
desc: Thirty seven bone and stone fragments, samples, one of jadeite, and sherds of pottery.

title: poison sample (Ucayali, River)
desc: Poison made of urari, curare.

title: Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton plain weave with paired warps and wefts; painted design; three outlined square segments, one with cross motif and one (partial) with bird motif with serrated tail(?); row of volutes between squares and border of stepped fret-and-scroll motifs at one side; cream and brown.

title: Nasca; Nasca-Huari textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; three pieces joined together by lacing stitch; small fragments of additional textiles sewn at both weft selvedges; cotton fine plain weave ground with camelid tapestry strips featuring stepped fet motifs with angular hooks; regular vertically aligned colour patterning; green, red, tan, blue, white.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Jar with wide neck, tapering slightly towards rim; portrays a pair of copulating figures, both face downwards; limbs and features in high relief.  Uncertain construction- possibly pair of hemispherical moulds used for bodies joining around equator of vessel (strong ridge on interior); heads made separately each in a two-part mould - grooves visible running along underside of each, inside.  Rough worn surface; wipe marks on exterior and interior of rim; burnishing faceting elsewhere including bas

title: Chimu-Inca vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Jar; flaring spout, tapering base and handle at rear.  Vessel depicts figure in double pointed headdress with patterned headband - concentric diamonds; tasseled earspools and hands placed on chest.  Vertical mould seams down sides visible inside chamber through hole in back; spout and handle added.  Rough finish - no burnishing.  Black.

title: Moche vase (Trujillo)
desc: Vase (in form of seated figure) made of pottery. The solemn bearing of this individual and the plain cape knotted at the front suggest that he is a priest. The square plaque may represent a pectoral of shell mosaic set in wood. Attached to his headdress is a pair of crouching pumas or monkeys (the rear legs are visible towards the back of the headdress). His facial adornment may have been painted or tattooed and he wears a string of small shells across his chin.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked '141 E'. Broken off from North 141b.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile; camelid fibre; main portion looped(?) with geometric patterning; borders along two edges: narrow strip of slit tapestry with interlocked stepped frets edged with plain weave stripes; remnants of piece of cane wrapped in yarn attached to one side; red; yellow, dark brown. Separate piece of L-shaped wood wrapped in yarn and with tassel at one end(?); unattached to main piece.

title: Chimu vase; vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Face-neck jar; flat-rimmed flaring spout and flat base.  Neck depicts personage in cylindrical headgear with humped back; pair of maize cobs in the round at either side on back.  Mould seams not detectable - well-smoothed - likely run down sides, including spout but the two cobs would be added - smoothing evident around bases.  Well-burnished but worn.  Dark grey with reddish patching.

title: Aztec figure (Mexico)
desc: Sandstone seated figure of Mictlantecuhtli, the Aztec god of death. The figure bears three glyphs on its back: 'Two Skull', 'Five Vulture' and 'Four House'.

title: Chimu-Inca vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle with round-profiled stirrup and flat-rimmed spout.  Chamber features incised diamond band; bird lug at spout base; two lugs at stirrup base are broken off.  Vertical mould seams down sides, well-smoothed; stirrup has broken off and been re-attached - so perhaps hand-built and applied, rather than incorporated in mould.  Polished.  Black.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from shallow, out-flared pottery bowl or plate (possibly a tripod) with basal ridge. Inner,eroded, reddish yellow ground, light red bands and darker lines; outer, reddish yellow.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton plain warp-faced weave ground cloth with camelid supplementary weft patterning; rows of anthropomorphic standing frontal figures with feet and hands rendered as feathers(?); birfurcating headgear; row of figures at one end is upside down relative to the others; at opposite end is border of interlocked birds with long tails.  Cream cotton with red and dark brown camelid fibre.

title: Chancay doll (Peru)
desc: Doll; wooden(?) framework; wrapped with cotton textiles; various plain weaves - mainly weft-faced(?); discontinuous weft in one  layer; outer layer of camelid fibre (white is cotton though) clothing; form of looping(?) - non-woven.  Brown and white cotton with red wool.

title: Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Copan; Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Chimu bowl (North Coast Peru)
desc: Shallow bowl with inward-tapering walls and rounded base.  No imagery.  Polished exterior including base.  Black; grey/brown on base.

title: Post-Classic spindle-whorl (Mexico)
desc: Three pottery spindle whorls

title: Colonial (Americas) vessel (Peru)
desc: Jar; made of pottery; double, vertically-stacked chambers and pedestal base.  Rounded bowl surmounts lower chamber depicting a deer head; antlers positioned against back of bowl.  Vertical mould seams down front and back of deer head; eyes are incised.  Black.

title: dance-mask (Mexico)
desc: Dance-mask; carved from wood; in the form of human face; holes carved for pupils; skin painted pink, eyes white, hair, moustache and beard black (lines incised to represent hair), eyelashes black, cheeks dark pink, lips dark pink, teeth white; holes carved at top centre and on sides, through which is threaded string for hanging mask.

title: Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Classic Maya stela (Pusilha)
desc: Stela (P & Y in the series excavated from Pusilha in 1928) made of limestone, reconstructed from one large upper fragment and a series of smaller fragments on the lower section. On one surface a life sized human figure is represented - much weathered, on the other surface a series of glyph blocks can be seen, divided into eight columns. Both surfaces are carved in low relief.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton warp and camelid fibre weft; slit tapestry with eccentric weft outlining in places; row of bird motifs with crescent headdresses/crests, flanked above and below by rows of interlocked volutes; tapestry fringe of tabs, each featuring bird motifs and below which each tab bifurcates into fringe. Red (? - faded brown), tan, indigo and dark brown.

title: Chimu vessel; whistle (North Coast Peru)
desc: Double-chambered whistling bottle; spout slightly flaring at base and flat bridge.  Front chamber surmounted by head of raptorial bird; outer surface of both chambers features vertical bands of pendant triangles; inner surfaces are stippled.  Mould seams not detectable but likely that single set used for both chambers, including spout; bridge added incorporating modelled whistle behind bird  head.  Smooth areas well-burnished.  Black.

title: Chancay head-band (Peru)
desc: Long headband(?); textile; patterned tapestry sewn onto plain gauze backing (folded into several layers to create 'padding' effect); tapered at either end; tapestry: slit; cotton warps and camelid wefts (but white is cotton); geometric design of forked elements in chevron-like arrangement; tan, red, white, dark brown.

title: Paracas textile (Peru)
desc: Textile, fringed border fragment, featuring flying mythical figures with tongue protruberances and staff, alternating in position.  Plain weave cotton ground covered in camelid stem-stitch embroidery (dark brown with red, green, gold, blue, white); cross-knit loop stitched border featuring serpents.

title: Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Codex Vienna (facsimile) (Object); Codex Vindobonensis (facsimile) (Object); Codex Vindobonensis Mexicanus 1 (facsimile) (Object)
desc: Codex; tracings of Codex Vienna comprising of 66 leaves; also known as Codex Vindobonensis and Codex Vindobonensis Mexicanus 1, a Mexican pictorial manuscript; ritual-calendrical also contains mythological genealogies.

title: Paracas textile (Peru)
desc: Textile; camelid fibre plain weave(?) ground cloth with rectangular fields of stem stitch embroidery in chess-board arrangement; each design rectangle features a supernatural figure, elaborately garbed and grasping a staff in either hand, terminating in serpent heads at each end; facial mask/appendages in form of serpent whiskers; each figure is colour patterned; on one half of the cloth red rectangular backgrounds to figures have disintegrated.  Black, red, browns, indigos, white.

title: Chimu; Colonial (Americas) whistling vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Double-chambered bottle; tall spout and flat rectangular bridge.  Small bird figure surmounts bridge; incised semi-circular patterning around spout base.  Vertical mould seams down sides of chambers, so two mould-sets used; bridge and bird added incorporating whistle.  Well-burnished; black.

title: Paracas textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; ground cloth(?) covered in camelid fibre stem stitch embroidery; interlocked serpentine motifs with serrated bodies and Oculate Being heads; feline motifs in intervening spaces with smaller feline figure in body cavity.  Remnants of cross knit loop stitch along one edge.  Red, indigo, green and tan.

title: serape (Mexico)
desc: Sarape; cotton warp and wool weft, woven on back-strap loom. Central roundel mainly brown with blue, maroon and white patterning. Main field is white with blue and maroon lozenge pattern. Border of main field is predominately brown with yellow, red and white flowers and shapes. Each short edge has striped strip in blue, brown, red, white and yellow.

title: necklace (Mexico)
desc: Woman's necklace (one of six); made of plastic; string of red plastic beads with turquoise ribbon attached.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from shallow, pottery bowl or plate (possibly a tripod) with basal ridge. Inner eroded, light brown and red with a glyph(?) just visible in black; no slip remains on the outer surface.

title: Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; cotton plain weave with paired warps and wefts; painted design; two outlined rectangular segments, one with a crescent headdress-wearing frontal figure, the other with a bird with head appendage; cream and brown.

title: Nasca textile (Peru)
desc: Textile motif; pale brown plain weave ground cloth covered with cross-knit loop stitch embroidery; depicts bird with outstretched wings, geometric patterning on wing tips, long tail feathers; gold/brown, black/dark brown, red, white.

title: Chancay bag (Peru)
desc: Bag; one textile folded over and sewn along two sides with cotton whip stitch; slit tapestry; camelid wefts; diagonal rows of interlocked bird heads(?) and serrated lines; one side seam has cotton edging; tan, brown, dark brown.

title: axe (Guadalajara)
desc: Axe-head made of stone.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked '40' and 'Front'.

title: Chimu jar; vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Black/greyware jar with flaring spout; vertical mould seams down two faces; depicts face of a feline with face in low relief ('cut-out') relative to rest of bottle.  Wipe marks in places and some faceting, but much of surface looks as though worn off.

title: vessel (Peru)
desc: Vessel made of pottery.

title: figurine (Mexico)
desc: Figurine (Indian, charcoal-carrier) made of wax.

title: Nasca; Huari bag (Peru)
desc: Coca bag (chuspa) made from camelid fibers. It features a braided strap and three wrapped tassels at the bottom. The main body depicts a  star-shaped motif and the main colours used are tan, dark brown, blue, red, white.

title: token (Issued in: Wincanton)
desc: Alloy token.; A lion rampant.; Initials.

title: figurine; model group; figure (Metepec)
desc: Figurine (from nativity scene [nacimiento in Spanish]) (part of model group); made of pottery; in the form of Wise Man; cylindrical robe, hands raised to shoulder level, disk in right hand; skin painted brown, top half of robe fuchsia with gold accents, sash (from left shoulder to right hip) yellow with gold accents, rop belt fuchsia with gold accents, bottom half of robe purple with gold accents, cape yellow with gold, green and orange accents, disk orange with black facial features and gold ac

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from pottery plate or shallow out-flared bowl (possibly a tripod), with medial ridge on the outer surface. Polychrome decorated - Orange/re and buff inner with black lines running circumferentially.

title: Chimu vase; vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle (missing spout); four chambers and round-profiled stirrup. Vessel depicts four fruits (lucuma?); monkey lug. Very well-finished so mould seams not detectable - likely that two two-part vertical mould-sets used for chambers and separate one for stirrup. Very high polish. Black with metallic  sheen.

title: Classic Maya jar (Pusilha)
desc: Large fragment of a pottery jar reformed from 18 sherds to approximately half of side and bottom of vessel. Painted with twist and bud motiffs, a rosette motiff, and 'u' forms in red and yellowish red, organised in bands betweeen black lines.

title: Inca pin (Peru)
desc: Cloak pin in the form of a spoon.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle; rounded stirrup profile; slight medial pronouncement around equator; flat base.  Small monkey lug at base of spout; chamber plain.  Vertical mould seams but well smoothed - most evident along upper surface.  Stirrup spout looks added - slight raise and indentation around stirrup base.  Finely polished - faceting mostly removed; base burnished but worn.  Black with pale grey down one side and on stirrup.

title: Classic Maya eccentric flint (Xunantunich)
desc: Eccentric flint of brown chert.

title: Paracas textile (Peru)
desc: Textile, long strip, camelid fibre, plain weave ground covered in stem-stitch embroidery with cross-knit loop stitching at one end and along the bottom edge, dark brown with red, gold, pale blue, brown; features flying birds, alternating in colour schemes.

title: Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked 'West 81'.

title: Early Quimbaya helmet (Colombia)
desc: Hemispherical helmet made of tumbaga by hammering and embossing. The circumference is slightly oval; two opposite pairs of holes at the edge might have been used for a chin strap. Most of its surface is decorated with an embossed geometric design composed of lines and dots.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile; cotton; yarns are twisted in pairs to give mesh of open squares; cream.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Bottle with spout-and-handle; flat strap handle, flat base and straight spout.  Two design panels on each side in low relief relative to rest of body; each features a frontal figure in crescent headdress with upraised arms; three of the figures feature a staff to their right; the fourth figure features a staff to the left side. Fine stippling fills panel backgrounds.  Perforated lug at spout base - but not pierced through.  Vertical mould seams down sides of spout and body; also seam along under

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Double-spout-and-bridge whistling bottle with narrow tapering spout, chamber with pronounced equator and ring base.  Surmounting chamber is seated female in double-pointed headdress and necklace, bearing a child on mat, on her lap.  Horizontal mould set for chamber; figure made in vertical two-part mould with limbs modelled and applied; child in one-part mould(?); whistle modelled.  Upper surface is polished.  Black.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Quirigua)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked 'North 120 W'.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Spout-and-handle bottle; straight spout and strap handle.  Portrays canine with concentric markings on face; limbs in low relief on vessel sides; dispersed stippling on chamber.  Vertical mould seams down dog chest and along spine, including spout and handle.  Rough surface but non-stippled areas originally burnished.  Dark brown; orange-fired patching.

title: Lambayeque textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; two strips sewn together with whipping stitch, plus remnants of a third piece at one side; cotton warps and camelid wefts; tapestry; checkerboard arrangement of bird figures, outlined in black stitching on alternately tan and dark brown backgrounds; rows of motifs alternately face right and left.  Eye, feather and claw details also outlined in stitching; browns, black and yellow.

title: model; altar (Mexico)
desc: Model of circular "sacrificial stone", altar (?; with relief ornament), made of wax.

title: The Yaxchilan Lintels (Series)
desc: Stone panel of hieroglyphic inscriptions; Yaxchilán Lintel 25 dedication text.

title: Chancay tapestry (Peru)
desc: Textile; corner fragment; slit tapestry; cotton warps and camelid wefts; design laid out in diagonal bands; central stripe with interlocked long-tailed bird/serpent(?) motifs with serrated edges bordered on either side by narrow stripes; row of three small bird motifs and in each 'step' of the fret shape is a feline motif; motifs are all outlined in black (using a wrapped warp); border along one edge of complementary weft(?) (extension of main warp and then folded and sewn, using wrapping stitch

title: Classic Maya bead (Palenque)
desc: Fragment, in two pieces, of a highly polished, large bead, perforated and made of basalt.

title: Late Classic Maya; Postclassic Maya figurine; bell; pendant (Palenque)
desc: Gold pendant (bell?) with a miniature portrait representing a Maya ruler.

title: Chancay textile (Central Coast Peru)
desc: Textile; two strips sewn together; cotton ground cloth; warp-faced plain weave; camelid supplementary weft 'reserve-line patterning' in which supplementary wefts float mainly across front of fabric, with the places where they pass under a warp arranged to form outline of design - a grid of diamond-shaped motifs; patterning occurs in colour blocks alternating down the length of each strip. Cream cotton with tan, mustard, red and pink camelid fibre.

title: lanyard (Mexico)
desc: Lanyard?; made of fibre; strip of red and white twisted fibre folded in half, with leather handle? at fold; ends joined together with white fibre circle accented with red zigzag lines and small multicoloured balls hanging on fibre; ends separate into two tassels, decorated at top with tube of fibre with brown, red and white zigzag lines; each tassel then separated into two tassels decorated with fibre circles and balls hanging on fibre.

title: Huari; Tiahuanaco textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; one weft selvedge; partial wrapped warp selvedge; fine ground weave with supplementary weft, imitating tapestry; features row of standing mythical figures with snake appendages on head and three fingers on each hand. Red, blue, green, tan and white.

title: Moche vase (Trujillo)
desc: Stirrup spout bottle with globular chamber  and painted motifs consisting of  two versions of the Strombus monster with big rounded eyes, a mouth with big fangs and a long tail.

title: Chancay head-band (Peru)
desc: Long headband(?); textile; patterned tapestry sewn onto plain gauze backing (folded into several layers to create 'padding' effect); tapered at either end; tapestry: slit; cotton warps and camelid wefts (but white is cotton); geometric design of forked elements in chevron-like arrangement; tan, red, white, dark brown.

title: Classic Maya mould (Lubaantun)
desc: Pottery mould for jaguar head figurines.

title: Humboldt Fragment 7 (facsimile) (Object); Mizquiahuala Sales Receipts (facsimile) (Object)
desc: Codex; tracings of  Humboldt Fragment 7, also known as Mizquiahuala Sales Receipts, a Mexican pictorial manuscript comprising of one sheet. Drawings of zacate, fish and other goods sold to Manuel de Olvera. The manuscript represent a receipt for the services made to Manuel de Olvera, corregidor of Mizquiahuala.

title: pin (Lambayeque (department))
desc: Pin made of copper.

title: Aztec sample (Cholula)
desc: 13 fragments, samples, of stucco and adobe,one with a surface painted red.

title: Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from shallow, pottery bowl or plate (possibly a tripod) with  basal ridge. Light yellow/red ground with red bands and black lines, some disign element visible in black; outer light brown and red.

title: Maya vessel (Belize)
desc: Brown ware pottery vessel, sherd, with incised decoration

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Double-chambered bottle with tall tapering spout, flat arched bridge and flat base.  One chamber depicts spondylus shell; other represents a monkey bearing a bird (toucan or muscovy duck?) upon its back.  Complex construction - vertical mould seams down sides of spondylus chamber, then two separate moulds used for monkey and bird; bridge added to incorporate whistle.  Smooth areas well-burnished but surface is worn.  Black/dark grey.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd, with basal flange, part of a pottery vessel, plate or dish (possibly a tripod). The inner surface is eroded, pale brown and black, black suface has some gloss; outer is yellowish red with a faded black band at the rim, decorative elements in faded black and red, and a yellowish red band on the flange.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from shallow, pottery bowl or plate (possibly a tripod). Inner surface reddish yellow and red.

title: Chimu-Inca; Inca vessel; aryballos (North Coast Peru)
desc: Aribola with flaring, flat-rimmed spout, pointed base and lug handles low down on chamber sides.  Miniature aribola is attached to front face of main vessel.  Vertical mould seams down sides of main vessel from spout to base, but are well-smoothed.  Seams on miniature vessel can be felt inside opening - two sets of vertical moulds used for whole vessel.  Well-burnished all over.  Black; some grey patches.

title: Chimu vessel (Peru)
desc: Small spout-and-handle bottle; straight spout and flat base.  Chamber portrays armoured crustacean; pair of front claws.  Vertical mould seams along spine of creature and down chest; include spout but handle added.  Well-burnished.  Black.

title: Early Classic Maya mask (Veracruz)
desc: Mask carved in green aventurine quartz, with four holes pierced through the edges.

title: Classic Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?). The edge is marked 'Left Side 15'.

title: bead (Trujillo)
desc: Bead; made of stone; square of green stone with four holes drilled on back for threading bead.

title: Chimu figurine; vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Front chamber of a double-chambered bottle; join marking upper bridge on reverse and much of lower portion of vessel missing around join of lower bridge; flat base.  Vessel portrays a seated figure in large crescent headdress, patterned headband, long tunic with patterned border and bearing a spear in both hands.  Burnished surface but wipe marks still visible on rear around upper bridge join; base underside wiped only.  Black but gray fabric shows through in places.

title: Paracas textile (Peru)
desc: Fringed textile fragment; camelid fibre ground cloth covered with embroidery; three main 'flying' figures, each with multiple serpentine appendages sprouting from heads, each grasping trophy head in one hand and with several others attached to face; body cavity features a double-headed bird motif; background space features small feline motifs and other anthropomorphic forms; along one edge is a border of miniature, simplified versions of the main figures; along opposite edge is a band of cross k

title: Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Loincloth(?); textile; cotton warp-faced plain weave with paired warps; metal fish motifs sewn on in rows; along bottom edge are sewn pendant sheet metal motifs folded into semi-circular shapes; along top of fabric is sewn a separate woven tie - also cotton warp-faced plain weave with paired warps.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; long strip; camelid fibre warp and weft; plain weave bordering on either side a decorative band of complementary weave(?); segments each containing a bird motif; red, dark brown, pale brown, pink, cream.

title: Jalisco figurine (Mexico)
desc: Seated male 'sheep-faced' figurine made of pottery, with pointed nose and ears and elongated head. Glossy red slip with clothing and ornament painted in white pigment.

title: Chimu beaker (Peru)
desc: Silver beaker; ornithomorphic face on two sides; headdress with circular indentations; sheet metal.

title: Early Quimbaya helmet (Peru)
desc: Hemispherical helmet made of tumbaga by hammering and embossing. The circumference is slightly oval; two opposite pairs of holes at the edge might have been used for a chin strap. The embossed design is composed of geometric forms made of lines and dots, and a naked female with upraised arms and a swollen belly that suggests she is pregnant.

title: The Turquoise Mosaics (Series)
medium: human bone; turquoise; conch shell; pyrite; deer skin; lignite; pine; agave
desc: Human skull covered with turquoise and lignite mosaic held in place with pine resin adhesive. The nasal cavity is lined with red thorny oyster shell (Spondylus princeps). The eyes are made of polished pyrite surrounded by white conch (Strombus) shell. There is a lining made of deerskin and maguey (Agave) fibre on the interior and long deerskin straps which were originally painted red (traces of the iron rich ochre pigment remain). The skull is believed to represent Tezcatlipoca, or Smoking Mirro

title: Late Classic Maya vessel (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd with basal flange, part of a pottery vessel, plate or dish  (possibly a tripod).Inner is largely blackened over a light brown slip with darker brown lines visible; outer is light brown with a reddish brown decorative element; some soil present on the outer surface.

title: Chimu textile (Peru)
desc: Textile; two pieces sewn together down centre; cotton; double twill weave;  main design laid out in rhomboidal grid with diamond fields, each containing a frontal figure with upturned hands and feet; ear (spools?) in form of bird heads and forking head appendages.  Diamond fields outlined in rows of volutes.  The two main design fields are edged with row of geometric diamond and triangular motifs.  Dark brown and tan.

title: Teotihuacan whistle; figurine (Mexico)
desc: Whistle (with human head) made of pottery.

title: Maya piece-mould (Copan)
desc: Copan; Unidentified piece-mould: Plaster piece-mould, the moulded side of which is coated with boiled linseed oil(?).

title: Late Classic Maya vessel (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd with basal flange, part of a pottery vessel, plate or dish. Inner surface is yellowish red and red; outer red fabric visible.

title: Chimu; Inca vessel (Peru)
desc: Jar with flaring spout and flat base.  Jar neck features human face on both sides; large earspools and long ears; collar at neck.  Vessel body features design panel on two sides; each contains four volutes; stippled backgrounds.  Stippling is low relief relative to body. Vertical mould seams down sides.  Body burnished; spout wiped inside and out; base unfinished.  Black with reddish brown patch on one side.

title: Late Classic Maya vessel; plate; dish (Pusilha)
desc: Rim sherd from shallow, pottery bowl or plate (possibly a tripod) with  basal flange. Both surfaces yellow/red to red.

title: Chimu vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Double-chambered whistling bottle; narrow tapering spout, flat bridge; chambers taper towards base.  Front chamber surmouted by male figure seated cross-legged, in flat headgear (broken), large earspools, necklace and pectoral; bears beaker in left hand.  Both chambers stippled except down sides where smoothing of seams occurred.  Vertical  mould seams down sides of each chamber, so two mould-sets used; bridge added to incorporate whistle mechanism.  Smooth areas polished.  Black.

title: Aztec figure (Mexico)
desc: Stone figure of a male standing on a plinth, broken in two pieces. It has two holes, one on the chest and the other on the simple head-dress which may have served to insert gem-stones. Both hands are pressed against the belly.

title: rebozo (Mexico)
desc: Rebozo; woven of cotton; rectangle of beige cotton with warp pattern weave stripes of blue (ikat-dyed?) and white, brown and off-white, and blue and off-white; finger-knotted fringe at each end.

title: Chimu bowl; vessel (North Coast Peru)
desc: Bowl with figurative handle and rounded base.  Handle in form of hairless canine; rib or folds of skin detailing along sides.  Vertical mould seams run down sides and along base;dog's limbs modelled and applied.  Burnished.  Black.

title: Chancay textile (Peru)
desc: Textile fragment; loincloth side border?; shaped to semicircle at one end; cross knit loop stitch edging; cotton warp and camelid fibre weft; slit tapestry with eccentric weft outlining of figures; long slits at sides closed at intervals by dovetailing; interlocked birds; cream  cotton with tan, red, indigo,  and black wool.

title: ear-ornament (South Africa)
desc: Pair of circular ear-discs; wooden base with decoration in plastic or perspex. One side is blue with central band in orange with black and white centre. On b), orange is closer to red in colour. Reverse has white central band, with green and red semi-circles. On b), green semi-circle is missing.

title: bracelet; rattle (Nigeria)
desc: Bracelet made of iron; with three rattles and a knot join.

title: Vandal coin (Minted in: Carthage)
desc: Silver coin.; Diademed, draped and cuirassed, bust of Hilderic, right.; Denominational mark within wreath.

title: Benin Ivory (Series)
desc: Musical horn (oko); made of elephant ivory. Side-blown trumpet with opening on convex side. Decorated with carved crocodile head on upper section.

title: waist-pendant (Benin City)
desc: Waist pendant; lost-wax cast in brass. U-shaped in form with single large suspension loop at top. Depicts central female figure in relief. Figure wears coral bead ornaments, skirt and headdress, and holds mirror charm in right proper hand. Series of small loops around outer edge of pendant for susension of crotals or bells.

title: textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile; woven from raffia palm fibre and dyed purple.  Embroidered using natural and dark brown dyed raffia palm fibre thread in conventional and cut pile techniques to form geometric patterns.  Edges folded over and sewn.

title: perfume-holder; cosmetic-container (Lesotho)
desc: Perfume-holder; cosmetic container (?); made of turtle-shell with pink and navy blue glass beadwork attached to bottom.

title: Benin Ivory (Series)
desc: Gong- or bell-striker; a long rod made of elephant ivory in a cylindrical form with the head of a reptile carved at one end (the other being broken).

title: textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile made of raffia palm fibre with cut pile geometric design. Three edges are turned in and hemmed.

title: spear (Africa)
desc: Spear, made of wooden shaft, metal head, binding of wire, and butt wrapped in skin. Skin with dark brown and cream-coloured fur or hair. Wire binding woven in spiralling pattern.

title: arrow (Oceania)
desc: Arrow, made of cane shaft, wooden head, with plant fibre binding. Head is formed of barbs pointing downward near tip, which appears charred and partly missing. Inserts into top of shaft, which is bound with a band of plant fibre binding.

title: figure (Nigeria)
desc: A cast bronze figure of two standing hook-beaked birds, one with head detached, on a rectangular base; both birds have long beaks, a short rectangular crest, large round eyes, a swan-shaped neck and fan-shaped tails; the wings patterned with two horizontal rows of oblique strokes (less distinct on the headed bird); the toes marked by four grooves [less distinct on headless bird); each bird leans a little to the centre; a small ridge under front of base gives figures balance.

title: drum (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Fragment of a wooden Bukita drum with a pattern of knots in relief.

title: cloth; garment (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Cloth (garment?); woven of cotton; six rectangles of pale purple cloth, sewn together lengthwise; cowrie shells sewn along each seam; decorated with tassels of cowrie shells with brass bells.

title: figure (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Female figure carved from elephant ivory with a rounded head, flat at the top and inserted with a loop of metal wire. The torso is decorated with circlar motifs in a 'X' pattern across the front and with two rows of eight motifs along the centre of the back. The back is also carved with two deep vertical lines and a series of horizontal carved marks which radiate from the spine to the arms.The right ankle has a small circular motif. The figure is hollow.

title: cloth; textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile made of raffia palm fibre; originally part of a larger piece. Dyed dark brown base with embroidered raised stitch in linear and geometric pattern in dyed brown and dark brown shades; with three turned and hemmed edges.

title: Benin Ivory (Series)
desc: Arm-cuff, one of a pair; made of ivory inlaid with copper. Formed of two inter-penetrating cylinders. Outer cylinder depicts repeating figures of Oba with mudfish legs, swinging crocodiles in each hand above head. Inner cylinder depicts twin elephant heads alternating with Oba figures.

title: spear (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Spear; with wooden shft and iron head. Large spear-head, six incised lines of varying length on one side of the blade. Plant fibre string wrapped around the upper section of the wooden shaft. Five bands of iron strips covering portions of the shaft. Socketed, tapering iron butt.

title: bracelet (Nigeria)
desc: Bracelet made of ?brass; with lapped ends, diamond-shaped section with finely hatched surface.

title: ear-ornament (South Africa)
desc: Pair of circular ear-discs; wooden base made of two pieces of wood, with perspex or plastic external decoration. Design of white semi-circle with smaller black inner semi-circle; and red semi-circle with smaller white inner semi-circle. Two green stripes as outer central bands with pairs of silver metal domes, and inner stripes in black and white. Reverse consists of pink and green semi-circles with white central stripe.

title: key (Benin City)
desc: Brass key hook with four-arm cross finial.

title: mat (Nigeria)
desc: Mat made of fibre and edged all round with red-dyed leather. Mat has lengths of fibre covering surface on one side.

title: skirt; textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile; skirt; woven from raffia palm fibre. Composed of multiple pieces hand sewn together. Main sections woven in natural shade with supplementary floating weft pattern. Central panel embroidered conventionally in diamond shapes with dark brown dyed raffia in geomateric pattern. Central panel has cut pile trim of natural colour. Main sections are flanked on length by panels embroidered conventionally using dark brown-dyed raffia in geometric pattern.

title: plate (Nigeria)
desc: Grass coil-sewn plate,slightly oval, in red, black and natural colours.

title: pubic-cover (South Africa)
desc: Pubic-cover made of glass beads on leather backing, of 'swallowtail' form.

title: bag (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Salt currency bag made of cloth (palm).

title: head-rest (Africa)
desc: Head-rest with flat base and concave rest supported by an openwork structure of columns. Embellished with numerous blocks of incised, triangular motifs.

title: mat (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Rectangular mat made of fibre.

title: belt (South Africa)
desc: A belt consisting of three rolls of hide sewn together and covered with pink, green, black and white glass beadwork, and with a number of tassels of plaited vegetable fibre at each end.

title: photographic print (Nigeria)
desc: Nigeria [?], vegetables displayed on European chair. Outdoor setting. Medium: Gelatin silver print.

title: bead (Nigeria)
desc: 18 coconut shell beads on a string

title: commemorative head (Benin City)
desc: Commemorative head; made of terracotta. Human head depicted wearing high beaded coral collar (at front only) and beaded udaeba headband. Has tiered hairstyle with two front partings, hair plaits at either side of face, three inset scarification marks above eyes and impressed irises. Hole through centre of head.

title: token (Issued in: South Africa)
desc: Brass token.; Profile portrait of Paul Kruger facing left at centre.; Giraffe and baby at centre.

title: pendant-mask (Benin City)
desc: Pendant mask (?); lost-wax cast in brass. In form of human head; pupils inlaid with iron nails. Three projections on headdress. Cat's whisker scarification marks at each side of mouth; striated beard on chin. Snakes from each eye down cheeks to corner of mouth. Loops for suspension of bells or crotals at sides of face. Lead repair at inner corner of left proper eye.

title: shield (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Shield made of wood, with cane and plaitwork.

title: pot; vessel (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Smoking pot, smoking vessel made of gourd; with six human figures engraved on surface. Gourd is open at one end with a small hole bored at opposite end. Gourd vessel has cracking on surface with indigenous repair carried out using two small metal strips.

title: armlet (Benin City)
desc: Armlet; lost-wax cast in copper alloy.

title: spoon; scraper (Santa Cruz Islands)
desc: Spoon of nautilus shell

title: beer-vessel (South Africa)
desc: Coiled spherical beer-vessel made of pottery; rimless with rounded base. Blackened and decorated with bands of small applied pellets.

title: necklace (South Africa)
desc: Necklace made of glass beads alternating with irregular lumps of brown vegetable matter.

title: drinking-cup (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Drinking cup made of wood.

title: petroglyph (South Africa)
desc: Approximately lozenge-shaped Andesite block with engraving of a quagga or a zebra.

title: breast-cloth; costume (South Africa)
desc: A married woman's roughly-shaped breast-cloth (part of overall costume) consisting of a complete, un-scraped, red-dyed sheepskin with a strip of cotton cloth attached to each of the protruding leg sections on the upper edge of the garment.

title: smoking-pipe; water-pipe (South Africa)
desc: Smoking/water pipe made of long, curved and pointed ?antelope horn; with narrow wooden tube inserted as mouthpiece into hole at side, with ?clay 'cap' with hole at each end fastened on end of tube.

title: snuff-bottle; snuff-box; stopper (South Africa)
desc: Snuff-bottle, snuff-box made of nut (?), wood (?) [a], with stopper made of horn [b].

title: textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile; woven from raffia palm fibre. Composed of two pieces hand-sewn together and dyed dark brown.  Tassels on two sides.

title: bow (Africa)
desc: Bow of wood, tapers towards each end and each tip is carved narrower (to hold bow string). Large spit in wood running lengthwise towards one end. Three areas of vegetal fibre binding wound around bow.

title: mat; textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile woven from raffia palm fibre. Three pieces handsewn together with french seams, edged with cotton binding that has been machined onto the textile, two adjacent edges turned to the upper surface, the other two to the lower. There are three patches machine sewn onto the textile

title: bell (Benin City)
desc: Bell; lost-wax cast in brass. Conical in form with loop handle and iron clapper.

title: drum (Nigeria)
desc: Large wooden drum ornamented with figures of a lizard etc in relief; skin membrane.

title: drum; drum-beater (Democratic Republic of Congo)
medium: wood; skin; leather; fibre; glass; gum-resin; rubber
desc: Wood drum (a) with leather handle and string to which is attached one blue bead. Drum-beater (b) made of wood with ?vegetable gum or rubber head.

title: box; stool (Nigeria)
desc: Wood and bark box (a) of circular form, with fitted lid (b).

title: roulette; potter's tool/implement (Nigeria)
desc: Roulette, potter's tool made of wood.

title: penis-sheath (South Africa)
desc: Gourd of a bottle shape with an opening at each end. Possibly a penis case.

title: drum (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Drum carved in wood with a handle and human hand.

title: token (Issued in: South Africa)
desc: Brass token.; Inscription around the edges.; Value at centre.

title: celt; religious/ritual equipment (Benin City)
desc: Stone celt. Kidney-shaped; yellow to brown in colour.

title: mat; garment (Oceania)
desc: Mat or garment made of loom(?) woven of cotton and vegetable fibre; rectangle of cotton, one half thin red and off-white stripes, one half thick off-white and thin red stripes; fringe on each short side.

title: rattle (Benin City)
desc: Rattle made of iron. Hollow cone in form with four prongs at top.

title: bracelet (Nigeria)
desc: Bracelet made of ?brass; with lapped ends, diamond-shaped section with finely hatched surface.

title: Lower Niger Bronzes (Series)
desc: Bell; lost-wax cast in low tin bronze. Flared conical form; with looped strap handle. Triangular geometric raised decoration on shoulders and waist; flared undecorated lower lip. Copper alloy object of cylindrical form (armlet?) inserted inside, heavily corroded.

title: cloth (Nigeria)
desc: Cloth: a single hand-sewn width of handspun cotton and raffia: the thread is of natural white cotton and cotton dyed in two shades of dark indigo. The warp is formed of alternating threads of raffia and dark indigo; these are divided by by six narrow stripes formed from the two shaes of indigo alone. The weft is entirely of the darker shade of indigo. The warp ends have been left as a fringe, with the cotton threads (but not the raffia) twisted to form tassels.

title: Colonial building (Object)
desc: Colonial residence. Location unidentified. Late 19th-Early 20th Century (?). Medium: Black and white. Gelatin silver print [?].

title: neck-band (Temotu)
medium: shell; coix seed; pearlshell; cowrie shell
desc: Neck-band made of black and white shells, coix seeds, pearl-shell, cord, cypraea shells.

title: box (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Box made of wood, camwood.

title: token (Issued in: South Africa)
desc: Brass token.; Profile portrait of young Queen Victoria at centre. Inscription around the edges.; Double-headed eagle at centre.

title: cloth (Nigeria)
desc: Cloth (aso oke). Composed of thirteen narrow strips, machine-sewn together. Repeating warp strips: magenta-dyed cotton with ikat (magenta-dyed cotton on white cotton) stripes. Magenta-dyed weft with hidden white cotton weft stripes. Tassels on back of cloth in seam. Some joined warp strips. Machine-sewn hems at both ends.

title: skirt; textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Woman's skirt woven from raffia palm fibre. Composed of four central sections of natural-coloured raffia in plain weave with supplementary weft float patterning; hand-stitched together. Decorative borders embroidered in brown-dyed raffia with lozenge motifs. Central seam decorated with embroidered cross and zigzag patterns; edged with cut-pile bobbles.

title: drawing (Santa Cruz Islands)
desc: Drawing on paper (landscape), coloured pencils; white background with four objects; one purple, blue and red shell, one orange shell, one grey and green shell and one orange and green squid or octopus; a pink link connects the two middle shells. Signed and dated by the artist.

title: machete; dance-sword; dance-knife (Nigeria)
desc: Dance-sword, dance-knife; made of wood (iroko? or ebony?). Inform of broad-bladed machete.

title: token (Issued in: South Africa)
desc: Brass dog tax token with hole at upper centre; no.905.; Coat of arms at centre.; Inscription at centre.

title: circlet; necklet (South Africa)
desc: Circlet or necklet made of leather ornamented with cowrie shells.

title: celt (Nigeria)
desc: Stone, shaped? Irregular depressions on one side.

title: snuff equipment (Nigeria)
desc: A cast brass snuff-taker in the form of a tubular receptacle mounted on a narrow stand with circular base.

title: figure (Benin City)
desc: Three figures made of cast brass; part of larger object. Standing female figure (a) with beadwork headdress and collar; leopard with bead and bell collar (b); leopard without collar (c).

title: razor (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Razor of iron, with perforation.

title: textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile; woven from natural and dark brown-dyed raffia palm fibre to produce a geometric design. Fringes on two sides.

title: knife (Nigeria)
desc: Knife with loop handle made of iron.

title: spear (Oceania)
desc: Spear, made of wood. Head comprised of five barbs facing downward on one side. Butt tapers to a round blunted point.

title: cushion-cover (Lower Congo)
desc: A rectangular woven cushion-cover made of raffia palm fibre, decorated with cut-pile embroidery and with raffia pom-poms at each corner. It has been stuffed with paper at some time in the (relatively) recent past.

title: cooking-pot (Africa)
desc: Ceramic cooking-pot rim sherd; handmade; hard and brittle micaceous fabric with coarse grit inclusions.

title: finger-ring (Nigeria)
desc: Finger- ring, carved out of shell of nut, dark

title: token (Issued in: South Africa)
desc: Brass pass with hole at centre.; Numerals around the edges.; Ornamental wreath around the edges.

title: bow (Oceania)
desc: Bow of wood, dark brown in colour with tapered ends and slight curvature. Ends with remnants of vegetal fibre wrapped around.

title: arrow (Santa Cruz Islands)
desc: Group of thirteen arrows comprising reed shafts, with carved wood heads tipped with human bone and coloured red, black and white.

title: coin (Centurion)
desc: Silver coin.; Profile portrait of Paul Kruger facing left at centre.; Value and date surrounded by ornamental wreath at centre.

title: box; head-rest (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Box-headrest. Low box (a); carved  of wood; canoe-shaped, decorated with strip of bark. Box-lid (b) in shape of a head-rest carved of wood; a diamond-shaped piece of wood with arch on top for a head-rest.

title: textile (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Textile strip woven from raffia palm fibre and embroidered using a combination of conventional and cut-pile techniques in a geometric pattern. Section of pattern at end of strip unfinished. Part of border conventionally embroidered in dark brown-dyed raffia.

title: spoon (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Oil spoon made of wood.

title: ornament (Nigeria)
desc: Ornament made of copper.

title: spoon (Africa)
desc: Spoon of wood, carved with deep bowl and long handle. Below bowl on handle a carved collar that projects from the handle at a perpendicular angle is multi-pointed.

title: bell (Benin City)
desc: Bell; lost-wax cast in brass. Quadrangular in form with slightly flaring sides and strap handle. Decorated on front panel with human head in low relief set in ropework cartouche and surrounded by openwork lattice. Left and right panels have central mudfish with openwork lattice. Back panel is plain. Iron clapper suspended from hole at top.

title: crucible (Benin City)
desc: Crucible; made of pottery with melted glass residue on outside. Part of lost-wax casting series showing manufacture of pendant mask.

title: tooth-brush (Nigeria)
desc: Toothbrush made of bent wood, held in place by vegetable fibre.

title: Benin Coral (Series)
desc: Bracelet made from coral beads on vegetable fibre and wire.

title: necklace (South Africa)
desc: A child's necklace of red, green and white glass beads strung on cotton and with a small central panel with geometric motif, predominantly in black, green and yellow glass beads..

title: whistle (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Whistle made of ivory, with one finger-hole.

title: bow (Democratic Republic of Congo)
desc: Bow made of wood.

title: bag (Africa)
desc: Bag in cream cloth with design in blue-black, red and yellow with long fringe of tassels and suspended from a hooped metal handle.

title: Thomas W. Edge-Partington album 1 (Series)
desc: Digital image of a photograph, black & white, showing, Solomon Islands

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Gold coin (base).; Head, right (worn).; Horse and charioteer right; lyre below horse.

title: Iron Age II bowl (Baba Jan)
desc: Painted bowl; pottery; handmade; light brown ware with two red painted bands around the rim.

title: Late Bronze Age knife (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy socketed knife with a rectangular socket with four rivet holes.

title: Late Bronze Age armlet (Enkomi)
desc: Silver armlet, composed of a thick cylindrical ring with open straight ends; cast; the ends are decorated with faint incised cross-hatched lines and bands; surface corroded.

title: Iron Age strap-fitting; harness-fitting (Stanwick)
desc: Fragment of a copper alloy strap-fitting, perhaps from harness.  A central stud fitting with staple strap-attachment on underside is all that remains.

title: Early Bronze Age I; Proto-Urban Period bowl (Bab ed-Dhra)
culture: Early Bronze Age I; Proto-Urban Period
period: Early Bronze Age I; Proto-Urban Period
desc: Pottery bowl.

title: Late Bronze Age toggle (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked slotted antler fragment; hollowed deer antler; lower extremity original, upper extremity the result of fracturing; highly polished surface, appears to have been burnt; similar objects are commonly referred to as 'toggles' although their precise function is unknown.

title: Iron Age shield; binding (Mill Hill)
desc: Copper alloy binding from corner of  the shield and the clamp on the end of the curved edge. The binding has a straight and a concave length meeting at a globular knob. The knob is hollow and seems to have been worked from sheet, not cast. The curved end is unbroken and terminates halfway under the clamp. The end of the straight length is slightly angled and not obviously unbroken, but when it was excavated it was within clamp 1990,0102.13.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Gold coin.; Head, left.; Horse, left; wheel below.

title: Iron Age animal remains (Makeshift Cemetery)
desc: Left humerus bone of sheep.

title: Late Bronze Age needle (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Copper alloy needle shank; tip of needle is missing but slight splitting of the alloy forming pronges which would indicate a needle hole; corroded, dull green; circular in cross-section; shank thins and flattens towards the needle head

title: Iron Age hook (miscellaneous) (Stanwick)
desc: Copper alloy hook, with split curve along hook.  Loop at top, probably for suspension.  Appears to have been cast flat, with flattish underside, and then curved round to form a hook shape.

title: Iron Age ornament (Stanwick)
desc: Copper alloy decorated sheet metal object with openwork decoration.

title: Neolithic adze (Pahang)
desc: Celt, adze. Made of stone. Adze blade, ground.

title: Late Bronze Age sleeve fastener (Ireland)
desc: Gold sleeve fastener. The crescent-shaped body is decorated with longitudinal incised grooves on the outer surface. The ends are decorated with a fine diamond pattern between two bands of horizontal incised grooves. The terminals expand to form two big flat plain discs set at an angle to the body.

title: Late Bronze Age sword (Thames, River)
desc: Bronze sword: hilt and upper blade. Three slots for rivets.  Khaki patina; sword bent almost to breaking point.

title: Iron Age plaque (Tuft Hill)
desc: Flat lanceote plaque made from split long-bone of a sheep/goat; pierced, and with many fine scratch-marks on one face.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Brittany)
desc: Silver coin.; Head, right.; Horse, right; boar below horse.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, left.; Bull butting, right.

title: Bronze Age jar (Subr)
desc: Rim of a jar; ledge-rim; hard-fired; greyish ware with thick salmon-pink core; dark maroon slip paint on the top and exterior

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Issued in: Gaul (historic))
desc: Silver coin.; Helmeted head of Roma, left.; Horse, left.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, right, with curly hair.; Horse, left; cross below.

title: Iron Age awl; point (Little Woodbury)
desc: Bone awl or point.

title: Iron Age pedestal vase (Malmains Farm)
desc: Pottery pedestal vase.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Bare-headed, draped bust, right.; Bull, right.

title: Iron Age ring; ingot (Ken Hill)
desc: Gold alloy ingot ring with overlapping terminals.

title: Early Iron Age spear-head
desc: Bronze spear-head with conical socket and narrow foliate blade. Very narrow elongated blade with slightly angular profile at lower end, slender faceted socket. Two lateral pin-holes in socket. Dull green to brown patina.

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Fragment of gold alloy torc: four twisted wires twisted together.

title: Iron Age bead (Nilgiri Hills)
desc: A  rock crystal bead in long cross section like a long parallel-sided diamond.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Issued in: Gaul (historic))
desc: Silver coin.; Head, left.; Horse left; below, dolphin.

title: Late Bronze Age axe (Mahalon)
desc: Copper alloy socketed axes. Ten.

title: Iron Age strap-fitting (Westhall)
desc: Copper alloy strap fitting (strap union), with glass 'enamel'. Cast plate with two loops projecting at the back.  The upper surface of the plate is decorated with red blue and white enamel.  The red enamel infills pelta and related forms, while the blue and white enamel infills small circular cells.

title: Iron Age vessel (Hertford Heath)
desc: Pottery sherd.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
