desc: Undiagnostic fired clay fragment with a single smoothed surface; pale orange sandy fabric with no inclusions; abraded; unknown function

title: Iron Age casting waste (Stanwick)
desc: Small irregular, amorphous copper alloy lump. Possibly waste from casting or other metalworking, or an object melted down so completely that it is now unidentifiable.

title: Late Bronze Age mould (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: A probable mould fragment; buff to pale brown very sandy fabric; all edges broken and abraded; flat exterior; interior with presumed fragment of matrix, very slight groove present

title: Late Bronze Age worked stone fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked stone fragment. Mid grey sandstone; single worked surface.

title: Late Bronze Age razor (Weiby)
desc: Copper alloy razor, handle broken off recently, cross line bundles at handle end, three S-bands in multiple line frame. At handle alternating bundles of six transversal and lengthwise lines, green patina.

title: Iron Age bead (Cyprus)
desc: Sard bead cylindrical bead with tapered ends; chipped.

title: Late Bronze Age loom-weight (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay loomweight fragment. A fragment of a loomweight; most likely triangular in shape, abraded and broken; a rounded smoothed surface; central perforation visible which is straight and cyclindrical; slight sooting around the top of the inside of the perforation; orange-brown sandy fabric with occasional large flint inclusions (up to 10.2mm)

title: Iron Age ring; sample (Ken Hill)
desc: One of a chain of ten silver ingot rings.

title: Late Iron Age mail-armour (Ashwell)
desc: Fragments of iron mail armour (“chain-mail”) made from alternate rows of interlinked riveted and butt-welded rings.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, left.; Bull, left.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment with a single impression; the impression could be made by a wattle or could be the perforation from a loomweight, unable to determine which; orange-brown sandy fabric with no inclusions; very abraded, secondarily fired

title: Iron Age spear-head (Lisnacroghera Bog)
desc: Iron spear-head. Slender, leaf-shaped blade, lozenge-shaped in cross-section.  Areas of haphazard, rocked-graver lines at the base of the blade on each face give a rough matted effect, but these are scarcely visible to the naked eye.  The hammered, round-sectioned socket is open along the vertical seam.  Two transverse iron rivets pierce the lower end; each projects slightly from the surface of the socket.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Gold coin.; Abstract design, sometimes thought to represent a boat or a boar.; Abstract design.

title: Middle Bronze Age spear-head (Thames, River)
desc: Copper alloy socketed spearhead.  Blade rapier-shaped, with bevelled edges.  String type loop at base of, but separate from, blade.  Decoration comprises of two pairs of conical gold studs on bottom part of blade; loops and blunt underside of blade are incised with v-shaped lines; pointillé outlining the junction of wings and midrib.  Part of wooden shaft remains in socket.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Rhône Valley)
desc: Gold-plated coin.; Helmeted head, left.; Hippocamp, left.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Czech Republic)
desc: Silver coin.; Laureate head of Zeus, right.; Horseman, right.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Spiral around central pellet.; Three S-shaped motifs around central pellet.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Figure with plaited hair advancing right, holding spear in right hand and torc or disc in left hand.; Animal, right; fibula-shaped object above.

title: Iron Age jar (Cyprus)
desc: Pottery cooking pot on three tall legs; wheel-made; two band-handles and two lugs.

title: Late Bronze Age vessel (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. Fabric is composed of fine sand inclusions, almost untempered. The rim is squared but represents less than 10% of the vessel circumference. The sherd is burnished. The sherd is grey/buff in colour.

title: Middle Bronze Age vessel (El Argar)
desc: Pottery vessel, bowl with a deep round body, round straight and slightly notched rim, smoothened surface, dark grey colour with brown areas.

title: Late Bronze Age retouched flake (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Flint retouched flake; pale brown-grey flint, moderately patinated; retouched at distal end.

title: Late Bronze Age disc (Podmockl)
desc: Gold disc. Thin circular foil of beaten gold with pointillé and embossed decoration with four symmetrical perforations along the outer edge. The decoration consists of a circle of pointillé dots along the outer edge, four bands of three pointillé lines forming a cross and five bosses inside a circle of pointillé. There are traces of a brown substance, possibly soil, on both the surfaces of the disc.

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. Fabric is composed of sparse fine flint inclusions. The rim is squared but represents less than 10% of the vessel diametre. The sherd has soot on the exterior. The sherd is dark brown/black in colour. The surfaces are burnished.

title: Iron Age sheet (Welwyn)
desc: One box of copper alloy sheet fragments

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. Fabric is composed of abundant coarse flint inclusions and the sherd is brown in colour. The rim is squared but represents less than 5% of the rim diametre. The sherd has smoothed surfaces.

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Silver torc: two twisted strands each of two wires twisted together; triple loop terminal, ingot ring linked to one terminal and wire ring and bent ingot ring fragment linked to other terminal.

title: Iron Age casting waste (Stanwick)
desc: Small irregular, amorphous copper alloy lump. Possibly waste from casting or other metalworking, or an object melted down so completely that it is now unidentifiable.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul (historic))
desc: Gold coin.; Laureate head, right.; Horse left, looking back.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Brittany)
desc: Silver coin.; Laureate head of Apollo, right.; Horse and charioteer, right; wheel below horse. The horse has a human face.

title: Late Bronze Age bowl (Levant)
desc: Glazed composition hemispherical blossom bowl; mould-made with modelled and incised detail; twelve-petalled rosette in relief on exterior with alternating white and coloured petals (colour of glaze not preserved); the spaces between the tops of petals are black; white band at rim; interior solid colour (glaze not preserved); surface worn; mended from fragments and partially reconstructed.

title: Iron Age ingot (Ken Hill)
desc: Copper alloy bar ingot.

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery body/base sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. Fabric is composed of abundant coarse, poorly sorted, flint inclusions. The base is simple and slightly splayed and represents 10% of a 12cm base diametre. The sherd has smoothed surfaces and is dark brown in colour. The underside is abraded.

title: Iron Age II juglet (West Bank)
desc: Juglet; pottery; one-handled; smooth.

title: Late Bronze Age; Iron Age drinking-horn (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy ornamental band, probably from drinking-horn. Formed of rivetted sheet metal.

title: Iron Age II horse bridle-bit (Luristan)
desc: Copper alloy horse-bit pieces in the form of winged goats; two loops on back and bit has single bar; cheekpieces made as lost-wax castings; bit made by hammering.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Spiral around central pellet.; Three S-shaped motifs around central pellet.

title: Iron Age; La Tène vessel (Beine)
desc: Pottery long-necked vessel with painted decoration of two repeats of double-scroll in self-coloured orange, with red coated background. Exterior horizontal burnish on neck, vertical on body; interior smoothed off horizontally. Firing: single stage oxidized; self-coloured orange, with dark red coating.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, left.; Bull, left.

title: Iron Age; La Tène I ornament (Somme-Bionne)
desc: A thin concave copper alloy disc with a central knob of coral secured by a copper alloy nail whose shank is broken on the underside. There is an openwork border which is badly broken, but the outermost points are rounded, suggesting that its full extent is represented.

title: Iron Age terret (Stanwick)
desc: Cast copper alloy terret with tanged attachment bar, rectangular in cross-section.  The ring is curved on the inner side and facetted on the outside, to give an almost lozenge-shaped cross-section.  The ring is drilled with rows of circular holes which probably once held enamel.

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Silver torc of two strands twisted together, one surviving double-loop terminal; distorted.

title: Late Bronze Age wheel (Heathery Burn Cave)
desc: Bronze nave band: annular band cast with two ribs and a spreading flange; used to strengthen the hub of a wooden spoked wheel and prevent splitting; surface corroded; green.

title: Iron Age hammerstone (Denmark)
desc: Facetted black hammerstone, brown quartzite

title: Iron Age binding (Stanwick)
desc: Short, bent length of copper alloy binding strip, loosely curved into very open U-shape, broken at both ends.

title: Iron Age spatula (Nilgiri Hills)
desc: A narrow, thin-sectioned bar of iron, flattened at one end to form a kind of spatula.

title: Late Bronze Age casting waste (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Copper alloy small ovoid piece of metal; no obvious damage; most likely waste from metal-working process; mostly rough pale green surface, small patch of reed green patina

title: Iron Age bead (Nilgiri Hills)
desc: A long rock crystal bead with square section.

title: Late Bronze Age plaque (Podmockl)
desc: Gold plaque. Thin trapezium-shape foil of beaten gold with pointillé and embossed decoration which has been perforated at the top. The decoration consists of a row of pointillé dots along the outer edge. The centre of the plaque is decorated with two bosses and rows of pointillé dots probably forming a human figure. There are traces of a brown substance, possibly soil, on both the surfaces of the plaque.

title: Middle Bronze Age; Iron Age torc (Law Farm)
desc: Gold alloy ribbon torc. The torc was made by beating an ingot into a flat band that was then twisted from left to right.The terminals are circular in section and bent-back to form hooks. They also have conical knobs.

title: Late Bronze Age spindle-whorl (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay spindlewhorl. A fired clay object, possibly a spindlewhorl; oval in shape, was formed round but one side has been flattened; an attempt at a perforation but clay morphed around a stick to form the hole as it does not go all the way through the object, then a second attempt made; a stab mark in the side of the spindlewhorl and fingernail impression or two; brown-orange sandy fabric with sparse coarse small-medium flint inclusions; highly fired

title: Late Bronze Age disc (Podmockl)
desc: Gold disc. Thin circular foil of beaten gold with pointillé and embossed decoration with four symmetrical perforations along the outer edge. The decoration consists of a circle of pointillé dots along the outer edge, four bands of three pointillé lines forming a cross and five bosses inside a circle of pointillé. There are traces of a brown substance, possibly soil, on both the surfaces of the disc.

title: Early Iron Age spear-head
desc: Bronze spear-head with conical socket and foliate blade. Long blade with rounded profile towards lower end, long and slender plain socket with two lateral pin-holes. Small parts missing. Smooth brown patina with green areas.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment with a roughly smoothed surface and fingernail impressions in the surface; orange-red sandy fabric with no inclusions; abraded

title: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age socketed axe (Netherhampton)
culture: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
period: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
desc: Copper alloy Type Portland socketed axe. Flared, trapezoidal-shaped body with three vertical ribs ending in a round pellet decoration on both faces.The central rib is shorter with a more prominent pellet. Single mouth moulding with casting flash, small side-loop below. Defined side facets and straight, broad-edged blade.

title: Late Bronze Age spear-head; socket (Broadward)
desc: Copper alloy socketed spear-head, socket fragment with rivet in place.

title: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age socketed axe (Netherhampton)
culture: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
period: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
desc: Possible copper alloy socketed axe socket fragment with ribbed decoration.

title: Late Bronze Age spindle-whorl (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay spindlewhorl.A probably fragment of a spindlewhorl; no perforation visible; smoothed surface grey sandy fabric with sparse small flint inclusions; highly fired

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. Fabric is composed of sparse, finer flint inclusions. The rim is rounded but represents less than 5% of the vessel circumference. Most likely belongs to a jar with rounded, slightly bulbous bodies and short upright neck. The surfaces are burnished and is brown/black in colour.

title: Early Iron Age bulla-pendant (Ruvo)
desc: Bronze bivalve circular pendant (bulla) with upper transverse tube for suspension. Both symmetrical faces of bivalve and upper, linking tube formed from one hammered sheet, shaped and folded to create a hollow bulla with flat borders, joined by four rivets with large, decorative heads on front only. Bronze wire threaded through upper tube and twisted to form decorative coils and loop for suspension. Dull green patina with much corrosion.

title: Early Bronze Age; Middle Bronze Age drinking-cup (Central Anatolia Region)
culture: Early Bronze Age; Middle Bronze Age
period: Early Bronze Age; Middle Bronze Age
desc: Pottery cup of orange red hand-made ware (Cappadocian) with brown painted decoration with painted geometric designs; high handle; pointed bottom deliberately flattened; firing uneven so that colour varies in patches from buff to russet red; decorated in brown varnish; around shoulder decoration of rows of chequers and lozenges broken by vertical bars and lines; parallel to this on lower body, a chevron of latticed lines.

title: Bronze Age; Iron Age casting waste (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy amorphous blob, from casting process.

title: Iron Age rod; bridle-bit (Stanwick)
desc: Very worn length of copper alloy rod, flattened oval in cross-section, and tapering towards one end. Moulded in the round, with median ridge on either side. Possibly part of a bridle-bit side link.

title: Late Bronze Age spear-head (Hørsholm)
desc: Copper alloy socketed spear-head, two rivet holes, on the edges from the rivet holes are small notched ribs, fine brown bog patina.

title: Neolithic celt (Naga Hills)
desc: Polished brown stone celt with mark.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Moldavia (historic))
desc: Silver coin.; Laureate head of Zeus, left.; Horseman, left; cross above, branch below.

title: Late Neolithic dagger (Daugård)
desc: Flint dagger, amorphous handle, grey.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Gold coin.; Blank.; Stylised horse, right.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Brittany)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, right.; Horse and charioteer right; figure below horse. The horse has a human face.

title: Late Iron Age mail-armour (Ashwell)
desc: Fragments of iron mail armour (“chain-mail”) made from alternate rows of interlinked riveted and butt-welded rings.

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim/shoulder sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. The fabric is composed of moderate to abundant finer flint inclusions. The rim is squared belonging to a jar with a high rounded shoulder and an upright neck, but represents less than 5% of the vessel diametre. The sherd is secondarily fired and is orange/grey in colour.

title: Middle Bronze Age ingot (Fitzleroi Farm)
desc: Gold ingot fragment of plano-convex section. One terminal is rounded, the other end chisel-cut and snapped.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment belonging to a pin-pricked slab. A very abraded piece of fired clay with evidence of pin-pricks but surface has come off; two smoothed surfaces (one of them partially come away) and a squared edge; pin pricks do not go all the way through but can see the ends of them, arranged in rows; grey sandy fabric with no inclusions.

title: Late Bronze Age offcut; spatula (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked bone offcut; proximal end of a sheep/goat metapodial that has been split between the condyles, most likely in the formation of a spatula.

title: Iron Age vessel (Welwyn)
desc: Pottery vessel.

title: Late Bronze Age shank (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked bone shank fragment, possibly belonging to a awl or pin; species and bone unidentifiable; broken at both ends; triangular in cross-section; polish all over.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment with a smoothed surface forming a right angle, clay almost looks cut; most likely daub abutting a structural element;  dark core and orange-yellow surface sandy fabric with no inclusions.

title: Late Bronze Age disc (Podmockl)
desc: Gold disc. Thin circular foil of beaten gold with pointillé and embossed decoration which has four symmetrical perforations along the outer edge. The decoration consists of a circle of pointillé dots along the outer edge, four bands of three pointillé lines forming a cross and five bosses inside a circle of pointillé. There are traces of a brown substance, possibly soil, on both the surfaces of the disc.

title: Iron Age plaque (North Dalton)
desc: Bone elliptical plaque, one cup-mark on one face; one end missing.

title: Late Bronze Age worked antler fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Antler sawn from skull.  Slightly burnt, pinkish in colour. Broken at main beam, sawn off at brow tine.

title: Iron Age ring; ferrule (Stanwick)
desc: Copper alloy ring-shaped ferrule. Narrow ribs define the edges, and there is a heavy raised central rib around the external circumference.

title: Late Bronze Age armlet; leglet (Kiroingen)
desc: Large armlet or leglet made of thin metal, probably hollow-cast round a clay core. The rims have inturned flanges. The exterior surface is copiously decorated with fine incised geometric ornament divided into three panels and with linear zones within.

title: Late Neolithic/Early Bronze Age disc (Hong Kong)
desc: Disc. Made of quartz (rock crystal).

title: Early Bronze Age box; pyxis (Bayindirköy)
desc: Hand made pottery box or pyxis, and lid with incised decoration; 4 holes in rim, and through lid for suspension; black.

title: Iron Age torc (Bawsey Church)
desc: Gold alloy wire torc fragment.

title: Late Bronze Age offcut (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked bone cattle rib off-cut; cut marks around one end with break; one cut notch in the lateral.

title: Iron Age terret (Antrim)
desc: Cast copper alloy platform-decorated terret.  Ring approximately D-shaped, circular to oval in cross-section.  Three plaforms of inverted sub-conical form project at equal intervals from the outer circumference.  The straigh attachment bar is V-shaped in section with a concave underside, damaged along one edge.  The curved section of the terret ring expands to flattened 'cushion' terminals where it meets the attachment bar.

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Fragment of silver torc: of three wires twisted together and merged into a loop terminal.

title: Iron Age shield; model (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy miniature shield, with raised central boss. Handle rivetted to back.  Distinctive 'hide' shape. Incised curvilinear decoration in four panels on front of shield.  Incised line around front edge suggests shield binding.

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Gold alloy torc constructed using two complex techniques.  Firstly, eight wires were twisted in pairs to the right. The resulting four strands were then spiralled in the opposite direction, to the left; they were then finished with cast open terminals. Typical 'crimping' provides the only wrought decoration.

title: Late Bronze Age point (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked bone rib point; species unidentifiable; asymmetrically worked to form a rounded point; one edge of rib intact and the other is broken; point is worked by one half of rib being rounded and the other side being concave with a prominent notch; handle end is broken; part of edge purposefully removed; slightly burnt.

title: Late Bronze Age spindle-whorl (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay spindlewhorl fragment. Two refitting fragments of a spindlewhorl; most likely biconical in shape; D-shaped profile; smoothed exterior surface; no central perforation visible; highly fired fabric, similar to a pottery fabric; brown fabric with abundant small-medium flint inclusions.

title: Iron Age; Hallstatt D; La Tène I pendant (Bergères-les-Vertus)
culture: Iron Age; Hallstatt D; La Tène I
period: Iron Age; Hallstatt D; La Tène I
desc: Pendant formed of an open ring in copper alloy wire with simple hooked ends threaded with a perforated cockle-shell.

title: Late Bronze Age button (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Copper alloy, complete, near circular disc with two concentric mouldings separated by marked furrows, encircling a central domed projection; complete and little damage; found with both faces covered with organic material (now separated); the organic material is most likely grass or straw (see Keepax Ancient Monuments Laboratory Report within Longley, 1982: pg 27); outer moulding with near flat side and more or less flattened crests on face and reverse; interior of reverse depressed with approxim

title: Iron Age; Hallstatt D; La Tène I brooch (Marson)
culture: Iron Age; Hallstatt D; La Tène I
period: Iron Age; Hallstatt D; La Tène I
desc: Copper alloy and iron brooch in two fragments: a bead of coral on the elaborate foot; twenty-coil spring; half now separate, with the pin on an iron bar, with coil-like washers at either end.

title: Iron Age; La Tène III stud (Malmains Farm)
desc: Dome-shaped copper-alloy stud

title: Iron Age penannular brooch (Polden Hill)
desc: Fragment of a copper alloy penannular brooch, missing pin and one terminal.

title: Iron Age vessel (Ken Hill)
desc: Rim fragment of silver vessel.

title: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age socketed axe (Netherhampton)
culture: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
period: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
desc: Copper alloy socketed axe, mouth fragment. Ribbed. Slight silver patina.

title: Late Bronze Age worked bone fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked bone fragment of a sheep/goat scapula with a cut slot; broken in half both in width and longitudinally.

title: Iron Age spindle-whorl (Glastonbury Lake Village)
desc: Ceramic spindle-whorl. Bulbous in form. Circular in plan, with some damage to one face. Circular perforation through the centre for the spindle.

title: tsung (Object)
desc: Tsung of opaque and variegated green stone. This slightly sloping cylinder within a prism with rounded ends extending, has been divided int five sections, each section marked with grooves delineating stylized marks. The inside of the aperture has been irregularly polished leaving slight ridges in the centre.

title: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age socketed axe (Netherhampton)
culture: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
period: Late Bronze Age; Early Iron Age
desc: Copper alloy socketed axe fragment, ribbed. Slight silver patina.

title: Middle Bronze Age; Late Bronze Age ring (Borough Hill)
culture: Middle Bronze Age; Late Bronze Age
period: Middle Bronze Age; Late Bronze Age
desc: Gold small penannular composite ring. The ring was made by stacking three short lengths of circular sectioned wire, one on top of the other, to produce a ribbed ring. The ends of the wires are smoothed to give a continuous edge.

title: Iron Age spear; ferrule (Lisnacroghera Bog)
desc: Cast copper alloy spear-butt or ferrule. Hollow circular socket terminating in basal knob of flattened-spherical form. Waisted central portion, and convex moulding near the top. The edges of the socket moulding and the upper portion of the basal knob are defined by a reserved, hyphenated line. There is also a faint, incised line around the mouth of the socket. Two opposed circular rivet holes in the socket moulding. Base worn. Almost identical to 1880,0802.123.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Undiagnostic fired clay fragment with a single impression; the impression could be made by a wattle or could be the perforation from a loomweight, unable to determine which; orange-brown sandy fabric with no inclusions; very abraded

title: Middle Neolithic gouge (Rosenvold)
desc: Grey flint polished gouge, hollow edge, pointed butt, damaged edge.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Rhône Valley)
desc: Silver coin.; Helmeted head, left.; Hippocamp, left.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin; pierced for suspension.; Head, right, with curly hair.; Horse, left; cross below.

title: Late Bronze Age cheek-piece (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked antler cheekpiece fragment; broken edges and end; part of a rectangular slot is visible; the partially intact end is polished and smoothed; small circular perforation is visible  to one side of the cut slot.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Europe)
desc: Silver coin.; Laureate head of Zeus, right.; Horseman, left.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Czech Republic)
desc: Silver coin.; Diademed head, right.; Horseman, left.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Balsthal (Balsthal hoard (probably)))
desc: Silver coin.; Helmeted head of Roma, left.; Horse, left.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, right.; Bull, right.

title: Late Bronze Age gouge (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy socketed gouge with single mouth-moulding and edges slightly converging.

title: Neolithic vessel (Thessaly)
desc: Pottery sherd. Micaceous fabric. Painted.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment, belonging to a pin-pricked slab.A fragment of fired clay with two smoothed surfaces, a rounded edge, pin-pricks on one of the smoothed surfaces and a groove marking the edge of the pin-pricks; the pin-pricks do no go all the way through the piece and are arranged in neat rows; pale orange-brown very sandy fabric with no inclusions; abraded

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Gold alloy torc fragment: broken strand from loop terminal.

title: Iron Age artefact (Arcillera)
desc: Silver fragment, in the form of a triple wire loop, with pellet near one end.  Each wire is formed from a twisted strand of silver.  The object is in two pieces. The smaller piece comprises the smallest (?inner) loop and pellet; the two larger (?outer) loops form the bigger fragment.

title: Iron Age strap-fitting; harness-fitting; fastener (Stanwick)
desc: Copper alloy strap-fitting also known as a button and loop fastener. Consists of a ring, from the underside of which protrudes a right-angled shank, terminating in a strap bar with openwork decoration.  Cast; ring is D-shaped in section, flat on the upper side, and shank is flat on reverse except for where it meets the strap bar.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment with a partial smoothed surface and a small impression, possibly belonging to a wattle; most likely belonging to a structure; pale orange-brown very sandy fabric with no inclusions; highly abraded

title: Iron Age mount (Corbridge)
desc: Copper alloy belt mount. It comprises two trumpet terminals with convex mouths joined by an elaborate broken-backed curve. At each end of the back is a square-headed stud.

title: Iron Age spindle-whorl (Glastonbury Lake Village)
desc: Ceramic spindle-whorl. Bulbous in form. Circular in plan, with some damage to one face. Circular perforation through the centre for the spindle.

title: Iron Age sword (Amlash)
desc: Copper alloy sword with winged guards; the tang would have been set into a hilt of a different material; wide blade and midrib.

title: Iron Age shield-ornament (Mill Hill)
desc: Coral knob with copper alloy pin and washer, from shield. The knob is found in association with 1990,0102.15 and may have been fitted within the central perforations of the ring. The knob is shaped underneath and set in a thin bronze ring (the washer), decorated with radiating grooves; the coral knob has been secured by a central copper alloy pin, which runs through a perforation in the coral.

title: Late Bronze Age plaque (Podmockl)
desc: Gold plaque. Thin trapezium-shape foil of beaten gold with pointillé and embossed decoration which has been perforated at the top. The decoration consists of a row of pointillé dots along the outer edge. The centre of the plaque is decorated with two bosses and rows of pointillé dots probably forming a human figure. There are traces of a brown substance, possibly soil, on both the surfaces of the plaque.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Dol-de-Bretagne (Dol-de-Bretagne hoard (probably - according to Allen 1990, p.64)))
country: Dol-de-Bretagne (Dol-de-Bretagne hoard (probably - according to Allen 1990, p.64))
desc: Silver coin.; Helmeted head, left.; Horse, left.

title: Late Bronze Age socket; knife (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Copper alloy socket fragment; asymmetrically curved cross-section indicating elliptical socket if original; otherwise possibly distorted round socket; three, possibly all four, broken edges; elliptical socket would suggest socketed knife family of implements; dark green patina interrupted by lumpy corrosion and some brighter green patches

title: Late Iron Age mail-armour (Ashwell)
desc: Fragment of iron mail armour (“chain-mail”) made from alternate rows of interlinked riveted and butt-welded rings.

title: Early Bronze Age; Middle Bronze Age model; chariot (Eastern Anatolia Region)
culture: Early Bronze Age; Middle Bronze Age
period: Early Bronze Age; Middle Bronze Age
desc: Bronze chariot model similar to chariots on seals and impressions; two-wheeled with long yoke (not now attached); wheels on an axle and turn freely; axle bent at ends to hold wheel in position; yoke not now attached to chariot itself and is in two pieces.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, right, with wild hair.; Horse, left.

title: Iron Age; Hallstatt D ear-ring (Marson)
desc: Copper alloy basket-shaped earring, decorated with cordons, two of which are ribbed.

title: Iron Age cauldron (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy miniature cauldron, damaged.

title: Neolithic celt (Nepal)
desc: A  polished stone celt with a lenticular section, a rounded blade with distinct bevelling on one side, a short body and a rough, ground down distinctly angled foot. Small quantities of a reddish material appear to adhere to rough areas of the body surface.

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery body/base sherd. Fabric comprises of very abundant medium to large flint inclusions. The sherd shows a gritted underneath of the base with some fingertip impressions on exterior wall towards base and on the interior base, this is most likely due to forming but very prominent. The sherd is buff in colour. It is a simple base shape representing 15% of a 12cm diametre base. The sherd shows some burning.

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, right.; Horse, right, looking back; human figure below.

title: Late Bronze Age awl (Vejby)
desc: Copper alloy awl; green patina, square ended.

title: Iron Age; La Tène I wheel; cart-fitting (Somme-Bionne)
desc: Iron nave hoop, D-shaped in section. Wood traces on the inside.

title: Middle Bronze Age torc (Fitzleroi Farm)
desc: Gold flange twisted torc fragment. The fragment is bent and the ends are chisel cut and snapped.

title: Early Iron Age vessel (Luristan)
desc: Body sherd; pottery; buff ware. Wheel-made.

title: Late Bronze Age; Iron Age drinking-horn (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy ornamental band, probably from drinking-horn. Formed of rivetted sheet metal.

title: Iron Age cauldron (Netherhampton)
desc: Copper alloy miniature cauldron fragment; part of rim present, with one perforated lug.

title: Iron Age; La Tène I sword (Lisnacroghera Bog)
desc: Iron sword, metal components well preserved. Leaf-shaped blade, with pronounced mid-rib flanked on each face by two shallow, longitudinal grooves. Concave sloping shoulders near the hilt. The cross-section of the tang is a flattened-oval, and maintains constant width to the tip, which is slightly recessed. Steeply bell-shaped copper alloy hilt-guard mount, rectangular in section, and finely milled along one edge.  On the tang there are two thin, oval copper alloy washers, with worn milling aroun

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay fragment with a single smoothed surface; unknown function; orange-brown sandy fabric with no inclusions; abraded

title: Iron Age torc (Sorup)
desc: Copper alloy torc, with square ends ornamented with dot-circles, body transversally ribbed. The same as 1869,0724.71 but with dot-cirlces on the outside of the ends.

title: Iron Age bead (Cyprus)
desc: String of twelve beads of steatite, rock crystal and and steatite. (1-3) are three stone biconical beads of different type (1) is undecorated green softstone.(2) is larger, black in colour, and decorated with incised lines and concentric circles (4-11) are glass eye beads, all spherical except (11) which is elongated. (12) is a depressed spherical bead of rock crystal.

title: Iron Age sheet (Stanwick)
desc: Broken fragment of thin copper alloy sheet, with smooth surfaces. No original edges or other identifiable features.

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Fragment of gold alloy torc: of two strands twisted together.

title: Late Bronze Age fired clay fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Undiagnostic fired clay fragment; one smoothed surface covered with shallow flat 'grass' impressions and two deeper slashes running acoss; deeper of these has circular profile; reverse has at least four further impressions at c.30 degree angle to the front flate examples; only one of these rear ones has a clearly round profile, the others are abraded and shallow; orange to dark grey fabric with sparse white inclusions less than 1mm in diameter

title: Continental Iron Age coin (Gaul)
desc: Copper alloy coin.; Head, right (?).; Horse, right (head absent or off flan).

title: Iron Age torc (Ken Hill)
desc: Fragment of gold alloy torc: of two wires coiled together.

title: Iron Age ring (Hanging Cliff)
desc: Narrow copper alloy strip with beaded edge, bent into a penannular ring. Possibly a suspension loop.

title: Iron Age spindle-whorl (Staple Howe)
desc: Clay spindle-whorl; squashed spherical body, perforation tapers from top to bottom; buff.

title: Iron Age brooch (Aylesford)
desc: Copper alloy brooch.

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. Fabric is composed of sand-tempering. The rim is squared and internally lipped but represents less than 5% of the vessel circumference. The sherd is burnished and secondarily fired and buff in colour.

title: Iron Age; La Tène II bracelet (Somsois)
desc: Circular copper alloy penannular bracelet. Circular in cross-section, with undefined terminals.

title: Iron Age door; pivot (Cyprus)
desc: Rough marble cube and piece of lead which acted as lower pivot of tomb door.

title: Iron Age bracelet (Ken Hill)
desc: Silver ingot bracelet with broken end.

title: Bronze Age strip (Flixton)
desc: Two joining fragments of beaten gold, forming a parallel strip or ribbon. They are decorated with twenty four finely incised longitudinal grooves. Both ends are neatly squared suggesting the complete object is present.

title: Iron Age disc (Kimmeridge)
desc: Shale disc. Possibly a rough-out for a shale armlet, or a waste product from producing a vessel or similar.

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim/shoulder sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. The fabric is composed of abundant coarse flint inclusions. The rim is squared belonging to a jar with a high rounded shoulder and an upright neck, represents 10% of a 14cm rim diametre. The rim is decorated with fingertip impressions on the rim-top and impressed below the exterior rim. The fingertip impressions on the rim top have significantly moved the clay, so decorated when still quite wet. The sherd has roughly smoothed surfaces and

title: Late Bronze Age spindle-whorl (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Fired clay spindlewhorl fragments, three in total; two refitting pieces and one non-refitting but belonging to the same object; plano-convex in shape, flat slightly concave in 'base'; brown-orange fine fabric with no obvious inclusions; highly fired; central perforation visible

title: Late Bronze Age sherd (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Pottery rim sherd dating to the Late Bronze Age. The fabric is composed of sparse fine flint inclusions and the sherd is black in colour. The rim is squared but represents less than 10% of the rim diametre. The rim most likely belongs to a fineware jar with a highly smoothed surface.

title: Bronze Age spear-head (Fourdan)
desc: Copper alloy socketed spearhead, fragment, pegged.

title: Pottery Neolithic figurine (Marv Dasht)
desc: Fired clay animal figurine representing a quadruped; handmade; relatively low-fired; dark grey fabric with low density small angular voids and small grit inclusions; small stout legs, one of which is broken off; head also broken off.

title: Iron Age ring; brooch (Hanging Cliff)
desc: Fragment of copper alloy wire, originally coiled into a penannular ring with overlapped ends, possibly part of a ring-headed pin or a brooch spring.

title: Late Bronze Age worked antler fragment (Runnymede Bridge)
desc: Worked antler fragment; cut on one end and broken on the other, whittled down forming faceted surfaces; four cut marks on the exterior; slightly polished.
